questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Let $\gamma \in \mathbb{R}$ be such that the lines $L_1: \frac{x+11}{1}=\frac{y+21}{2}=\frac{z+29}{3}$ and $L_2: \frac{x+16}{3}=\frac{y+11}{2}=\frac{z+4}{\gamma}$ intersect. Let $R_1$ be the point of intersection of $L_1$ and $L_2$. Let $O=(0,0,0)$, and $\hat{n}$ denote a unit normal vector to the plane containing both the lines $L_1$ and $L_2$.

Match each entry in List-I to the correct entry in List-II.

List-I List-II
(P) $\gamma$ equals (1) $-\hat{i} - \hat{j} + \hat{k}$
(Q) A possible choice for $\hat{n}$ is (2) $\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}}$
(R) $\overrightarrow{OR_1}$ equals (3) $1$
(S) A possible value of $\overrightarrow{OR_1} \cdot \hat{n}$ is (4) $\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}} \hat{i} - \frac{2}{\sqrt{6}} \hat{j} + \frac{1}{\sqrt{6}} \hat{k}$
(5) $\sqrt{\frac{2}{3}}$

The correct option is :
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} & L_1: \frac{x+11}{1}=\frac{y+21}{2}=\frac{z+29}{3}=a \\ & L_2: \frac{x+16}{3}=\frac{y+11}{2}=\frac{z+4}{\gamma}=b \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & x=a-11=3 b-16 \Rightarrow a-3 b=-5 \quad \text{.... (1)}\\ & y=2 a-21=2 b-11 \Rightarrow 2 a-2 b=10 \quad \text{.... (2)}\\ & z=3 a-29=b r-4 \Rightarrow 3 a-b y=25 \quad \text{.... (3)} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { from (1) & (2) } \\ & a=10, b=5 \end{aligned}$$

Now from (3)

$$\begin{aligned} & 3(10)-5 \gamma=25 \quad \therefore \gamma=1 \\ & \mathrm{R}_1 \equiv(-1,-1,1) \\ & \mathrm{OR}_1=-\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \vec{n}=\left|\begin{array}{lll} i & j & k \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 3 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right|=-4 \hat{i}-(-8) \hat{j}-4 \hat{k} \\ & \vec{n}=-4 \hat{i}+8 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}=-4(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}) \\ & \hat{n}= \pm \frac{4(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})}{4 \sqrt{6}}= \pm \frac{(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})}{\sqrt{6}} \\ & \overrightarrow{O R} \cdot \hat{n}= \pm(-\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k})\left(\frac{\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}}{\sqrt{6}}\right)= \pm \frac{2}{\sqrt{6}}= \pm \sqrt{\frac{4}{6}}= \pm \sqrt{\frac{2}{3}} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Let $\mathbb{R}^3$ denote the three-dimensional space. Take two points $P=(1,2,3)$ and $Q=(4,2,7)$. Let $\operatorname{dist}(X, Y)$ denote the distance between two points $X$ and $Y$ in $\mathbb{R}^3$. Let

$$ \begin{gathered} S=\left\{X \in \mathbb{R}^3:(\operatorname{dist}(X, P))^2-(\operatorname{dist}(X, Q))^2=50\right\} \text { and } \\ T=\left\{Y \in \mathbb{R}^3:(\operatorname{dist}(Y, Q))^2-(\operatorname{dist}(Y, P))^2=50\right\} . \end{gathered} $$

Then which of the following statements is (are) TRUE?
options: There is a triangle whose area is 1 and all of whose vertices are from $S$.
There are two distinct points $L$ and $M$ in $T$ such that each point on the line segment $L M$ is also in $T$.
There are infinitely many rectangles of perimeter 48 , two of whose vertices are from $S$ and the other two vertices are from $T$.
There is a square of perimeter 48 , two of whose vertices are from $S$ and the other two vertices are from $T$.
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{S}=\left\{\mathrm{X}:(\mathrm{XP})^2-(\mathrm{XQ})^2=50\right\} \\ & \mathrm{T}=\left\{\mathrm{Y}:(\mathrm{YQ})^2-(\mathrm{YP})^2=50\right\} \end{aligned}$$

for finding $$S \equiv X(x, y, z)$$ and for $$T \equiv Y(x, y, z)$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \left((x-1)^2+(y-1)^2+(z-1)^2\right)-\left((x-4)^2+(y-2)^2+(z-7)^2\right)=50 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad S=\{(x, y, z): 6 x+8 z=105\} \\ & \quad \quad T=\{(x, y, z): 6 x+8 z=5\} \end{aligned}$$

Since S and T both are plane;

(A) There exist a triangle in plane S whose area $$=1$$ (always)

(B) L & M lies on plane T, hence line segment joining L & M will lie on plane T.

(C) Distance between $$\mathrm{S}$$ & $$\mathrm{T}$$

$$\mathrm{d}=\left|\frac{105-5}{10}\right|=10$$

Hence for rectangle of perimeter 48 can exist.

There will be infinite such rectangle possible.

(D) For square

Hence Answers A, B, C, D are correct.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: A straight line drawn from the point $P(1,3,2)$, parallel to the line $\frac{x-2}{1}=\frac{y-4}{2}=\frac{z-6}{1}$, intersects the plane $L_1: x-y+3 z=6$ at the point $Q$. Another straight line which passes through $Q$ and is perpendicular to the plane $L_1$ intersects the plane $L_2: 2 x-y+z=-4$ at the point $R$. Then which of the following statements is (are) TRUE?
options: The length of the line segment $P Q$ is $\sqrt{6}$
The centroid of the triangle $P Q R$ is $\left(\frac{4}{3}, \frac{14}{3}, \frac{5}{3}\right)$
The perimeter of the triangle $P Q R$ is $\sqrt{2}+\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{11}$
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} & \text { line }: \frac{x-1}{1}=\frac{y-3}{2}=\frac{z-2}{1} \\ & (x, y, z)=(\lambda+1,2 \lambda+3, \lambda+2) \\ & \text { Put in } L_1: x-y+3 z=6 \\ & (\lambda+1)-(2 \lambda+3)+3(\lambda+2)=6 \\ & 2 \lambda=2 \Rightarrow \lambda=1 \\ & Q=(2,5,3) \end{aligned}$$

line: $$\frac{x-2}{1}=\frac{y-5}{-1}=\frac{z-3}{3}$$

$$(\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y}, \mathrm{z})=(\mathrm{t}+2,5-\mathrm{t}, 3 \mathrm{t}+3)$$

Put in $$L_2: 2 x-y+z=-4$$

$$\begin{aligned} & 2(t+4)-(5-t)+(3 t+3)=-4 \\ & 6 t=-6 \Rightarrow t=-1 \\ & R=(1,6,0) \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { Perimeter }=\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{13}+\sqrt{11} \\ & \text { Centroid }=\left(\frac{4}{3}, \frac{14}{3}, \frac{5}{3}\right) \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Chemistry Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
Assertion (A): p-methoxyphenol is a stronger acid than $p$-nitrophenol.

Reason (R): Methoxy group shows + I effect whereas nitro group shows -I effect.
options:
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason ( $R$ ) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
 Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason ( $R$ ) is not the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
 Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
 Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
questions.solution: $P$-nitrophenol is a more acidic than $p$-methoxyphenol. The nitro group present in para position exerts -R effect and increases acidic character. Methoxy group in para position exerts $+R$ effect and decreases acidic character.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Chemistry Delhi Set 1 - 2024

questions.solution: The stronger base is a conjugate form of its weak acid. ROH is the weakest acid. Also, the positive Inductive effect of the alkyl group in $\mathrm{RO}^{-}$ increases the negative charge and makes it a very strong base.

questions.question: Which of the following compounds will undergo self aldol condensation in the presence of cold dilute alkali?
questions.solution:
Aldehydes which contain $ \alpha $-hydrogen on a saturated carbon, i.e. $ \mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{CH}_{2} \mathrm{CHO} $ will undergo aldol condensation.

Although $ \mathrm{CH}_{2}=\stackrel{\alpha}{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H}-\mathrm{CHO} $ also contain a $ \alpha $-hydrogen atom but cannot be easily abstracted by a base to form a carbanion and hence does not undergo aldol condensation.

questions.solution:
(a) (i) 2-Ethyl benzaldehyde

(ii) (1)

(2) Fehling's reaction does not happen with benzaldehyde.

(iii)

Cannizzaro reaction does not affect the alkyl substituent. Same products are formed.

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{CHO}\left(\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3\right)+\mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_4\left(\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3\right) \\ & \mathrm{COONa}+{ }^{+}+\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_4\left(\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3\right) \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{OH} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
(a) (i) You are given three circuit elements $\mathrm{X}, \mathrm{Y}$ and $Z$. They are connected one by one across a given ac source. It is found that V and I are in phase for element $X$. V leads I by $\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$ for element $Y$ while I leads $V$ by $\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$ for element $Z$. Identify elements X, Y and $Z$.

(ii) Establish the expression for impedance of circuit when elements $X, Y$ and $Z$ are connected in series to an ac source. Show the variation of current in the circuit with the frequency of the applied ac source.

(iii) In a series LCR circuit, obtain the conditions under which (i) impedance is minimum and (ii) wattless current flows in the circuit.

OR

(b) (i) Describe the construction and working of a transformer and hence obtain the relation for $\left(\frac{v_s}{v_p}\right)$ in terms of number of turns of primary and secondary.

(ii) Discuss four main causes of energy loss in a real transformer.
questions.solution:
(a) (i) Since, the phase difference between $V$ and $I$ is zero, hence, the element X is a resistor.

Since, the voltage leads the current by $\frac{\pi}{4}$, hence, the element $Y$ is an inductor.

And, since, the current leads the voltage by $\frac{\pi}{4}$, hence, the element Z is a capacitor.

(ii)

Circuit diagram

Phasor diagram

$$\begin{aligned} &\begin{array}{ll} \text { Now, } & V_R=i_o R \\ & V_L=i_0 X_L \\ & V_C=i_o X_C \\ \Rightarrow & V^2=V R^2+\left(V_L-V_C\right)^2 \\ & V^2=i^2\left(R^2+\left(X_L-X_C\right)^2\right) \end{array} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} i & =\frac{V}{\sqrt{R^2+\left(X_L-X_C\right)^2}}=\frac{V}{Z} \\ \Rightarrow \quad Z & =\sqrt{R^2+\left(X_L-X_C\right)^2} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} \text { When }\quad X_L & =X_C \\ \omega_r L & =\frac{1}{\omega_r C} \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow \quad \omega_r & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{L C}} \\ 2 \pi v_r & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{L C}} \\ v_r & =\frac{1}{2 \pi \sqrt{L C}} \end{aligned}\\ &\text { At this frequency } v_r \text {, as } X_L=X_C\\ &\Rightarrow \quad Z=R \rightarrow \text { minimum } \end{aligned}$$

From the above relation, for frequencies greater than or less than $\omega_r$, the values of current are less than the maximum value $\left(\mathrm{I}_0\right)$.

(iii)(i) When, $\quad X_L=X_C$

$$\therefore \quad Z=R$$

So, the impedance is minimum when $X_L=X_C$.

(ii) $P=V I \cos \phi$

$$\begin{aligned} \text{When }\quad & \phi=\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & P=0 \end{aligned}$$

Therefore, wattless current flows when the impedance of the circuit is purely inductive or purely capacitive, i.e., $R=0$.

OR

(b) (i) Transformer: A transformer is a device that can be used to increase or decrease an alternating voltage to any desired level. Step down transformers are the first kind of transformers that produce an output voltage that is lower than the input voltage. Step up transformers are the second kind of transformers that provide an output voltage that is higher than the input voltage.

Principle: When the current in a nearby coil varies, an e.m.f. is induced in the secondary coil. Construction: The figure depicts a basic transformer. It is composed of two coils with varying turns wound around a closed, laminated soft iron core. Insulated wire is used to create the

coils. The primary coil is the one to which the AC input voltage is applied, and the secondary coil is the one across which the AC output voltage is measured.

Working: When the primary is subjected to an alternating voltage, the resulting current creates an alternating magnetic flux that connects the secondary and causes an electromagnetic field (emf) in it. The number of turns determines this emf's value.

Let $\phi$ be flux in each turn in the core at time $t$ due to current in primary when a voltage $V_P$ is applied to it.

So, $$\varepsilon_S=-N_S \frac{d \phi}{d t}$$

Also, $$\varepsilon_P=-N_P \frac{d \phi}{d t}$$

But $\varepsilon_P=V_P$ and for small current $\varepsilon_S=V_S$

$$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{V_S}{V_P}=\frac{N_S}{N_p}$$

(ii) 1. Copper loss

(1) Heat energy lost as a result of the resistance of the copper coils used in a transformer's windings is known as copper loss.

(2) Using wire with a large cross-sectional area in the coils can reduce copper loss.

2. Hysteresis loss

(1) Hysteresis loss is the term for energy lost as a result of the transformer's constant magnetization and demagnetization.

(2) Soft magnetic core materials, such as silicon iron or permalloy, can reduce hysteresis loss in transformers.

3. Flux loss

(1) Poor coupling between the primary and secondary coils results in flux loss.

(2) Winding a transformer's primary and secondary coils one over the other will lower flux loss.

4. Eddy current loss

(1) Eddy current loss results from energy loss in a metallic plate kept in a time-varying magnetic field.

(2) It can be reduced by using a transformer with a laminated iron core.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
The circuit shown in the figure contains an inductor $L$, a capacitor $C_0$, a resistor $R_0$ and an ideal battery. The circuit also contains two keys $\mathrm{K}_1$ and $\mathrm{K}_2$. Initially, both the keys are open and there is no charge on the capacitor. At an instant, key $\mathrm{K}_1$ is closed and immediately after this the current in $R_0$ is found to be $I_1$. After a long time, the current attains a steady state value $I_2$. Thereafter, $\mathrm{K}_2$ is closed and simultaneously $\mathrm{K}_1$ is opened and the voltage across $C_0$ oscillates with amplitude $V_0$ and angular frequency $\omega_0$.

Match the quantities mentioned in List-I with their values in List-II and choose the correct option.

List-I List-II
(P) The value of $I_1$ in Ampere is (1) $0$
(Q) The value of $I_2$ in Ampere is (2) $2$
(R) The value of $\omega_0$ in kilo-radians/s is (3) $4$
(S) The value of $V_0$ in Volt is (4) $20$
(5) $200$
questions.solution:
(P) When $$K_1$$ is closed current in $$R_0$$ is $$I_1$$

At $$\mathrm{t}=0$$; circuit will be

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{I}_1=0 \\ & \mathrm{P} \rightarrow(1) \end{aligned}$$

(Q) After long time inductor behave as a wire so $$I_2$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{I}_2=\frac{20}{5}=4 \mathrm{~A} \\ & \mathrm{Q} \rightarrow(3) \end{aligned}$$

$$\text { (R) When } K_2 \text { is closed and } K_1 \text { open }$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \omega_0=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{LC}}} \\ & \omega_0=\frac{1}{\sqrt{25 \times 10^{-3} \times 10 \times 10^{-6}}}=\frac{1}{5 \times 10^{-4}} \\ & \omega_0=2 \times 10^3 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s} \\ & \omega_0=2 \mathrm{kilo}-\mathrm{radian} / \mathrm{s} \\ & \mathrm{R} \rightarrow(2) \end{aligned}$$

(S) Now $$K_2$$ is closed and $$K_1$$ open

$$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{LI}_2^2=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{CV}_0^2 \\ & 25 \times 10^{-3} \times(4)^2=10 \times 10^{-6} \times \mathrm{V}_0^2 \\ & \mathrm{~V}_0^2=2500 \times 16 \\ & \mathrm{~V}_0=50 \times 4=200 \mathrm{~V} \\ & \mathrm{~S} \rightarrow(5) \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: 'Bat' is related to 'Mammal' in the same way as 'Spiders' is related to $ \qquad $
questions.solution:
We can solve this by identifying the classification of each animal:

A bat is a mammal.

Similarly, spiders belong to the phylum $$\text{Arthropoda}$$ (spiders are arachnids, which are part of the arthropods).

Thus, just as a bat is a type of mammal, a spider is a type of arthropod.

The correct answer is: Option B, Arthropoda.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question:
Select the number that is related to the third number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number.

$ 19: 12:: 23: ? $
questions.solution:
To solve the problem, we need to identify the relationship between the pairs of numbers.

For the pair 19: 12, the relationship is as follows:

$ (12 \times 2) - 5 = 19 $

To find the number related to 23 using the same pattern, we set up the equation:

$ (x \times 2) - 5 = 23 $

Solving for $ x $:

Add 5 to both sides:

$ 2x = 23 + 5 = 28 $

Divide by 2:

$ x = \frac{28}{2} = 14 $

Thus, the number related to 23 following the pattern is 14.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Select the word-pair in which the two words are related in the same way as are the two words in the following word-pair. Grapes - Bunch
questions.solution:
The relationship in the pair "Grapes – Bunch" is that the second word is the collective noun used to describe a natural grouping of the first. Grapes naturally grow in clusters known as a bunch.

Looking at the options:

Tree – Forest

A forest is composed of many trees. While this is a collective grouping, the term “forest” refers to a large area covered with trees rather than the specific natural grouping of individual items.

Sheep – Herd

A herd is the collective noun for sheep. In the same way that grapes are naturally grouped together in a bunch, sheep are often grouped together in a herd.

Flower – Garden

A garden is an area where flowers are planted, but it’s more of a designed space rather than a natural cluster like a bunch of grapes.

Star – Galaxy

A galaxy is a massive collection of stars. Although similar in the sense of being a grouping, galaxies pertain to celestial bodies and are on a completely different scale and context.

Since the relationship in “Grapes – Bunch” is based on a naturally occurring collective noun, the pair that fits best is:

$$\text{Sheep – Herd}$$

Thus, the correct answer is Option B.

questions.question:
Directions : Each item in this section consists of a sentence with an underlined word followed by four words or group of words. Select the option that is opposite in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the answer sheet accordingly. 

All human beings are fallible
questions.solution:
1. “Fallible” means “capable of making mistakes.”

  2. Check each option:

    – frail = weak (not related to making mistakes)

    – erring = making errors (similar in meaning)

    – mortal = subject to death (unrelated)

    – perfect = without fault or error (exact opposite)

  3. Opposite of “fallible” is “perfect.”

Answer: Option 4 (perfect)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Directions : Each item in this section consists of a sentence with an underlined word followed by four words or group of words. Select the option that is opposite in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the answer sheet accordingly. 

His words were vague
questions.solution:
The underlined word is vague, which means not clear or uncertain.

Now, let us check the options one by one:

  1. identical → means exactly the same. This is not the opposite of vague.

  2. obscure → means unclear or hidden. This is similar in meaning to vague, not opposite.

  3. precise → means clear and exact. This is the opposite of vague. ✅

  4. imperceptible → means unable to be noticed. This again is close in meaning to unclear, not opposite.

Correct Answer: Option 3 — precise

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Directions : Each item in this section consists of a sentence with an underlined word followed by four words or group of words. Select the option that is opposite in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the answer sheet accordingly. 

She performed all her duties conscientiously
questions.solution:
1. ‘Conscientiously’ means performing duties with care, diligence and responsibility.

  2. Meanings of options:

    • assiduously – with constant and close attention (similar to conscientiously)

    • painstakingly – with great care and thoroughness (similar)

    • scrupulously – very carefully and precisely (similar)

    • carelessly – without care or attention (opposite)

  3. Opposite in meaning: Option 2) carelessly.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Directions : Each item in this section consists of a sentence with an underlined word followed by four words or group of words. Select the option that is opposite in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the answer sheet accordingly. 

These risks are incidental to the work of a firefighter
questions.solution:
1. “Incidental” means secondary, unimportant or occurring by chance.

  2. Identify which option is its opposite (i.e., means “necessary” or “fundamental”):

    – essential: necessary, indispensable → antonym of incidental

    – adventitious: accidental → synonym of incidental

    – serendipitous: occurring by chance in a happy way → related to accidental

    – subsidiary: secondary, less important → synonym of incidental

  3. Correct answer: Option 1 (essential).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Directions : Each item in this section consists of a sentence with an underlined word followed by four words or group of words. Select the option that is opposite in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the answer sheet accordingly. 

She has always been disdainful of poor people
questions.solution:
1. Meaning of “disdainful”
  • Showing contempt or a lack of respect for someone.

  1. Meanings of the options

  2. reverential – showing deep respect or admiration

  3. derisive – mocking or ridiculing (similar to contempt)

  4. supercilious – behaving as if one thinks oneself superior (similar to disdainful)

  5. insolent – boldly disrespectful or rude

  6. Opposite in meaning

  • “reverential” is the only word that means showing respect, which is the opposite of contempt.

Answer: Option 1 – reverential

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Mohan was living in a deplorable condition.
questions.solution:
The underlined word in the sentence is "deplorable," which generally means deserving strong condemnation, shockingly bad, or in a wretched condition.

To find the opposite in meaning, let's analyze the options:

Option A: reprehensible - This word means deserving censure or condemnation, which is similar in meaning to "deplorable."

Option B: opprobrious - This word means expressing scorn or criticism, which also aligns with a negative connotation similar to "deplorable."

Option C: despicable - This word means deserving hatred and contempt, again similar in negative meaning to "deplorable."

Option D: commendable - This word means deserving praise. It is the opposite of strong condemnation or bad conditions.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option D: commendable

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: You have to persevere with difficult students.
questions.solution:
The underlined word in the sentence is "persevere." To persevere means to continue in a course of action even in the face of difficulty or with little or no indication of success. Therefore, the opposite in meaning would be to stop or discontinue the action, especially when facing difficulty.

Let's analyze the given options:

Option A: give up

This means to stop trying or to surrender. This is the correct opposite of "persevere."

Option B: persist

This is very similar in meaning to "persevere" and means to continue firmly in an opinion or a course of action despite difficulty.

Option C: carry on

This also means to continue or proceed with an activity or task.

Option D: stand firm

This means to remain resolute or steadfast, particularly in the face of difficulty.

The option that is opposite in meaning to "persevere" is Option A: give up.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Everywhere they go, they squander their earnings.
questions.solution:
The word squander means to waste something, especially money or time, in a reckless and foolish manner. To find the opposite in meaning, we need to identify a word that conveys the idea of using resources prudently and effectively.

Let's analyze the options:

Option A: fritter away

This phrase means to waste time, money, or effort on unimportant matters. This is synonymous with "squander" rather than being its opposite.

Option B: splurge

To splurge means to spend money freely or extravagantly, which is also similar to "squander," as it implies using money without care.

Option C: squabble

This word means to quarrel noisily over a trivial matter. It is unrelated to the idea of spending money or resources.

Option D: manage

This word means to control or deal with resources efficiently, which is the opposite of wasting or squandering them. Therefore, "manage" is the correct opposite of "squander."

Correct Answer: Option D (manage)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: These exercises will counteract the effects of stress and tension.
questions.solution:
The objective of this exercise is to select the word that has the opposite meaning to the underlined word "counteract." "Counteract" means to act against something in order to reduce its force or neutralize it. Let's analyze each option to find the correct antonym.

Option A: Exacerbate

To exacerbate means to make a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling worse. This word directly conveys the opposite meaning of alleviating or neutralizing. Thus, this fits the criteria for being the antonym of "counteract."

Option B: Foil

To foil means to prevent (something considered wrong or undesirable) from succeeding. This action does not address the opposite meaning of "counteract" but rather something that aims to stop a particular course.

Option C: Thwart

To thwart means to prevent someone from accomplishing something. Similar to foiling, this word doesn't align well with being the direct opposite of "counteract."

Option D: Contravene

To contravene means to violate the prohibition or order of (a law, treaty, or code of conduct). This word is about acting against a command or law and doesn't directly oppose the meaning of "counteract."

Answer: Option A – Exacerbate

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Their new colour scheme is hideous.
questions.solution:
The word hideous means extremely unpleasant or ugly. Therefore, we need to find the option that has the opposite meaning, which would be something pleasant or attractive.

Let's analyze the options:

Option A: appalling

This word means causing shock or dismay, similar to hideous in terms of negative connotation.

Option B: beautiful

This word means pleasing the senses or mind aesthetically. It is the opposite of hideous.

Option C: grotesque

This word means comically or repulsively ugly or distorted, which is similar to hideous.

Option D: macabre

This word means disturbing because concerned with or causing a fear of death. It shares a negative connotation, though in a different context.

Thus, the option that is opposite in meaning to hideous is beautiful.

Correct Option: B

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Consider the function $ f(x)=\frac{|x-1|}{x^{2}} $, then $ f(x) $ is
questions.solution:
Given, $ f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cl}\frac{x-1}{x^{2}} ; & x \geq 1 \\ \frac{-x+1}{x^{2}} ; & x < 1\end{array}\right. $

$ f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cl} \frac{1}{x}-\frac{1}{x^{2}} ; & x \geq 1 \\ -\frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{x^{2}} ; & x < 1 \end{array}\right. $

$ f^{\prime}(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll} \frac{-1}{x^{2}}+\frac{2}{x^{3}} ; & x \geq 1 \\ \frac{1}{x^{2}}-\frac{2}{x^{3}} ; & x < 1 \end{array}\right. $

$ f^{\prime}(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll} \frac{2-x}{x^{3}} ; & x \geq 1 \\ \frac{x-2}{x^{3}} ; & x < 1 \end{array}\right. $

By observation, $ f^{\prime}(x) $ will be

Decreasing in $ (0,1) \cup(2, \infty) $

and increasing in $ (-\infty, 0) \cup(1,2) $

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The maximum area of rectangle inscribed in a circle of diameter $ R $ is
questions.solution:
According to question,

Diameter $ =\boldsymbol{R} $

Diagonals, $ d_{1}=d_{2}=R $

$ \begin{aligned} \text { Max Area of rectangle (any quadrilateral) } & =\frac{1}{2} d_{1} \times d_{2} \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \times R \times R=\frac{R^{2}}{2} \end{aligned} $

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: The function $f(x)=\frac{x}{2}+\frac{2}{x}$ has a local minima at $x$ equal to
questions.solution:
We have,

$$\begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\frac{x}{2}+\frac{2}{x} \\ & f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{2}-\frac{2}{x^2} \\ & \text { Put, } \quad f^{\prime}(x)=0 \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2}-\frac{2}{x^2}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{2}=\frac{2}{x^2} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x^2=4 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x= \pm 2 \\ & \text { Now, } \quad f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{6}{x^3} \\ & \text { At } x=2, \quad f^{\prime \prime}(2)=\frac{6}{8}>0 \\ & \text { At } x=-2, \quad f^{\prime \prime}(-2)=-\frac{6}{8}<0 \end{aligned}\\ &\text { Thus, } f(x) \text { has local minima at } x=2 \text {. } \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: Given a curve $y=7 x-x^3$ and $x$ increases at the rate of 2 units per second. The rate at which the slope of the curve is changing, when $x=5$ is
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} &\text { Given, }\\ &y=7 x-x^3 \end{aligned}$$

Differentiating both sides w.r.t. $x$, we get

$$\frac{d y}{d x}=7-3 x^2$$

Thus, slope, $\quad s=7-3 x^2$

Now, differentiating w.r.t. ' $t$ ', we get

$$\begin{array}{rlrl} \frac{d s}{d t} & =-6 x \frac{d x}{d t} \\ \therefore & \left.\frac{d s}{d t}\right|_{\text {at } x=5} & =-6 \times 5 \times 2 \end{array}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \quad\left[\because \frac{d x}{d t}=2 \text { units } / \mathrm{s} \text { (given) }\right] \\ & =-60 \mathrm{units} / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: The area of the circle is increasing at a uniform rate of $2 \mathrm{~cm}^2 / \mathrm{s}$. How fast is the circumference of the circle increasing when the radius $r=5 \mathrm{~cm}$ ?
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} &\text { Let radius of the circle be } r \mathrm{~cm} \text {. }\\ &\begin{aligned} & \text { Given } & \frac{d A}{d t} & =2 \mathrm{~cm}^2 / \mathrm{s} \\ & \text { since } & A & =\pi r^2 \\ & \therefore & \frac{d A}{d t} & =2 \pi r \frac{d r}{d t}\quad\text{.... (i)} \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} &\text { Also, circumference, } C=2 \pi r\\ &\therefore \quad \frac{d C}{d t}=2 \pi \frac{d r}{d t}\quad\text{.... (ii)} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{array}{llrl} \text { from (i), } & 2 =2 \pi r \frac{d r}{d t} \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{d r}{d t} =\frac{1}{\pi r} \end{array}$$

Now, substituting the value of $\frac{d r}{d t}$ in eq. (ii) we get

$$\begin{aligned} \frac{d c}{d t} & =2 \pi \frac{1}{\pi r}=\frac{2}{r} \\ \text{Now,}\quad\left.\frac{d C}{d t}\right|_{\text {at } r=5} & =\frac{2}{5} \\ & =0.4 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned}$$

Thus, circumference of circle increases at the rate of $0.4 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}$.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: If $x=a \sin ^3 \theta, y=b \cos ^3 \theta$,then find $\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}$ at $\theta=\frac{\pi}{4}$.
questions.solution:
Given, $x=a \sin ^3 \theta$ and $y=a \cos ^3 \theta$

$$\begin{aligned} & \frac{d x}{d \theta}=3 a \sin ^2 \theta \cos \theta \\ & \frac{d y}{d \theta}=-3 a \cos ^2 \theta \sin \theta \\ & \text { and } \\ & \therefore \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-3 a \cos ^2 \theta \sin \theta}{3 a \sin ^2 \theta \cos \theta} \\ & =-\cot \theta \\ & \text { and } \quad \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\frac{d}{d \theta}(-\cot \theta) \frac{d \theta}{d x} \\ & =\operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta \frac{1}{3 a \sin ^2 \theta \cos \theta} \\ & =\frac{1}{3 a \sin ^4 \theta \cos \theta} \\ & \text { Now, }\left.\quad \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}\right|_{\text {at } x=\frac{\pi}{4}}=\frac{1}{3 \cdot a\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^4 \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}} \\ & =\frac{1}{3 \cdot a \frac{1}{4} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}=\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3 a} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
37. A rectangular visiting card is to contain $24 \mathrm{sq} . \mathrm{cm}$. of printed matter. The margins at the top and bottom of the card are to be 1 cm and the margins on the left and right are to be $1 \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~cm}$ as shown below:

On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

(i) Write the expression for the area of the visiting card in terms of $x$.

(ii) Obtain the dimensions of the card of minimum area.
questions.solution:
(i) Given, $x=$ length of printed area

$y=$ breadth of printed area

Area of printed part $=x y$

$$\begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} \text { or, }\quad 24 & =x y \\ \text { or, }\quad y & =\frac{24}{x}\quad\text{.... (i)} \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

Now, area of visiting card $=(x+3)(y+2)$

$$\begin{aligned} \text{or, }\quad & A=(x+3)(y+2) \\ \text{or, }\quad & A=(x+3)\left(\frac{24}{x}+2\right) \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} \text { or, }\quad & A=\frac{(x+3)(24+2 x)}{x} \\ \text { or, }\quad & A=\frac{2 x^2+30 x+72}{x} \\ \text { or, }\quad & A=2 x+30+\frac{72}{x} \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} &\text { (ii) We have, Area, } A=2 x+30+\frac{72}{x}\\ &\begin{array}{ll} \therefore & \frac{d A}{d x}=2+0-\frac{72}{x^2} \\ \text { and } & \frac{d^2 A}{d x^2}=\frac{144}{x^3} \end{array} \end{aligned}$$

for minimum area, put $\frac{d A}{d x}=0$

i.e., $$2-\frac{72}{x^2}=0$$

or, $$x^2=36 \Rightarrow x= \pm 6$$

At $x=6 \cdot \frac{d^2 A}{d x^2}>0$ Hence, minimum

Therefore, length of vising card

$$\begin{aligned} & =x+3=6+3=9 \mathrm{~cm} \\ \text { and breadth of visiting card }\quad & =y+2=\frac{24}{x}+2 \\ & =\frac{24}{6}+2=6 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: What is the greatest value of f(x)?
questions.solution:
First, compute the derivative and find critical points:

f ′(x)=d/dx[cos2x + x]=−2 sin2x + 1.

Set f ′(x)=0 ⇒ 1−2 sin2x=0 ⇒ sin2x=½

⇒ 2x=π/6 or 5π/6 ⇒ x=π/12 or 5π/12.

Next, evaluate f(x)=cos2x + x at these and at the endpoints x=±π/2:

At x=π/12:

$$f\bigl(\tfrac{\pi}{12}\bigr)=\cos\!\bigl(\tfrac{\pi}{6}\bigr)+\tfrac{\pi}{12} =\frac{\sqrt3}{2}+\frac{\pi}{12}\approx1.128.$$

At x=5π/12:

$$f\bigl(\tfrac{5\pi}{12}\bigr)=\cos\!\bigl(\tfrac{5\pi}{6}\bigr)+\tfrac{5\pi}{12} =-\frac{\sqrt3}{2}+\frac{5\pi}{12}\approx0.443.$$

At x=π/2:

$$f\bigl(\tfrac{\pi}{2}\bigr)=\cos\pi+\tfrac{\pi}{2}=-1+\tfrac{\pi}{2}\approx0.571.$$

At x=−π/2:

$$f\bigl(-\tfrac{\pi}{2}\bigr)=\cos(-\pi)-\tfrac{\pi}{2}=-1-\tfrac{\pi}{2}\approx-2.571.$$

The largest value is

$$\frac{\sqrt3}{2}+\frac{\pi}{12},$$

so Option 2 is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: What is the least value of f(x)?
questions.solution:
Let’s find the minimum of

$$f(x)=\cos2x+x\,,\qquad x\in\bigl[-\tfrac\pi2,\tfrac\pi2\bigr].$$

Compute the derivative:

$$f'(x)=-2\sin2x+1=0\quad\Longrightarrow\quad\sin2x=\tfrac12.$$

This gives interior critical points

$$x=\frac\pi{12}\quad\text{and}\quad x=\frac{5\pi}{12}$$

(both lie in $[-\tfrac\pi2,\tfrac\pi2]$).

Evaluate $f$ at the critical points and the endpoints:

$x=-\tfrac\pi2:\;f(-\tfrac\pi2)=\cos(-\pi)-\tfrac\pi2=-1-\tfrac\pi2$

$x=\tfrac\pi{12}:\;f(\tfrac\pi{12})=\tfrac{\sqrt3}{2}+\tfrac\pi{12}>0$

$x=\tfrac{5\pi}{12}:\;f(\tfrac{5\pi}{12})=-\tfrac{\sqrt3}{2}+\tfrac{5\pi}{12}>0$

$x=\tfrac\pi2:\;f(\tfrac\pi2)=-1+\tfrac\pi2>0$

The smallest value is

$$f\bigl(-\tfrac\pi2\bigr)=-1-\tfrac\pi2\,, $$

which matches Option 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: The non-negative values of $b$ for which the function $\frac{16x^3}{3} - 4bx^2 + x$ has neither maximum nor minimum in the range $x>0$ is
questions.solution:
A function has neither a maximum nor minimum in an interval if its derivative is either always positive or always negative in that interval.

Let's find the derivative of the given function:

$$f'(x) = 16x^2 - 8bx + 1$$

For the derivative to be always positive or always negative, the discriminant of the quadratic expression must be negative. This is because if the discriminant is positive, the quadratic has two distinct real roots, meaning the derivative changes sign and the function has a maximum or minimum.

The discriminant of the quadratic is:

$$D = (-8b)^2 - 4 \cdot 16 \cdot 1 = 64b^2 - 64$$

We want this discriminant to be negative:

$$64b^2 - 64 < 0$$

Dividing both sides by 64:

$$b^2 - 1 < 0$$

Factoring:

$$(b - 1)(b + 1) < 0$$

This inequality holds when $b$ lies between -1 and 1. However, we are only interested in non-negative values of $b$. Therefore, the solution is:

$$0 < b < 1$$

Therefore, the correct answer is Option A.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: What is $\varphi(a)$ equal to?
questions.solution:
To determine what $\varphi(a)$ is equal to, let's analyze the given function:

$$\varphi(a) = \int_{a}^{a + 100 \pi} |\sin x| dx$$

First, consider the behavior and period of the sine function. The function $\sin x$ is periodic with a period of $2 \pi$. Therefore, $|\sin x|$ also repeats every $2 \pi$.

Given that the integral is over an interval of length $100 \pi$, we can express this in terms of the periods of $|\sin x|$:

The interval length is $\frac{100 \pi}{2 \pi} = 50$ periods.

Next, consider the integral over a single period of $|\sin x|$. Since $|\sin x|$ is symmetric and periodic over $[0, 2 \pi]$, we can simplify the computation:

Let's calculate the integral over one period:

$$\int_{0}^{2 \pi} |\sin x| dx$$

We know that:

$$\int_0^{2 \pi} |\sin x| dx = 2 \int_0^{\pi} \sin x dx$$

The integral of $\sin x$ from $0$ to $\pi$ is:

$$\int_0^{\pi} \sin x dx = [-\cos x]_0^{\pi} = -\cos(\pi) - (-\cos(0)) = 1 + 1 = 2$$

Thus:

$$\int_{0}^{2 \pi} |\sin x| dx = 2 \cdot 2 = 4$$

Each period of $|\sin x|$ contributes $4$ units to the integral. Since there are $50$ periods within our interval, the total integral is:

$$\varphi(a) = 50 \cdot 4 = 200$$

So, $\varphi(a)$ is a constant value of $200$, regardless of $a$.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D

200

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: What is $\varphi'(a)$ equal to?
questions.solution:
To determine $\varphi'(a)$, let's start by understanding what the function $\varphi(a)$ represents. It is given by

$$\varphi(a) = \int_{a}^{a + 100\pi} |\sin x| \, dx$$

We need to find the derivative of this function with respect to $a$. To do this, we can use the Leibniz rule for differentiation under the integral sign, which states that if you have an integral of the form

$$\int_{u(a)}^{v(a)} f(x) \, dx$$

then the derivative with respect to $a$ is given by

$$\frac{d}{da} \left( \int_{u(a)}^{v(a)} f(x) \, dx \right) = f(v(a)) \cdot \frac{d}{da}v(a) - f(u(a)) \cdot \frac{d}{da}u(a) + \int_{u(a)}^{v(a)} \frac{\partial}{\partial a} f(x, a) \, dx$$

In our case:

  • $u(a) = a$
  • $v(a) = a + 100\pi$
  • $f(x) = |\sin x|$ (independent of $a$)

Since $f(x)$ is independent of $a$, the partial derivative $\frac{\partial}{\partial a} f(x, a)$ is zero. Therefore, the derivative simplifies to

$$\varphi'(a) = |\sin(v(a))| \cdot v'(a) - |\sin(u(a))| \cdot u'(a)$$

Substitute $v(a) = a + 100\pi$ and $u(a) = a$:

  • $v'(a) = 1$
  • $u'(a) = 1$

Therefore, we get:

$$\varphi'(a) = |\sin(a + 100\pi)| \cdot 1 - |\sin(a)| \cdot 1$$

Since $\sin(x)$ has a period of $2\pi$, we know that adding $100\pi$ effectively cycles through the sine function 50 times (because $100\pi$ is $50 \cdot 2\pi$). Hence, $\sin(a + 100\pi) = \sin(a)$. This means that:

$$|\sin(a + 100\pi)| = |\sin(a)|$$

Substituting this in, we get:

$$\varphi'(a) = |\sin(a)| - |\sin(a)| = 0$$

Thus, $\varphi'(a) = 0$.

The correct answer is:

Option A: 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following is/are correct?

1. $f'(0) = 0$

2. $f''(0) < 0$

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Let's analyze the given statements one by one to determine which of them are correct.

Statement 1: $ f'(0) = 0 $

This statement is based on the condition that $ f(x) $ has a local maximum at $ x = 0 $. For a function to have a local maximum at a point, its first derivative at that point must be zero. Thus, we have:

$$ f'(0) = 0 $$

So, Statement 1 is correct.

Statement 2: $ f''(0) < 0 $

For a point $ x = 0 $ to be a local maximum, not only must the first derivative be zero, but the second derivative at that point must be negative. This indicates that the function changes concavity and thus confirms a local maximum:

$$ f''(0) < 0 $$

So, Statement 2 is also correct.

Therefore, both statements 1 and 2 are correct. The correct answer is:

Option C: Both 1 and 2

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: The function $y$ has a relative maxima at $x = 0$ for
questions.solution:
First, let's break down the problem. We are given a differentiable function $ f(x) $ that has a local maximum at $ x = 0 $. This means that at $ x = 0 $, the first derivative of $ f(x) $, $ f'(x) $, is zero, and the second derivative, $ f''(x) $, is less than or equal to zero (because we have a local maximum).

Now, let us consider the function $ y = 2f(x) + ax - b $. To find the conditions under which $ y $ has a relative maximum at $ x = 0 $, we need to look at the first and second derivatives of $ y $ and apply the conditions for a maximum.

The first derivative of $ y $ with respect to $ x $ is:

$$ y' = \frac{d}{dx}(2f(x) + ax - b) = 2f'(x) + a $$

At $ x = 0 $, for $ y $ to have a relative maximum, we need $ y'(0) = 0 $. So, we set:

$$ y'(0) = 2f'(0) + a = 0 $$

Since $ f(x) $ has a local maximum at $ x = 0 $, we know that $ f'(0) = 0 $. Therefore:

$$ 2(0) + a = 0 $$

This simplifies to:

$$ a = 0 $$

Next, let’s check the second derivative of $ y $, $ y'' $, to confirm it is less than 0 at $ x = 0 $ to confirm a local maximum:

$$ y'' = \frac{d^2}{dx^2}(2f(x) + ax - b) = 2f''(x) $$

At $ x = 0 $, we need:

$$ y''(0) = 2f''(0) $$

Since $ f(x) $ has a local maximum at $ x = 0 $, we know that $ f''(0) \leq 0 $. Therefore,

$$ y''(0) = 2f''(0) \leq 0 $$

This means that as long as $ a = 0 $, the conditions for a relative maximum are automatically satisfied, regardless of the value of $ b $.

Hence, the correct option is:

Option B: for all $b$ and $a = 0$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: Area of the region bounded by curve $y^2=4 x$ and the $X$-axis between $x=0$ and $x=1$ is
questions.solution:
Given,

$$\begin{aligned} \text { Required Area } & =2 \int_0^1 y d x \\ & =2 \int_0^1 2 \sqrt{x} d x \\ & =4 \int_0^1 x^{1 / 2} d x \\ & =4\left[\frac{x^{3 / 2}}{3 / 2}\right]_0^1 \\ & =\frac{8}{3} \text { square units } \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: Find the area of the region bounded by the curve $4 x^2$ $+y^2=36$ using integration.
questions.solution:
Given curve is $4 x^2+y^2=36$

or, $\quad \frac{x^2}{9}+\frac{y^2}{36}=1$

or, $\quad \frac{x^2}{3^2}+\frac{y^2}{6^2}=1$

since, ellipse is symmetrical along

$x$-axis and $y$-axis

$$\begin{aligned} \text { Area of ellipse } & =\text { Area of ABCD } \\ & =4 \times \text { Area of OBC } \\ & =4 \times \int_0^3 y d x \\ & =4 \times \int_0^3\left(2 \sqrt{9-x^2}\right) d x \\ & =8 \int_0^3 \sqrt{9-x^2} d x \end{aligned}$$

[since OBC is above $x$-axis]

$$=8 \int_0^3 \sqrt{3^2-x^2} d x$$

$$=8\left[\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{3^2-x^2}+\frac{3^2}{2} \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{3}\right]_0^3$$

$$=8\left[\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{9-x^2}+\frac{9}{2} \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{3}\right]_0^3$$

$$=8\left[0+\frac{9}{2} \sin ^{-1}(1)-(0+0)\right]$$

$$=8 \times \frac{9}{2} \times \frac{\pi}{2}$$

$$=2 \times 9 \pi$$

$$=18 \pi \text { sq. units }$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: Let $S=\left\{(x, y) \in \mathbb{R} \times \mathbb{R}: x \geq 0, y \geq 0, y^2 \leq 4 x, y^2 \leq 12-2 x\right.$ and $\left.3 y+\sqrt{8} x \leq 5 \sqrt{8}\right\}$. If the area of the region $S$ is $\alpha \sqrt{2}$, then $\alpha$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Point of intersection of all curves is $$(2,2 \sqrt{2})$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { Area }=\mathrm{A}_1+\mathrm{A}_2 \\ & \alpha \sqrt{2}=\int_\limits0^2 2 \sqrt{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}+\frac{1}{2} \times 3 \times 2 \sqrt{2} \\ & \alpha \sqrt{2}=2\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\frac{3}{2}}\right]_0^2+3 \sqrt{2} \\ & \alpha \sqrt{2}=\frac{17 \sqrt{2}}{3} \\ & \alpha=\frac{17}{3} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Let the function $f:[1, \infty) \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be defined by

$$ f(t)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc} (-1)^{n+1} 2, & \text { if } t=2 n-1, n \in \mathbb{N}, \\ \frac{(2 n+1-t)}{2} f(2 n-1)+\frac{(t-(2 n-1))}{2} f(2 n+1), & \text { if } 2 n-1 < t < 2 n+1, n \in \mathbb{N} . \end{array}\right. $$

Define $g(x)=\int_1^x f(t) d t, x \in(1, \infty)$. Let $\alpha$ denote the number of solutions of the equation $g(x)=0$ in the interval $(1,8]$ and $\beta=\lim \limits_{x \rightarrow l+} \frac{g(x)}{x-1}$.

Then the value of $\alpha+\beta$ is equal to _______.
questions.solution:
$$\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{t})=\left\{\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & ; & \mathrm{t}=1 \\ 4-2 \mathrm{t} & ; & 1<\mathrm{t}<3 \\ -2 & ; & \mathrm{t}=3 \\ -8-2 \mathrm{t} & ; & 3<\mathrm{t}<5 \\ 2 & ; & \mathrm{t}=5 \\ 12-2 \mathrm{t} & ; & 5<\mathrm{t}<7 \\ -2 & ; & \mathrm{t}=7 \\ -16+2 \mathrm{t} & ; & 7<\mathrm{t}<9 \end{array}\right.$$

$$\begin{aligned} & g(x)=\int_\limits1^x f(t) d t ; g^{\prime}(x)=f(x) \\ & \text { for } x \in(1,8] \\ & g(x)=0 \Rightarrow x=3,5,7 \therefore \alpha=3 \\ & \beta=\lim _{x \rightarrow 1^{+}} \frac{g(x)}{x-1} \end{aligned}$$

Apply L'pital

$$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{g^{\prime}\left(1^{+}\right)}{1}=f\left(1^{+}\right) \\ & \beta=2 \\ & \therefore \alpha+\beta=5 \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The area enclosed by the curves $ y=x^{3} $ and $ y=\sqrt{x} $ is
questions.solution:
Given curves are $ y=x^{3} $ and $ y=\sqrt{x} $

Both curves intersects at $ x=0 $ and $ x=1 $

Shaded region is required area

$ \begin{aligned} A & =\left|\int_{0}^{1}\left(x^{3}-\sqrt{x}\right) d x\right|=\left|\left[\frac{x^{4}}{4}-\frac{2 x^{3 / 2}}{3}\right]_{0}^{1}\right| \\ & =\left|\left[\frac{1}{4}-\frac{2}{3}\right]\right| \\ & =\frac{5}{12} \text { sq. unit } \end{aligned} $

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The line $ y=m x $ bisects the area unclosed by lines $ x=0, y=0 $ and $ x=\frac{3}{2} $ and the curve $ y=1+4 x-x^{2} $. Then, the value of $ m $ is
questions.solution:
Given, $ y=1+4 x-x^{2} $

$ \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=4-2 x=0 \Rightarrow x=2 $ [point of maxima]

$ \Rightarrow \quad y_{\max }=1+4 \times 2-4=5 $

when, $ x=0, y=1 $

$ \therefore $ Graph according to question and above information

$ \Rightarrow $ If $ y=m x $ bisects the area bounded

$ \Rightarrow A_{1}=A_{2} $

$ \Rightarrow \quad \int_{0}^{3 / 2}\left(1+4 x-x^{2}\right) d x=2 \int_{0}^{3 / 2} m x d x $

$ \Rightarrow\left[x+2 x^{2}-\frac{x^{3}}{3}\right]_{0}^{3 / 2}=\left[m x^{2}\right]_{0}^{3 / 2} $

$ \Rightarrow \frac{3}{2}+2 \times \frac{9}{4}-\frac{27}{8} \times \frac{1}{3}=m \times \frac{9}{4} $

$ \therefore \quad m=\frac{13}{6} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: What is the value of k?
questions.solution:
Explanation:

Given:

The area bounded by the parabola $ y^2 = kx $ and the line $ x = k $, where $ k > 0 $, is $ \frac{4}{3} $ square units.

The equation for the area bounded is:

$ \frac{4}{3} = 2 \int_0^k y \, dx $

Substituting $ y $ from the parabola equation $ y^2 = kx $, we have:

$ y = \sqrt{kx} $

So the integral becomes:

$ \frac{4}{3} = 2 \int_0^k \sqrt{k} \sqrt{x} \, dx $

This simplifies to:

$ 1 = k^2 $

Solving for $ k $, we find:

$ k = \pm 1 $

Since $ k > 0 $, the value of $ k $ is:

$ k = 1 $

Therefore, the value of $ k $ is 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: What is the area of the parabola bounded by the latus rectum?
questions.solution:
Explanation:

Given:

The area bounded by the parabola $ y^2 = kx $ and the line $ x = k $, where $ k > 0 $, is $ \frac{4}{3} $ square units.

$ \text{Area bounded} = \frac{4}{3} \, \text{sq. units} $

$ = 2 \int_0^k y \, dx $

Given:

$ \frac{4}{3} = 2 \int_0^k \sqrt{k} \sqrt{x} \, dx $

Therefore, solving the equation:

$ 1 = k^2 $

Since $ k > 0 $, $ k = 1 $.

Thus, the equation of the parabola becomes $ y^2 = x $.

For the parabola, $ 4a = 1 $:

$ a = \frac{1}{4} $

The area of the parabola $ y^2 = x $ bounded by the latus rectum at $ x = \frac{1}{4} $ is:

$ = 2 \int_0^{\frac{1}{4}} \sqrt{x} \, dx $

$ = 2 \times \frac{2}{3} \times \left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}} $

$ = \frac{1}{6} \, \text{sq. units} $

∴ The correct area is $\frac{1}{6}$ square units.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The degeneracy of hydrogen atom that has energy equal to $ \frac{-R_{\mathrm{H}}}{9} $ is (where, $ R_{\mathrm{H}}= $ Rydberg constant)
questions.solution:
The energy of a hydrogen atom is given by the equation:

$ E = -\frac{R_{H}}{9} $

which can also be expressed using the formula for energy levels:

$ E_{n} = -\frac{R_{\mathrm{H}}}{n^{2}} $

From these equations, we know:

$ n^{2} = 9 $

$ n = 3 $

For $ n = 3 $, the possible values of the azimuthal quantum number $ l $ are $ 0, 1, $ and $ 2 $, which correspond to the 3s, 3p, and 3d sub-shells, respectively.

The number of orbitals (which indicates the degeneracy) in each sub-shell is:

3s: 1 orbital

3p: 3 orbitals

3d: 5 orbitals

Adding these gives the total degeneracy:

$ 1 + 3 + 5 = 9 $

Thus, the degeneracy of the hydrogen atom with the given energy level is 9.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: If the de-broglie wavelength of a particle of mass $ (m) $ is 100 times its velocity. Then, its value in terms of its mass $ (m) $ and plank constant $ (h) $ is
questions.solution:
To find the value of the de Broglie wavelength in terms of its mass $ m $ and Planck's constant $ h $, we start by letting the wavelength of the given particle be $ x $. According to the problem, the velocity $ v $ is given by:

$ v = \frac{x}{100} $

Using the de Broglie wavelength formula, we have:

$ \lambda = \frac{h}{mv} $

Substituting the given velocity into the formula:

$ x = \frac{h}{m \left(\frac{x}{100}\right)} $

Simplifying this expression:

$ x = \frac{100h}{mx} $

Rearranging gives:

$ x^2 = 100 \frac{h}{m} $

Solving for $ x $, we find:

$ x = 10 \sqrt{\frac{h}{m}} $

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Which of the following transitions of $ \mathrm{He}^{+} $ion will give rise to spectral line which has same wavelength as the spectral line in hydrogen atom?
questions.solution:
To determine which transitions of a $\mathrm{He}^{+}$ ion can produce a spectral line with the same wavelength as a given transition in a hydrogen atom, let's examine the Rydberg formula:

For a hydrogen atom ($\mathrm{Z}=1$), the wavelength $\lambda_1$ for a transition from $n_2$ to $n_1$ is given by:

$ \frac{1}{\lambda_1} = R \left(\frac{1}{n_1^2} - \frac{1}{n_2^2}\right) $

Here, $R$ is the Rydberg constant, and $n_1$ and $n_2$ are the principal quantum numbers with $n_2 > n_1$.

For a $\mathrm{He}^{+}$ ion (with $Z=2$), if the electron transitions from $n_4$ to $n_3$, the wavelength $\lambda_2$ is given by:

$ \frac{1}{\lambda_2} = R \cdot 4 \left(\frac{1}{n_3^2} - \frac{1}{n_4^2}\right) $

Assuming $\lambda_1 = \lambda_2$, we have:

$ R \left(\frac{1}{n_1^2} - \frac{1}{n_2^2}\right) = R \cdot 4 \left(\frac{1}{n_3^2} - \frac{1}{n_4^2}\right) $

By comparing both sides, we get the relationships:

$ n_1 = \frac{n_3}{2}, \quad n_2 = \frac{n_4}{2} $

Both $n_1$, $n_2$, $n_3$, and $n_4$ must be integers, as they represent principal quantum numbers.

This condition is satisfied when $n_3 = 2$ and $n_4 = 4$, ensuring that transitions corresponding to these quantum numbers yield the same wavelength for both $\mathrm{He}^{+}$ and hydrogen.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: The energy of an electron in the ground state of hydrogen atom is -13.6 eV . The kinetic and potential energy of the electron in the first excited state will be
questions.solution:
For electrons in a hydrogen atom, the energy in the ground state is given as -13.6 eV. When considering the energy changes in excited states, we use the principal quantum number $ n $.

For an electron in the first excited state, $ n = 2 $.

First, calculate the energy of the electron in this excited state:

$ E_1 = \frac{-13.6}{n^2} = \frac{-13.6}{(2)^2} = \frac{-13.6}{4} = -3.4 \, \text{eV} $

Next, determine the kinetic energy ($ K $) and potential energy ($ U $) of the electron in the first excited state.

The kinetic energy is the negative of the total energy:

$ K = -E = -(-3.4) = 3.4 \, \text{eV} $

The potential energy is twice the total energy:

$ U = 2E = 2(-3.4) = -6.8 \, \text{eV} $

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
Assertion (A): An alpha particle is moving towards a gold nucleus. The impact parameter is maximum for the scattering angle of $180^{\circ}$.

Reason (R): The impact parameter in an alpha particle scattering experiment does not depend upon the atomic number of the target nucleus.
options:
If both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is correct explanation of Assertion (A).
If both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
If Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
If both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are false.
questions.solution:
Impact parameter, $b=\frac{Z e^2 \cot \left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 E}$

So, it depends upon the atomic number of target nucleus and its value is 0 when scattering angle is $180^{\circ}$. Therefore, both assertion and reason are false.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: The potential energy between two nucleon's inside a nucleus is minimum at a distance of about
questions.solution: The potential energy between two nucleons inside a nucleus is minimum at a distance of about 0.8 fm . If the distance reduces, the force becomes repulsive.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: The earth revolves around the sun in an orbit of radius $1.5 \times 10^{11} \mathrm{~m}$ with orbital speed $30 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{s}$. Find the quantum number that characterises its revolution using Bohr's model in this case (mass of earth $\left.=6.0 \times 10^{24} \mathrm{~kg}\right)$
questions.solution:
Given,

$$\begin{aligned} r & =1.5 \times 10^{11} \mathrm{~m} \\ v & =3 \times 10^4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\ m & =6.0 \times 10^{24} \mathrm{~kg} \end{aligned}$$

According to Bohr's model, angular momentum is given as,

$$\begin{aligned} & m v r=\frac{n h}{2 \pi} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & n=\frac{m v r 2 \pi}{h} \\ = & \frac{6 \times 10^{24} \times 3 \times 10^4 \times 1.5 \times 10^{11} \times 2 \times 3.14}{6.626 \times 10^{-34}} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & n \simeq 3.0 \times 10^{74} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
(a) Two energy levels of an electron in hydrogen atom are separated by 2.55 eV . Find the wavelength of radiation emitted when the electron makes transition from the higher energy level to the lower energy level.

(b) In which series of hydrogen spectrum this line shall fall?
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} &\text { (a) Given, } E=2.55 \mathrm{eV}=2.55 \times 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \mathrm{~J}\\ &\begin{array}{rlrl} & E =4.08 \times 10^{-19} \mathrm{~J} \\ \text { Since, } & E =\frac{h c}{\lambda} \\ \Rightarrow & 4.08 \times 10^{-19} =\frac{6.626 \times 10^{-34} \times 3 \times 10^8}{\lambda} \\ \Rightarrow & \lambda =4872 \mathop A\limits^o \end{array} \end{aligned}$$

(b) This line falls in Balmer series of hydrogen spectrum.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A particle of mass $m$ is moving in a circular orbit under the influence of the central force $F(r)=-k r$, corresponding to the potential energy $V(r)=k r^2 / 2$, where $k$ is a positive force constant and $r$ is the radial distance from the origin. According to the Bohr's quantization rule, the angular momentum of the particle is given by $L=n \hbar$, where $\hbar=h /(2 \pi), h$ is the Planck's constant, and $n$ a positive integer. If $v$ and $E$ are the speed and total energy of the particle, respectively, then which of the following expression(s) is(are) correct?
questions.solution:
The central force will provide necessary centripetal force

$$\Rightarrow \mathrm{kr}=\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{\mathrm{r}}$$

or, $$\mathrm{kr}^2=\mathrm{mv}^2\quad \text{... (1)}$$

By quantisation rule $$\mathrm{n} \hbar=\mathrm{mvr}$$

or, $$\frac{\mathrm{n} \hbar}{\mathrm{r}}=\mathrm{mv}\quad \text{... (2)}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \frac{(1)}{(2)^2} \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{kr}^2}{\frac{\mathrm{n}^2 \hbar^2}{\mathrm{r}^2}}=\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{\mathrm{~m}^2 \mathrm{v}^2} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{n}^2 \hbar^2} \mathrm{r}^4=\frac{1}{\mathrm{~m}} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{r}=\left(\frac{\mathrm{n}^2 \hbar^2}{\mathrm{~km}}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \Rightarrow \mathrm{r}^2=\frac{\mathrm{n} \hbar}{\sqrt{\mathrm{mk}}} \end{aligned}$$

(B) Using (1), $$\mathrm{K} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{n} \hbar}{\sqrt{\mathrm{mk}}}=\mathrm{mv}^2$$

$$\Rightarrow \mathrm{v}^2=\mathrm{n} \hbar \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{m}^3}}$$

(C) $$\frac{\mathrm{L}}{\mathrm{mr}^2}=\frac{\mathrm{mvr}}{\mathrm{mr}^2}=\frac{\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{r}}=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{m}}}$$ from (1)

(D) $$\mathrm{E}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{kr}^2=\frac{\mathrm{n} \hbar}{2} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{m}}}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{k} \frac{\mathrm{n} \hbar}{\sqrt{\mathrm{mk}}}$$

$$\mathrm{E}=\mathrm{n} \hbar \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{m}}}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The coefficient of $ x^{2} $ term in the binomial expansion of $ \left(\frac{1}{3} x^{\frac{1}{2}}+x^{\frac{-1}{4}}\right)^{10} $ is
questions.solution:
General term of given binomial expansion is

$ \begin{aligned} T_{r+1} & ={ }^{10} C_{r}\left(\frac{1}{3} x^{\frac{1}{2}}\right)^{10-r}\left(x^{-\frac{1}{4}}\right)^{r} \\ & ={ }^{10} C_{r} \times\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{10-r} x^{\frac{10-r}{2}-\frac{r}{4}} \end{aligned} $

We have to find coefficient of $ x^{2} $.

$ \begin{aligned} \frac{10-r}{2}-\frac{r}{4} & =2 \Rightarrow r=4 \\ \Rightarrow \quad T_{4+1} & ={ }^{10} C_{4}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{6} x^{2} \\ \therefore \text { Coefficient of } x^{2} & ={ }^{10} C_{4}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{6} \\ & =\frac{10 \times 9 \times 8 \times 7}{4 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1} \times \frac{1}{3^{6}}=\frac{70}{243} \end{aligned} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: In the expansion of (1 + x)<sup>p</sup> (1 + x)<sup>q</sup>, if the coefficient of x<sup>3</sup> is 35, then what is the value of (p + q)?
questions.solution:
First note that

$$(1+x)^p\,(1+x)^q=(1+x)^{\,p+q}$$

so the coefficient of $x^3$ is

$$\binom{p+q}{3} \;=\;35.$$

That means

$$\frac{(p+q)(p+q-1)(p+q-2)}{6}=35.$$

Try $p+q=n$:

If $n=7$, then $\tfrac{7\cdot6\cdot5}{6}=35$.

Hence

$ p+q=7. $

Answer: Option 3 (7).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: What is the remainder when 7<sup>n </sup>- 6n is divided by 36 for n = 100?
questions.solution:
To find the remainder of $ 7^n - 6n $ when divided by 36 for $ n = 100 $, consider the expression:

$ p(n) = 7^n - 6n $

This can be rewritten as:

$ p(n) = (6 + 1)^n - 6n $

Using the binomial expansion, we have:

$ = 1 + 6n + \binom{n}{2}6^2 + \binom{n}{3}6^3 + \ldots + 6^n - 6n $

This simplifies to:

$ = 1 + 6^2\left(\binom{n}{2} + \binom{n}{3} \times 6 + \ldots + 6^{n-2}\right) $

For $ n = 100 $:

$ = 1 + 36\left(\binom{100}{2} + \binom{100}{3} \times 6 + \ldots + 6^{98}\right) $

Thus, the expression becomes:

$ p(100) = 1 + 36k $

Where $ k $ is some integer determined by the combinations and powers of 6.

Therefore, when $ 7^n - 6n $ is divided by 36, the remainder is:

$ \boxed{1} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: <font style="vertical-align: inherit;"><font style="vertical-align: inherit;">What is V + W equal to? </font></font>
questions.solution:
Explanation:

Given:

$ (8 + 3\sqrt{7})^{20} = U + V \quad \text{...(i)} $

$ (8 - 3\sqrt{7})^{20} = W \quad \text{...(ii)} $

Where $ U $ is an integer, $ 0 < V < 1 $, and $ 0 < W < 1 $.

Adding Equations (i) and (ii):

$ U + V + W = (8 + 3\sqrt{7})^{20} + (8 - 3\sqrt{7})^{20} $

Expanding the expression using the binomial theorem:

$ = 2 \left[ \binom{20}{0} \cdot 8^{20} + \binom{20}{2} \cdot 8^{18} \cdot (3\sqrt{7})^2 + \ldots + (3\sqrt{7})^{20} \right] $

Since the binomial expansion results in an even number:

$ U $ is an integer.

Given that $ 0 < V < 1 $ and $ 0 < W < 1 $:

$ V + W $ must be an integer.

Thus, we conclude that:

$ V + W = 1 $

Therefore, option (d) is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: <font style="vertical-align: inherit;"><font style="vertical-align: inherit;">What is the value of (U + V)W? </font></font>
questions.solution:
Here’s a quick way to see it:

Note that

$$ (8+3\sqrt7)(8-3\sqrt7) = 8^2 - (3\sqrt7)^2 = 64 - 63 = 1. $$

Raising both sides to the 20th power gives

$$ (8+3\sqrt7)^{20}\,(8-3\sqrt7)^{20} = 1^{20} = 1. $$

By definition,

$$(8+3\sqrt7)^{20} = U+V,\quad (8-3\sqrt7)^{20} = W,$$

so

$$(U+V)\,W = 1.$$

Answer: 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: What is the coefficient of $x^{10}$ in the expansion of $(1-x^2)^{20}\left(2-x^2-\frac{1}{x^2}\right)^{-5}$?
questions.solution:
To find the coefficient of $ x^{10} $ in the expansion of the expression:

$ (1 - x^2)^{20} \left( 2 - x^2 - \frac{1}{x^2} \right)^{-5} $

we need to simplify and manipulate the expression carefully.

Step 1: Simplify the Second Factor

First, rewrite the second factor to make it easier to handle:

$ 2 - x^2 - \frac{1}{x^2} = \frac{2x^2 - x^4 - 1}{x^2} $

Now, write the entire expression:

$ (1 - x^2)^{20} \left( \frac{2x^2 - x^4 - 1}{x^2} \right)^{-5} $

Step 2: Simplify the Denominator

Notice that $ 2x^2 - x^4 - 1 = - (x^4 - 2x^2 + 1) $, which factors further:

$ - (x^4 - 2x^2 + 1) = - (x^2 - 1)^2 $

So, the expression becomes:

$ (1 - x^2)^{20} \left( \frac{ - (x^2 - 1)^2 }{ x^2 } \right)^{-5} $

Step 3: Simplify the Negative Exponent

Apply the negative exponent:

$ \left( \frac{ - (x^2 - 1)^2 }{ x^2 } \right)^{-5} = \left( -1 \right)^{-5} x^{10} (x^2 - 1)^{-10} $

Since $ (-1)^{-5} = -1 $, the expression simplifies to:

$ - x^{10} (x^2 - 1)^{-10} $

Step 4: Combine with the First Factor

Now, combine this with the first factor:

$ (1 - x^2)^{20} \times \left( - x^{10} (x^2 - 1)^{-10} \right) = - x^{10} (x^2 - 1)^{20} (x^2 - 1)^{-10} = - x^{10} (x^2 - 1)^{10} $

Step 5: Expand $ (x^2 - 1)^{10} $

Use the binomial theorem to expand:

$ (x^2 - 1)^{10} = \sum_{k=0}^{10} \binom{10}{k} (x^2)^{10 - k} (-1)^k $

Step 6: Multiply and Find the Coefficient

Multiply by $ - x^{10} $:

$ - x^{10} (x^2 - 1)^{10} = - \sum_{k=0}^{10} \binom{10}{k} (-1)^k x^{10} \cdot x^{2(10 - k)} = - \sum_{k=0}^{10} \binom{10}{k} (-1)^k x^{30 - 2k} $

We are interested in the term where the exponent of $ x $ is 10:

$ 30 - 2k = 10 \implies 2k = 20 \implies k = 10 $

Step 7: Calculate the Coefficient for $ k = 10 $

Compute the coefficient:

$ \text{Coefficient} = - \binom{10}{10} (-1)^{10} = -1 \times 1 = -1 $

Answer:

$\boxed{-1}$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: If the 4th term in the expansion of $\left(mx + \frac{1}{x}\right)^n$ is $\frac{5}{2}$, then what is the value of $mn$?
questions.solution:
To find the value of $ mn $, we start with the general term in the expansion of $ \left(mx + \frac{1}{x}\right)^n $, given by the binomial theorem:

$$ T_{k+1} = \binom{n}{k} \left(mx\right)^{n-k} \left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^k $$

We need to find the 4th term in this expansion, so let $ k = 3 $. Substituting $ k = 3 $ into the general term formula, we get:

$$ T_4 = \binom{n}{3} \left(mx\right)^{n-3} \left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^3 $$

Simplify the expression:

$$ T_4 = \binom{n}{3} m^{n-3} x^{n-3} \cdot \frac{1}{x^3} $$

Combine the exponents of $ x $:

$$ T_4 = \binom{n}{3} m^{n-3} x^{n-3-3} = \binom{n}{3} m^{n-3} x^{n-6} $$

Given that the 4th term is $ \frac{5}{2} $, this implies:

$$ \binom{n}{3} m^{n-3} x^{n-6} = \frac{5}{2} $$

For the term to be a constant (i.e., not dependent on $ x $), the exponent of $ x $ must be zero:

$$ n - 6 = 0 \Rightarrow n = 6 $$

Substitute $ n = 6 $ into the equation for the 4th term:

$$ T_4 = \binom{6}{3} m^{6-3} x^0 = \frac{5}{2} $$

This simplifies to:

$$ \binom{6}{3} m^3 = \frac{5}{2} $$

Calculate $ \binom{6}{3} $:

$$ \binom{6}{3} = \frac{6!}{3!3!} = 20 $$

Substitute $ \binom{6}{3} $ into the equation:

$$ 20 m^3 = \frac{5}{2} $$

Solve for $ m^3 $:

$$ m^3 = \frac{5}{2} \times \frac{1}{20} = \frac{5}{40} = \frac{1}{8} $$

Taking the cube root of both sides, we find:

$$ m = \frac{1}{2} $$

Finally, calculate $ mn $:

$$ mn = \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) \times 6 = 3 $$

Therefore, the value of $ mn $ is 3. The correct option is:

Option B

3

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: In a binomial expansion of $(x+y)^{2 n+1}(x-y)^{2 n+1}$, the sum of middle terms is zero. What is the value of $\left(\frac{x^2}{y^2}\right)$ ?
questions.solution:
The expression we need to expand is $$(x + y)^{2n+1}(x - y)^{2n+1}$$. To find the product, we can use the formula for the binomial expansion of two binomial expressions.

To make it simpler, let us first consider individual expansions:

The binomial expansion of $$(x + y)^{2n+1}$$ is:

$$ (x + y)^{2n+1} = \sum_{k=0}^{2n+1} \binom{2n+1}{k} x^{2n+1-k} y^k $$

Similarly, the binomial expansion of $$(x - y)^{2n+1}$$ is:

$$ (x - y)^{2n+1} = \sum_{k=0}^{2n+1} \binom{2n+1}{k} x^{2n+1-k} (-y)^k $$

Now, we multiply these two expansions together. Notice that when we multiply the terms of these expansions, we will have the product of the coefficients and the variables:

$$ (x + y)^{2n+1} (x - y)^{2n+1} = \sum_{i=0}^{2n+1} \sum_{j=0}^{2n+1} \binom{2n+1}{i} \binom{2n+1}{j} x^{(2n+1-i) + (2n+1-j)} y^{i+j} (-1)^j $$

The middle terms of the entire expansion will occur when the powers of $$x$$ and $$y$$ combined give the middle index. In this case, the sum of the powers of $$x$$ and $$y$$ will be $$2n+1$$, as the total power is $$(2n+1) + (2n+1) = 4n+2$$ and the middle term power will be half, which is $$2n+1$$.

If the sum of the middle terms is zero, then their contributions must cancel each other out. This can only happen if the exponents of $$x$$ and $$y$$ in these middle terms lead to symmetric contributions.

To derive the specific exponents, we observe that for each pair, the contributions require the exponents:

$$x^{k}$$ and $$y^{2n+1-k}$$ versus $$x^{k}$$ and $$(-y)^{2n+1-k}$$

Since it is given the middle terms cancel out, this implies that:

$$ (x + y)y = 0 \implies (x - y)(x + y) = 0 $$

Thus, the symmetry in the exponents implies that the powers of $$x$$ and $$y$$ must be equal.

Finally, let’s conclude. Given integer terms of the binomial expansion, we need:

$$ \left(\frac{x}{y}\right)^2 = 1 \implies \left(\frac{x^2}{y^2}\right) = 1 $$

Thus, the value of $$\left(\frac{x^2}{y^2}\right)$$ is $$1$$.

Option Value
A 1
B 2
C 4
D 8

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one of the following statements about lysosomes is NOT correct ?
options: They are waste disposal system of the cell
They breakdown all inorganic materials
These are also referred to as “suicidal bags”
Lysosomes stop entry of foreign materials
questions.solution:
Option 2

  1. Lysosomes contain acid hydrolases that digest organic macromolecules (proteins, lipids, carbohydrates, nucleic acids), not inorganic substances.

  2. They act as the “waste-disposal system” by breaking down cellular debris and pathogens.

  3. Because they can digest the cell’s own components in case of damage, they are called “suicidal bags.”

  4. Prevention of entry of foreign materials is a function of the cell membrane (e.g., receptor-mediated endocytosis), not of lysosomes.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which of the following two cell organelles have their own genetic material ?
questions.solution:
1. Organelles with their own genetic material are those that contain DNA independent of the nuclear genome.

  2. Mitochondria

    • Contain a small circular DNA (mtDNA) in their matrix.

    • Encode some proteins needed for oxidative phosphorylation.

  3. Plastids (e.g., chloroplasts)

    • Contain their own circular DNA (cpDNA) in the stroma.

    • Encode proteins for photosynthesis and other functions.

  4. Endoplasmic reticulum, lysosomes and Golgi apparatus do not contain DNA.

Answer: Option 3 (Mitochondria and plastids)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one among the following tissues help to connect two bones ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 2: Ligaments.

Here's why:

  • Ligaments are a type of dense connective tissue that are elastic and have considerable strength. Their primary function is to connect two bones together at a joint. They contain very little matrix.

  • Tendons are also a type of dense connective tissue, but they connect muscles to bones. They are strong and inelastic.

  • Areolar tissue is a loose connective tissue found between the skin and muscles, around blood vessels and nerves, and in the bone marrow. It fills the space inside the organs and helps in tissue repair. It does not primarily connect bones.

  • Cartilage is another type of connective tissue with a solid but flexible matrix. It is found in the nose tip, outer ear, trachea, and larynx. It also smoothens bone surfaces at joints but does not directly connect two bones.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one among the following is the source of energy in cells ?
questions.solution:
1. ATP stands for Adenosine Triphosphate.

  2. Structure of ATP:

    • Adenine (nitrogenous base)

    • Ribose (5-carbon sugar)

    • Three phosphate groups (–P–P–P)

  3. Hydrolysis reaction:

    $$\mathrm{ATP + H_2O \longrightarrow ADP + P_i + \text{Energy}}$$

    • $P_i$ = inorganic phosphate

    • Energy is released when the terminal (third) phosphate bond breaks

  4. Role of ATP in cells:

    • Provides energy for all cellular activities (muscle contraction, active transport, biosynthesis)

    • Acts as a rechargeable “energy currency”:

    – ADP + $P_i$ can be converted back into ATP during cellular respiration

  5. Comparison with other nucleotides:

    • ADP (Adenosine Diphosphate): has only two phosphates – lower energy

    • AMP (Adenosine Monophosphate): one phosphate – still lower energy

    • NAD (Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide): coenzyme for redox reactions, not a direct energy currency

Answer: Option 2, ATP.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: In which one among the following the vegetative propagation takes place through Eye Buds ?
questions.solution:
1. Vegetative propagation: asexual reproduction in plants without seeds, producing clones of the parent.

  2. Main methods (NCERT):

    a. Stem tubers – potato

    b. Bulbs – onion

    c. Rhizomes – ginger

    d. Stem cuttings/setts – sugarcane

  3. Eye buds (“eyes”) are small vegetative buds on stem tubers.

  4. Potato tubers bear these eyes; each eye can give rise to a new shoot and then a whole plant.

Answer: Option 1 Potato.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one among the following separation techniques is used for blood tests in diagnostic laboratories ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 3 — Centrifugation

Explanation:

  1. In diagnostic laboratories, blood samples are collected to test plasma, serum, or different components.

  2. Since blood is a mixture of plasma, red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets, we need to separate these components.

  3. Centrifugation is the process where the sample is rotated at very high speed in a machine called a centrifuge.

  4. Due to centrifugal force, the denser particles (like red blood cells) settle at the bottom, and the lighter components (like plasma) stay at the top.

  5. This allows doctors to test specific parts of blood easily.

Hence, centrifugation is the technique used for blood tests in diagnostic laboratories.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one among the following diseases is caused due to deficiency of Vitamin D in humans?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Rickets.

Concept:

Vitamins are essential nutrients required for the body to function properly. A lack of certain vitamins can lead to specific health conditions or diseases.

Vitamin D is especially important for maintaining healthy bones and teeth.

It aids in the absorption of calcium and phosphorus, which are critical for forming bone structure.

A deficiency in Vitamin D can result in rickets in children, a condition that negatively impacts bone development, potentially leading to bone pain, delayed growth, and skeletal deformities.

Explanation:

Rickets: Caused by a deficiency of Vitamin D, resulting in weakened bones, bone pain, and skeletal deformities in children. Severe cases can lead to growth issues and fractures.

Beriberi: Due to a deficiency of Vitamin B1 (thiamine), affecting cardiovascular and nervous systems, with symptoms like weakness, limb pain, and swelling.

Scurvy: Results from a lack of Vitamin C, causing bleeding gums, bruising, joint pain, and anemia.

Pneumonia: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, caused by bacteria, viruses, or fungi, not by a vitamin deficiency.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one among the following combinations most appropriately represent the components of balanced diet?
options: Carbohydrate, vitamins, proteins, fat, minerals, fibres and water.
Carbohydrate, vitamins, proteins, fat and minerals
<!-- x-tinymce/html -->Carbohydrate, vitamins, proteins, minerals and water
Vitamins, proteins, fat, minerals, fibres and water
questions.solution:
A balanced diet provides the necessary nutrients and energy for the body to function correctly. It includes a variety of foods in adequate proportions to ensure the intake of essential nutrients. The key components of a balanced diet include carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals, fibers, and water:

Carbohydrates: They are the primary source of energy for the body. Foods like bread, rice, and pasta are rich in carbohydrates.

Proteins: Essential for growth, repair, and maintenance of body tissues. Sources include meat, fish, eggs, and legumes.

Fats: Necessary for energy storage, insulation, and protection of vital organs. Healthy sources include nuts, seeds, and oils.

Vitamins: They support various bodily functions, including immunity, energy production, and bone health. Found in fruits, vegetables, and dairy products.

Minerals: Essential for bone health, fluid balance, and muscle function. Common sources include leafy greens, nuts, seeds, and dairy.

Fibers: Important for digestive health and preventing constipation. They are found in whole grains, fruits, and vegetables.

Water: Crucial for hydration, temperature regulation, and transport of nutrients. Drinking sufficient water daily is vital.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->In which part or organ of the human body is cartilage NOT found?
questions.solution:
Cartilage is found in

• the nose (nasal cartilage)

• the trachea (C-shaped rings)

• the knee joint (articular cartilage)

Bronchioles, however, are small airways that lack cartilage and rely on smooth muscle to stay open.

Answer: Option 3 (Bronchiole)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which part of nephron of the human kidney is responsible for filtration of blood for excretion?
questions.solution:
1. Nephron (functional unit of kidney) consists of two main parts:

    a. Renal corpuscle (glomerulus + Bowman's capsule)

    b. Renal tubule (proximal convoluted tubule, loop of Henle, distal convoluted tubule, collecting duct)

  2. Filtration of blood takes place in the renal corpuscle:

    – Glomerulus: a network of capillaries where blood pressure forces water and small solutes out of blood.

    – Bowman's capsule: a cup-shaped structure that surrounds the glomerulus and collects the filtrate.

  3. Among the given options, the structure directly responsible for collecting the filtrate is

    Option 4: Bowman's capsule.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Organisms of which of the following phylum are called sponges?
questions.solution:
Explanation:

Organisms belonging to the phylum Porifera are commonly known as sponges.

Porifera are simple, multicellular animals, primarily marine, though some species inhabit freshwater.

Sponges have porous bodies, a characteristic that facilitates water circulation, aiding in nutrient and oxygen intake and waste removal.

They possess a water transport or canal system.

Choanocytes, or collar cells, line the spongocoel and canals.

Digestion is intracellular.

The body's support structure is made of spicules or spongin fibers.

Sponges are hermaphrodites, producing both eggs and sperm within the same individual.

They reproduce asexually by fragmentation and sexually through gamete formation.

Fertilization is internal, and development is indirect, featuring a larval stage that is distinct from the adult form.

Examples include Sycon, Spongilla, and Euspongia.

Other Options:

Coelenterata (Cnidaria): Includes organisms like jellyfish, corals, and sea anemones. They have one body cavity and specialized cells (cnidocytes) with stinging organelles.

Platyhelminthes: Comprises flatworms, such as tapeworms and planarians. These bilaterally symmetrical organisms lack a body cavity (acoelomates) and often have parasitic members.

Echinodermata: Encompasses starfish, sea urchins, and sea cucumbers. They feature a unique water vascular system and adult bodies with five-part radial symmetry.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding animal cell membrane is correct?
options: They are composed of phospholipids and proteins only.
They are composed of phospholipids, proteins and cholesterol (lipid).
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: They are composed of phospholipids, proteins, and cholesterol (lipid).

The animal cell membrane, also known as the plasma membrane, is primarily composed of a lipid bilayer. This bilayer is made up of phospholipids, which are amphipathic molecules containing both hydrophilic (water-attracting) heads and hydrophobic (water-repelling) tails. This arrangement allows the formation of a semi-permeable membrane that serves as a barrier to the free passage of substances in and out of the cell.

In addition to phospholipids, the cell membrane includes proteins that are embedded within or attached to the lipid bilayer. These proteins serve a variety of functions, including transport, signal transduction, and acting as enzymes. The proteins are divided into two main categories: integral (or intrinsic) proteins, which span the entire membrane, and peripheral (or extrinsic) proteins, which are attached to the exterior or interior surfaces of the membrane.
