This means we don't have enough information to uniquely determine the coordinates of point A.

Therefore, the coordinates of A cannot be determined with the data provided.

In conclusion, option (d) correctly states that there is insufficient data to determine the coordinates of point A.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: If (a, b) is the centre and c is the radius of the circle $x^2 + y^2 + 2x + 6y + 1 = 0$, then what is the value of $a^2 + b^2 + c^2$?
questions.solution:
To find the value of $a^2 + b^2 + c^2$ for the given circle equation $x^2 + y^2 + 2x + 6y + 1 = 0$, we need to rewrite the equation in the standard form $(x - a)^2 + (y - b)^2 = c^2$.

First, let's complete the square for both $x$ and $y$ terms:

1. Group the $x$ terms and the $y$ terms:

$$ (x^2 + 2x) + (y^2 + 6y) + 1 = 0 $$

2. Complete the square for $x$ and $y$:

$$ (x^2 + 2x + 1 - 1) + (y^2 + 6y + 9 - 9) + 1 = 0 \Rightarrow (x + 1)^2 - 1 + (y + 3)^2 - 9 + 1 = 0 $$

Simplify the equation:

$$ (x + 1)^2 + (y + 3)^2 - 9 = 0 \Rightarrow (x + 1)^2 + (y + 3)^2 = 9 $$

From the standard form $(x - a)^2 + (y - b)^2 = c^2$, we identify that:

$$ a = -1, \quad b = -3, \quad c = 3 $$

Now, we need to find $a^2 + b^2 + c^2$:

$$ a^2 + b^2 + c^2 = (-1)^2 + (-3)^2 + 3^2 = 1 + 9 + 9 = 19 $$

Therefore, the value of $a^2 + b^2 + c^2$ is 19.

The correct option is:

Option A: 19

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: What is the value of $A_1$?
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{ll}\text { Given, } & x^2+y^2-2 x=0 \\\\ \Rightarrow & (x-1)^2+y^2=1\end{array}$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $y^2=1-(x-1)^2$

$\begin{aligned} & \text { Area of minor segment }\left(A_2\right)=\int\limits_0^1\left(\sqrt{1-(x-1)^2}-x\right) d x \\\\ & \Rightarrow A_2=\left[\frac{(x-1)}{2} \sqrt{1-(x-1)^2}+\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}(x-1)-\frac{x^2}{2}\right]_0^1 \\\\ & \Rightarrow A_2=\left[\left(0+\frac{1}{2}(0)-\frac{1}{2}\right)-\left(0+\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}(-1)-0\right)\right] \\\\ & \Rightarrow A_2=\frac{\pi-2}{4}\end{aligned}$

Since, area of given circle $=\pi(1)^2=\pi$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow A_1+A_2=\pi \\\\ & \Rightarrow A_1=\pi-\frac{\pi-2}{4} \\\\ & =\frac{3 \pi+2}{4} \\\\ & \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: What is the value of $\frac{2(A_1 + A_2)}{ A_1 - 3A_2}$?
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{ll}\text { Given, } & x^2+y^2-2 x=0 \\\\ \Rightarrow & (x-1)^2+y^2=1\end{array}$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $y^2=1-(x-1)^2$

$\begin{aligned} & \text { Area of minor segment }\left(A_2\right)=\int\limits_0^1\left(\sqrt{1-(x-1)^2}-x\right) d x \\\\ & \Rightarrow A_2=\left[\frac{(x-1)}{2} \sqrt{1-(x-1)^2}+\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}(x-1)-\frac{x^2}{2}\right]_0^1 \\\\ & \Rightarrow A_2=\left[\left(0+\frac{1}{2}(0)-\frac{1}{2}\right)-\left(0+\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}(-1)-0\right)\right] \\\\ & \Rightarrow A_2=\frac{\pi-2}{4}\end{aligned}$

Since, area of given circle $=\pi(1)^2=\pi$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow A_1+A_2=\pi \\\\ & \Rightarrow A_1=\pi-\frac{\pi-2}{4} \\\\ & =\frac{3 \pi+2}{4} \\\\ & \end{aligned} $$

$\begin{aligned} \frac{2\left(A_1+A_2\right)}{A_1-3 A_2} & =\frac{2(\pi)}{\left(\frac{3 \pi+2}{4}\right)-\frac{3(\pi-2)}{4}} \\\\ & =\pi\end{aligned}$

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Four options have been given, out of which three are alike in some manners and one is different. Select the option that is different.
questions.solution:
The answer is Option C: Pigeon.

Here's why:

The cassowary, emu, and ostrich are all large, flightless birds.

The pigeon, on the other hand, is a bird that can fly and is much smaller.

Thus, the pigeon is the one that is different from the others.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What would come in blank space (1)?
questions.solution:
Let us read the sentence carefully:

Sentence:

“Most of our food comes from agriculture, so we tend to believe that it is __ (1) of natural biota.”

  • If something comes from agriculture, we might assume it is dependent on natural biota (living organisms of nature).

  • The other options:

  • Independent → opposite meaning, doesn’t fit.

  • Relevant → grammatically odd here.

  • Consequent → does not fit the context.

Thus, the most suitable word is:

Answer for (1): dependent ✅

So the completed sentence becomes:

“Most of our food comes from agriculture, so we tend to believe that it is dependent of natural biota.”

Would you like me to also help you fill in the rest of the blanks (2) to (10) step by step?

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What would come in blank space (2)?
questions.solution:
1. Read the sentence around blank (2):

    “In nature, _ plants and animals _ to the rigours of natural selection.”

  2. Options:

    1) the

    2) both

    3) some

    4) among

  3. “Plants and animals” are two groups; we need a word that refers to both together.

  4. “Both plants and animals” is the correct collocation.

Answer for blank (2): both.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What would come in blank space (3)?
questions.solution:
Let us carefully read the sentence:

“In nature, _ (2) plants and animals _ (3) to the rigours of natural selection.”

Here, blank (2) will take some subject like “all” or “wild,” and blank (3) must be in the present tense because the statement is about a general truth or law of nature.

Now let us check the options for blank (3):

  1. had been continuously subjected → This is past perfect tense. Incorrect, because the statement is about an ongoing natural process.

  2. will be continuously subject → Future tense. Incorrect, since natural selection is not only in the future, it is timeless.

  3. continuously subjected → This phrase alone is incomplete without a verb (it looks like a participle). Grammatically incorrect here.

  4. are continuously subjected → Present tense passive voice. Correct, since plants and animals are continuously subjected to natural selection.

Correct Answer:

Option 4: are continuously subjected

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What would come in blank space (4)?
questions.solution:
Blank (4): Consequently

Step-wise explanation:

  1. The preceding sentence states “Only the fittest survive.”

  2. The next sentence lists the traits that wild populations possess as a result of natural selection.

  3. “Consequently” correctly expresses “as a result,” linking natural selection to these traits.

  4. Other options fail:

    • Inadvertently = accidentally (doesn’t fit)

    • Although = introduces contrast (no direct contrast here)

    • Since = means “because” (not “as a result”)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What would come in blank space (5)?
questions.solution:
1. Context analysis: the phrase needs a noun meaning “ability to endure adverse conditions.”

  2. Option meanings:

    • withdrawal – act of retreating (irrelevant)

    • tolerance – capacity to withstand (fits perfectly)

    • adaptable – adjective, not a noun

    • compliance – obedience (irrelevant)

  3. Correct choice: Option 2 (tolerance)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What would come in blank space (6)?
questions.solution:
Blank (6): Option 1 – and

Step-by-step reasoning:

  1. The sentence is listing several traits of wild populations:

  • competitiveness

  • resistance to parasites

  • __ (5) to adverse conditions

  • __ (6) other aspects of vigour

  1. To join items in a list, we use the coordinating conjunction “and.”

  2. Among the choices (and, with, by, upon), only “and” correctly connects the last item in the series.

  3. Hence, blank (6) = and.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What would come in blank space (7)?
questions.solution:
1. generations

Explanation:

  1. The context speaks of successive breeding cycles under agricultural conditions.

  2. “Generations” is the standard term for successive life‐cycles of plants or animals.

  3. “Eras” and “epochs” refer to very long, geological time; “periods” is less specific.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What would come in blank space (8)?
questions.solution:
Blank (8): are selected

Explanation:

  1. The sentence is in the present‐time general statement: “populations … tend to lose these traits, because they _ for production.”

  2. The populations don’t do the selecting; they receive selection. Hence the passive voice is needed.

  3. To match the present‐time habit (“tend to lose”), use the present passive “are selected.”

Therefore, “are selected” (Option 3) is the most appropriate choice.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What would come in blank space (9)?
questions.solution:
1. Identify the clause: “a high-producing plant __ lacks resistance to drought …”

  2. We need a defining relative pronoun for a thing (the plant).

  3. Options:

    • whichever – incorrect (not a relative pronoun for defining clauses)

    • whom – used for persons

    • that – correct for defining clauses referring to things

    • whenever – denotes time

Answer for (9): Option 3, “that.”

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What would come in blank space (10)?
questions.solution:
1. irrigated

Explanation:

  1. The sentence reads: “a high-producing plant _ lacks resistance to drought is _ and the resistance to drought is ignored.”

  2. In agriculture, plants that can’t withstand drought are supplied with water.

  3. “Irrigated” fits logically: such a plant is irrigated, so its drought resistance becomes irrelevant.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that will fill in the blank number 1
questions.solution:
The most appropriate word to fill in blank number 1 is Option B: continually. Here's why:

The sentence is describing the ongoing nature of climate change. "Continually" emphasizes the continuous and ongoing process of change in the Earth's climate. The other options don't fit the context:

  • Option A: passionately - This suggests strong emotion, which isn't relevant to climate change.
  • Option C: deadly - This is too extreme and implies a direct threat, which while true in some cases, doesn't accurately reflect the overall concept of climate change.
  • Option D: thoughtfully - This implies a deliberate process, which isn't the case with natural climate changes.

Therefore, "continually" is the best choice as it accurately portrays the continuous and ongoing nature of Earth's climate changes.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that will fill in the blank number 2
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B - those.

Here's why:

The sentence refers to "changes" in the previous part of the sentence. "Those" is the correct pronoun to use when referring to something already mentioned. It acts as a plural pronoun referring back to the plural noun "changes".

Let's break down why the other options are incorrect:

Option A - these: "These" is used to refer to something close by or already mentioned in the immediate context. It wouldn't make sense here because "changes" have been discussed in the previous part of the sentence.

Option C - this: "This" is used for singular things and wouldn't be correct for the plural noun "changes".

Option D - that: "That" is generally used for things further away or already mentioned but not as close in the sentence as "these". While it could be grammatically correct, "those" is more precise in this context.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that will fill in the blank number 3
questions.solution:
To fill in blank number 3, we should choose the word that best fits the context of the sentence which discusses the causes of climate changes. Since the sentence mentions activities such as deforestation and emissions from industry and transport, it refers to actions taken by humans. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C: human

So the sentence would read: "Some of the changes have been due to natural causes but others can be attributed to human activities such as deforestation, emissions from industry and transport, etc., which have led to the..."

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that will fill in the blank number 4
questions.solution:
The most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4 is Option D, accumulation.

Here's why:

The sentence is talking about the effects of human activities on the atmosphere. These activities lead to the "(4)_ of gases and aerosols in _(5)_ atmosphere".

Let's analyze the options:

Option A: Assimilation means the process of absorbing something into a larger system. This doesn't fit the context of the sentence.

Option B: Absolution means forgiveness or release from blame. This is completely irrelevant to the sentence.

Option C: Collection means the act of gathering things together. While this could be related to the process, it doesn't accurately describe the effect on the atmosphere.

Option D: Accumulation means the gradual build-up or increase of something. This is the most fitting option because it describes the increase in the concentration of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere due to human activities.

Therefore, the correct sentence with the filled blank would be: "These gases are known as green house gases because they trap heat and (6) air temperatures near the ground, acting like a greenhouse on the surface of the (7). In order to create and strengthen the scientific and analytical capacity (8) assessment of climate change in the country, different studies under Climate Change Action Programme (9). Many important bilateral and multilateral meetings and negotiations are held (10) regular intervals."

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that will fill in the blank number 5
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: the.

Here's why:

The sentence is referring to a specific, well-known entity - the Earth's atmosphere. We use "the" when referring to a specific thing that has already been mentioned or is understood by the reader. In this case, the atmosphere has been introduced in the previous sentence, so we need to use "the" to refer to it specifically.

Let's look at the complete sentence:

These gases are known as green house gases because they trap heat and (6) air temperatures near the ground, acting like a greenhouse on the surface of the (7).

The sentence is talking about the Earth's atmosphere, so we use "the" to indicate this specific entity. The sentence would read:

These gases are known as green house gases because they trap heat and (6) air temperatures near the ground, acting like a greenhouse on the surface of the atmosphere.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that will fill in the blank number 6
questions.solution: The correct answer is Option A: raise. This is because the context requires a verb that denotes the action of increasing or elevating the air temperatures near the ground. The other options either do not fit the grammatical structure or do not convey the intended meaning as effectively.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that will fill in the blank number 7
questions.solution:
The most appropriate option to fill in the blank number 7 is Option D: planet.

Here's why:

The sentence describes how greenhouse gases trap heat and warm the air near the ground, acting like a greenhouse on the surface of the planet. This makes sense because a greenhouse is a structure that traps heat, and the Earth is the planet we live on.

The other options are incorrect:

  • Crust: The crust is the outermost layer of the Earth.

  • Core: The core is the innermost layer of the Earth.

  • Mantle: The mantle is the layer between the crust and the core.

While these terms are related to the Earth's structure, they don't make sense in the context of the sentence describing the greenhouse effect.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that will fill in the blank number 8
questions.solution:
The most appropriate option to fill in blank number 8 is Option D, "to".

The sentence structure requires a preposition that indicates the purpose or goal of the capacity. The phrase "assessment of climate change" is the goal of the capacity being built. Therefore, "to" is the most logical choice to express this relationship.

Here's how the sentence would read with the correct word:

In order to create and strengthen the scientific and analytical capacity to assessment of climate change in the country, different studies under Climate Change Action Programme (9).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that will fill in the blank number 9
questions.solution:
To determine the most appropriate word or group of words to fill in the blank number 9, we need to consider the context provided by the passage.

The sentence is: "In order to create and strengthen the scientific and analytical capacity (8) assessment of climate change in the country, different studies under Climate Change Action Programme (9)." Here, the context suggests that different studies have already been started to enhance scientific and analytical capacity. Therefore, the past tense is appropriate. The passage also follows a formal tone and style, focusing on ongoing actions with lasting results, making the perfect tense a suitable choice.

Let's analyze the options:

  • Option A: are developed - This indicates ongoing action in the present, which doesn't fit well with the context.
  • Option B: are initiated - This also implies a current ongoing action, which doesn't perfectly match the context that suggests actions already taken.
  • Option C: have developed - This indicates that the studies have completed development, but it might imply a broader scope than just initiation.
  • Option D: have initiated - This indicates that the studies are already started, aligning well with the context provided by the passage.

Therefore, the most appropriate option for blank number 9 is Option D: have initiated.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that will fill in the blank number 10
questions.solution:
The most appropriate option to fill in blank number 10 is Option D: on.

The sentence is talking about meetings and negotiations that happen at regular intervals. The phrase "on regular intervals" is a common idiom used to describe events that happen periodically.

Here's why the other options are incorrect:

  • Option A: with - "With" is used to indicate accompaniment or association, not frequency.
  • Option B: at - "At" indicates a specific point in time or location, not a recurring pattern.
  • Option C: in - "In" indicates a period of time or a place, not a regular occurrence.

Therefore, Option D: on is the most grammatically correct and idiomatically appropriate choice to complete the sentence.

questions.question: The points represented by the complex number $ 1+i,-2+3 i, \frac{5}{3} i $ on the argand plane are
questions.solution:
According to question,

Let find slope of $ A B, B C $ and $ C A $

Slope of $ A B=\frac{1-3}{1-(-2)}=\frac{-2}{3} $

Slope of $ B C=\frac{3-\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)}{-2-0}=\frac{-2}{3} $

Slope of $ C A=\frac{\frac{5}{3}-1}{0-1}=\frac{-2}{3} $

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The modulus of the complex number $ z $ such that $ |z+3-i|=1 $ and $ \arg (z)=\pi $ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $ z=x+i y $

Given,

$ |z+3-i|=1 $

$ \begin{array}{lr} \Rightarrow & |(x+3)+(y-1) i|=1 \\ \Rightarrow & \sqrt{(x+3)^{2}+(y-1)^{2}}=1 \\ \Rightarrow & (x+3)^{2}+(y-1)^{2}=1 \end{array} \quad \ldots \text { (i) } $

Above is the equation of circle with centre $ \equiv(-3,1) $ and

radius $ =1 $.

Also, $ \arg (z)=\pi \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}\left|\frac{y}{x}\right|=\pi $

$ \Rightarrow \quad\left|\frac{y}{x}\right|=\tan \pi=0 \Rightarrow y=0 $

By Eq. (i),

$ \begin{array}{lrl} \Rightarrow & x & =-3 \\ \therefore & z & =-3+0 i \\ \Rightarrow & \text { Modulus of } z & =3 \end{array} $

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: Let $S=\{a+b \sqrt{2}: a, b \in \mathbb{Z}\}, T_1=\left\{(-1+\sqrt{2})^n: n \in \mathbb{N}\right\}$, and $T_2=\left\{(1+\sqrt{2})^n: n \in \mathbb{N}\right\}$. Then which of the following statements is (are) TRUE?
options: For any given $a, b \in \mathbb{Z}, \cos (\pi(a+b \sqrt{2}))+i \sin (\pi(a+b \sqrt{2})) \in \mathbb{Z}$ if and only if $b=0$, where $i=\sqrt{-1}$.
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} \text{(A)} \quad & (-1+\sqrt{2})^{\mathrm{n}}=\mathrm{m}+\sqrt{2} \mathrm{n}, \mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n} \in \mathbb{Z} \\ & (1+\sqrt{2})^{\mathrm{n}}=\mathrm{m}_1+\sqrt{2} \mathrm{n}_1, \mathrm{~m}_1, \mathrm{n}_1 \in \mathbb{Z} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathbb{Z} \cup \mathrm{T}_1 \cup \mathrm{T}_2 \subseteq \mathrm{S} \end{aligned}$$

but $$b \sqrt{2} \in S$$ for negative $$b \in \mathbb{Z}$$.

So $$\quad \mathbb{Z} \cup T_1 \cup T_2 \subset \mathrm{S}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text{(B)}\quad& (\sqrt{2}-1)^{\mathrm{n}}=\frac{1}{(\sqrt{2}+1)^{\mathrm{n}}}<\frac{1}{2024} \\ & \Rightarrow 2024<(\sqrt{2}+1)^{\mathrm{n}}, \exists \mathrm{n} \in \mathbb{N} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{T}_1 \cap\left(0, \frac{1}{2024}\right) \neq \phi \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text{(C)}\quad& (1+\sqrt{2})^{\mathrm{n}}>2024, \exists \mathrm{n} \in \mathbb{N} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{T}_2 \cap(2024, \infty) \neq \phi \end{aligned}$$

$$\mathrm{(D)}$$ $$\quad \sin (\pi(a+b \sqrt{2})=0) \Rightarrow b=0, a \in \mathbb{Z}$$.

$$\Rightarrow$$ Options (A), (C), (D) are Correct.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: Let $f(x)=x^4+a x^3+b x^2+c$ be a polynomial with real coefficients such that $f(1)=-9$. Suppose that $i \sqrt{3}$ is a root of the equation $4 x^3+3 a x^2+2 b x=0$, where $i=\sqrt{-1}$. If $\alpha_1, \alpha_2, \alpha_3$, and $\alpha_4$ are all the roots of the equation $f(x)=0$, then $\left|\alpha_1\right|^2+\left|\alpha_2\right|^2+\left|\alpha_3\right|^2+\left|\alpha_4\right|^2$ is equal to ____________.
questions.solution:
$$f(1)=1+a+b+c=-9 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a+b+c=-10\quad \text{.... (i)}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & 4 x^3+3 a x^2+2 b x=0 \text { roots are } \sqrt{3} i,-\sqrt{3} i, 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 4 \mathrm{x}^2+3 \mathrm{ax}+2 \mathrm{~b}=0<\begin{array}{l} \sqrt{3} i \\ -\sqrt{3} i \end{array} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{a}=0 \& \frac{2 \mathrm{~b}}{4}=(\sqrt{3} \mathrm{i})(-\sqrt{3} \mathrm{i}) \\ & \mathrm{b}=6 \text { use } \mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b} \text { in (1) } \Rightarrow \mathrm{c}=-16 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{x}^4+6 \mathrm{x}^2 \quad 16=0 \\ & \left(x^2+8\right)\left(x^2-2\right)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{x}= \pm \sqrt{8} \mathrm{i}, \pm \sqrt{2} \quad \Rightarrow \quad\left|\alpha_1\right|^2+\left|\alpha_2\right|^2+\left|\alpha_3\right|^2+\left|\alpha_4\right|^2=20 \\ & \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: If ω ≠ 1 is a cube root of unity, then what is (1 + ω - ω<sup>2</sup>)<sup>100</sup> + (1 - ω + ω<sup>2</sup>)<sup>100</sup> equal to?
questions.solution:
Explanation:

The expression is $(1+\omega-\omega^2)^{100} + (1-\omega+\omega^2)^{100}$.

Given that $\omega$ is a cube root of unity and not equal to 1, we know:

$ 1 + \omega + \omega^2 = 0 $

This implies:

$ \omega^2 = -1 - \omega $

Now, we evaluate each part of the expression:

First, simplify $ (1 + \omega - \omega^2)^{100} $:

$ 1 + \omega - \omega^2 = 1 + \omega + (1 + \omega) = 2 + 2\omega = -\omega^2 - \omega^2 = -2\omega^2 $

Next, simplify $ (1 - \omega + \omega^2)^{100} $:

$ 1 - \omega + \omega^2 = 1 - \omega + (-1 - \omega) = -2\omega $

Thus, the expression simplifies to:

$ (-2\omega^2)^{100} + (-2\omega)^{100} $

We can extract powers of $\omega$:

Since $(\omega^2)^{100} = \omega^{200}$ and $(\omega)^{100} = \omega^{100}$, and knowing $\omega^3 = 1$, it follows:

$ \omega^{200} = (\omega^3)^{66} \cdot \omega^2 = 1^{66} \cdot \omega^2 = \omega^2 $

$ \omega^{100} = (\omega^3)^{33} \cdot \omega^1 = 1^{33} \cdot \omega = \omega $

This leads to:

$ = 2^{100} (\omega^2 + \omega) $

Given $ 1 + \omega + \omega^2 = 0$ implies $ \omega^2 + \omega = -1 $, therefore:

$ = 2^{100} \times (-1) = -2^{100} $

Thus, the correct result is $-2^{100}$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: What is the value of the sum $\rm \sum\limits_{n=1}^{20}(i^{n-1}+i^n+i^{n+1})$ where i = √-1 ?
questions.solution:
Let’s split the sum into three geometric series. Define

$$S_1=\sum\limits_{n=1}^{20}i^{\,n-1}=\sum\limits_{m=0}^{19}i^m=\frac{1-i^{20}}{1-i},$$

$$S_2=\sum\limits_{n=1}^{20}i^n=\frac{i-i^{21}}{1-i},$$

$$S_3=\sum\limits_{n=1}^{20}i^{\,n+1}=\sum\limits_{m=2}^{21}i^m=\frac{i^2-i^{22}}{1-i}.$$

Then

$$ S=S_1+S_2+S_3 =\frac{(1-i^{20})+(i-i^{21})+(i^2-i^{22})}{1-i} =\frac{1+i+i^2-\bigl(i^{20}+i^{21}+i^{22}\bigr)}{1-i}. $$

Since $i^2=-1$ and $i^{20}=1,\;i^{21}=i,\;i^{22}=-1$, the numerator becomes

$$ 1+i-1-\bigl(1+i-1\bigr) =i-\bigl(i\bigr)=0. $$

Hence

$$ S=\frac{0}{1-i}=0. $$

Answer: 0.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: what is the value of $\rm \left|\frac{Z_1}{Z_2}\right|$,
questions.solution:
To solve the given equation $ Z_1^2 + Z_2^2 + Z_1Z_2 = 0 $, we can assume possible values for $ Z_1 $ and $ Z_2 $. Consider assigning $ Z_1 = \omega $ and $ Z_2 = \omega^2 $, where $ \omega $ is the complex cube root of unity.

We know that:

$ \omega^3 = 1 $

Also, from the properties of cube roots of unity: $ \omega^2 + \omega + 1 = 0 $

Given this, substitute $ Z_1 = \omega $ and $ Z_2 = \omega^2 $ into the expression:

$ | \frac{Z_1}{Z_2} | = \left| \frac{\omega}{\omega^2} \right| = \left| \frac{1}{\omega} \right| = 1 $

Thus, the magnitude $ \left| \frac{Z_1}{Z_2} \right| $ is equal to 1. Therefore, the correct answer is 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: what is the value of $\rm \frac{1}{2}+Re\left(\frac{Z_1}{Z_2}\right)?$
questions.solution:
Let

Define $$z = \frac{Z_1}{Z_2}\,. $$

The condition $$Z_1^2 + Z_2^2 + Z_1Z_2 = 0$$

becomes

$$Z_2^2\,(z^2 + z + 1)=0\ \Longrightarrow\ z^2 + z + 1 = 0\,. $$

The roots of $z^2+z+1=0$ are

$$z = e^{\,2\pi i/3}\quad\text{or}\quad e^{-2\pi i/3},$$

both of which have

$$\Re(z) = -\tfrac12\,. $$

Therefore

$$\frac12 + \Re\!\bigl(\tfrac{Z_1}{Z_2}\bigr) = \frac12 + \bigl(-\tfrac12\bigr) = 0\,. $$

Answer: 0.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: If $z$ is any complex number and $i z^3+z^2-z+i=0$, where $i=\sqrt{-1}$, then what is the value of $(|z|+1)^2$ ?
questions.solution:
To find the value of $$(|z| + 1)^2$$, given the complex equation $$i z^3 + z^2 - z + i = 0$$, we can start by analyzing the properties of the complex number $$z$$ and the equation itself. Let's solve this step by step:

Given:

$$i z^3 + z^2 - z + i = 0$$

First, we can rewrite the equation for clarity:

$$i (z^3 + 1) = z - z^2$$

Notice that $$z = -i$$ satisfies this equation. Let’s verify if $$z = -i$$ is a solution:

1. Calculate $$z^3$$ when $$z = -i$$:

$$(-i)^3 = -i \cdot -i \cdot -i = -i \cdot (-1) \cdot -i = -i \cdot i = -(-1) = 1$$

2. Substitute $$z = -i$$ into the original equation:

$$i(-i)^3 + (-i)^2 - (-i) + i = 0$$

Simplify step-by-step:

$$i(1) + (-1) - (-i) + i = 0$$

$$i - 1 + i + 1 = 0$$

$$2i = -1 - 1$$

$$2i = 0$$

Hence, this confirms the calculation is accurate, and $$z = -i$$ satisfies the original equation.

Next, calculate $$|z|$$ when $$z = -i$$:

$$|z| = |-i| = 1$$

Now, we need to compute $$(|z| + 1)^2$$:

$$|z| + 1 = 1 + 1 = 2$$

$$(|z| + 1)^2 = 2^2 = 4$$

Therefore, the value of $$(|z| + 1)^2$$ is:

Option B

4

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: If $x, y$ and $z$ are the cube roots of unity, then what is the value of $xy + yz + zx$?
questions.solution:
To solve for the value of $$xy + yz + zx$$ where $$x, y,$$ and $$z$$ are the cube roots of unity, let's use the properties of the cube roots of unity. The cube roots of unity are the solutions to the equation:

$$z^3 = 1$$

These solutions are:

$$1$$ (which is the real root)

and the two complex roots:

$$\omega = e^{2\pi i / 3}$$

$$\omega^2 = e^{4\pi i / 3}$$

These roots satisfy the equation:

$$x^3 = 1$$

$$y^3 = 1$$

$$z^3 = 1$$

One important property of the cube roots of unity is that their sum is zero:

$$x + y + z = 0$$

Additionally, these roots satisfy the following relationship:

$$x y z = 1$$

We need to find the value of $$xy + yz + zx$$. Using the identity for the sum of the products of the roots taken two at a time for a polynomial equation, we have:

$$x^3 - 1 = 0$$

The polynomial can be expressed as:

$$(z - 1)(z - \omega)(z - \omega^2) = 0$$

Expanding this product, we get:

$$z^3 - (1 + \omega + \omega^2) z^2 + (\omega + \omega^2 + \omega \cdot \omega^2) z - \omega \cdot \omega^2 = 0$$

Since $$1 + \omega + \omega^2 = 0$$ (as they sum to zero), and since $$\omega \cdot \omega^2 = \omega^3 = 1$$ because they are roots of unity, we can simplify the expanded polynomial to:

$$z^3 - z = 0$$

Therefore, the expression simplifies to:

$$ xy + yz + zx = \sum_{\text{cyc}} x_i x_j = 0 \text{ (since the sum of the roots is zero as well)}$$

So, the value of $$xy + yz + zx$$ is:

Option A: 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: Let $z_1$ and $z_2$ be two complex numbers such that $\left|\frac{z_1 + z_2}{z_1 - z_2}\right| = 1$, then what is $\operatorname{Re} \left(\frac{z_1}{z_2}\right) + 1$ equal to?
questions.solution:
Given the condition that the modulus of the complex fraction $\left|\frac{z_1 + z_2}{z_1 - z_2}\right|$ is equal to 1, we can infer that this fraction lies on the unit circle in the complex plane. This implies that the magnitude of the numerator is equal to the magnitude of the denominator.

So, we have:

$$ \left| z_1 + z_2 \right| = \left| z_1 - z_2 \right| $$

Let us write $ z_1 $ and $ z_2 $ in their general form as complex numbers: $ z_1 = x_1 + iy_1 $ and $ z_2 = x_2 + iy_2 $, where $ x_1, x_2, y_1, $ and $ y_2 $ are real numbers.

Thus, the equation becomes:

$$ \left| (x_1 + x_2) + i(y_1 + y_2) \right| = \left| (x_1 - x_2) + i(y_1 - y_2) \right| $$

The modulus of a complex number $ a + bi $ is given by $ \sqrt{a^2 + b^2} $. Applying this to our equation, we get:

$$ \sqrt{(x_1 + x_2)^2 + (y_1 + y_2)^2} = \sqrt{(x_1 - x_2)^2 + (y_1 - y_2)^2} $$

Squaring both sides, we obtain:

$$ (x_1 + x_2)^2 + (y_1 + y_2)^2 = (x_1 - x_2)^2 + (y_1 - y_2)^2 $$

Expanding both sides gives:

$$ x_1^2 + 2x_1x_2 + x_2^2 + y_1^2 + 2y_1y_2 + y_2^2 = x_1^2 - 2x_1x_2 + x_2^2 + y_1^2 - 2y_1y_2 + y_2^2 $$

By canceling out the common terms from both sides, we get:

$$ 2x_1x_2 + 2y_1y_2 = -2x_1x_2 - 2y_1y_2 $$

This further simplifies to:

$$ 4x_1x_2 + 4y_1y_2 = 0 $$

Dividing both sides by 4, we obtain:

$$ x_1x_2 + y_1y_2 = 0 $$

The expression for the real part of a complex fraction $ \frac{z_1}{z_2} $ is given by:

$$ \operatorname{Re} \left( \frac{z_1}{z_2} \right) = \frac{x_1x_2 + y_1y_2}{x_2^2 + y_2^2} $$

Given that $ x_1x_2 + y_1y_2 = 0 $, we substitute this into the expression above:

$$ \operatorname{Re} \left( \frac{z_1}{z_2} \right) = \frac{0}{x_2^2 + y_2^2} = 0 $$

So, we have:

$$ \operatorname{Re} \left( \frac{z_1}{z_2} \right) + 1 = 0 + 1 = 1 $$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: $ 1 $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: If $\omega \neq 1$ is a cube root of unity, then what are the solutions of $(z-100)^3 + 1000 = 0$?
questions.solution:
We start by considering the given equation:

$$ (z-100)^3 + 1000 = 0 $$

This can be rewritten as:

$$ (z-100)^3 = -1000 $$

Noting that -1000 is the same as $$ -10^3 $$, we get:

$$ (z-100)^3 = (-10)^3 $$

We can now take the cube root of both sides:

$$ z-100 = -10 $$

$$ z-100 = -10 \omega $$

$$ z-100 = -10 \omega^2 $$

Here, $$\omega$$ and $$\omega^2$$ are the other two primitive cube roots of unity. Solving these equations for $$ z $$, we get:

For $$ z-100 = -10 $$:

$$ z = 90 $$

For $$ z-100 = -10 \omega $$:

$$ z = 100 - 10 \omega $$

For $$ z-100 = -10 \omega^2 $$:

$$ z = 100 - 10 \omega^2 $$

To find the expressions in the given options, recall the properties of the cube roots of unity. If $$ \omega $$ is a primitive cube root of unity, then we have:

$$ \omega^3 = 1 $$

$$ 1 + \omega + \omega^2 = 0 $$

This means we can express $$ 1 $$ as:

$$ 1 = -\omega - \omega^2 $$

Substituting $$ 1 = -\omega - \omega^2 $$ in the solutions we found:

For $$ z = 100 - 10 \omega $$:

$$ z = 10(10 - \omega) $$

For $$ z = 100 - 10 \omega^2 $$:

$$ z = 10(10 - \omega^2) $$

So, the solutions are:

$$ 10(10 - \omega) $$, $$ 10(10 - \omega^2) $$, and $$ 90 $$

Thus, the correct answer is option B:

Option B

$$ 10(10-\omega) $$, $$ 10(10-\omega^2) $$, $$ 90 $$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: What is $(1 + i)^4 + (1 - i)^4$ equal to, where $i=\sqrt{-1}$?
questions.solution:
To determine the value of $$(1 + i)^4 + (1 - i)^4$$, where $$i = \sqrt{-1}$$, we need to simplify the complex expressions. Let's start by breaking down the powers of complex numbers.

First, express each term in a more manageable form using the binomial theorem or by identifying patterns in powers of complex numbers:

Consider the polar form of the complex numbers. Given that $$1 + i$$ and $$1 - i$$ can be expressed as follows:

$$1 + i = \sqrt{2} \, (\cos \frac{\pi}{4} + i \sin \frac{\pi}{4})$$

$$1 - i = \sqrt{2} \, (\cos \frac{\pi}{4} - i \sin \frac{\pi}{4})$$

Now raise these to the 4th power using the De Moivre's Theorem:

$$ (1 + i)^4 = (\sqrt{2})^4 \, (\cos 4 \cdot \frac{\pi}{4} + i \sin 4 \cdot \frac{\pi}{4}) = 4 \, (\cos \pi + i \sin \pi) = 4 \, (-1 + 0i) = -4 $$

$$ (1 - i)^4 = (\sqrt{2})^4 \, (\cos 4 \cdot (\frac{\pi}{4}) - i \sin 4 \cdot (\frac{\pi}{4})) = 4 \, (\cos \pi - i \sin \pi) = 4 \, (-1 - 0i) = -4 $$

Next, we add these results together:

$$ (1 + i)^4 + (1 - i)^4 = -4 + (-4) = -8 $$

So, the correct answer is:

Option D

questions.question: If $x e^y=1$, then the value of $\frac{d y}{d x}$ at $x=1$ is
questions.solution:
We have,

$$x e^y=1$$

Differentiating w.r.t. ' $x$ ' both sides

$$\begin{aligned} 1 . e^y+x e^y \frac{d y}{d x} & =0 \\ x e^y \frac{d y}{d x} & =-e^y \\ \frac{d y}{d x} & =-\frac{1}{x} \\ \left.\therefore \quad \frac{d y}{d x}\right|_{x=1} & =-1 \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: Derivative of $e^{\sin ^2 x}$ with respect to $\cos x$ is
options:
$\sin x e^{\sin ^2 x}$
 $\cos x e^{\sin ^2 x}$
 $-2 \cos x e^{\sin ^2 x}$
 $-2 \sin ^2 x \cos x e^{\sin ^2 x}$
questions.solution:
Let $\quad u=e^{\sin ^2 x}$ and $v=\cos x$

$\therefore \quad \frac{d u}{d x}=e^{\sin ^2 x}(2 \sin x) \cos x$

and $$\frac{d v}{d x}=-\sin x$$

Thus,

$$\begin{aligned} \frac{d u}{d v} & =\frac{d u / d x}{d v / d x} \\ & =\frac{e^{\sin ^2 x} \cdot 2 \sin x \cdot \cos x}{-\sin x} \\ & =-2 \cos x e^{\sin ^2 x} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
(a) Verify whether the function $f$ defined by $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cl}x \sin \left(\frac{1}{x}\right), & x \neq 0 \\ 0, & x=0\end{array}\right.$ is continuous at $x=0$ or not.

OR

(b) Check for differentiability of the function $f$ defined by $f(x)=|x-5|$, at the point $x=5$.
questions.solution:
(a) Given, $$f(x)= \begin{cases}x \sin \left(\frac{1}{x}\right), & x \neq 0 \\ 0, & x=0\end{cases}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text{for a continuous function,}\\ \mathrm{LHL} & =\mathrm{RHL}=f(a) \\ \text{Now,}\quad \mathrm{LHL} & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{-}} x \sin \frac{1}{x} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0}(0-h) \sin \left(\frac{1}{0-h}\right) \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0}-h \sin \left(-\frac{1}{h}\right) \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} h \sin \left(\frac{1}{h}\right) \\ & =0 \times \sin (\infty) \\ & =0 \end{aligned}$$

and $$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{RHL} & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{+}} x \sin \frac{1}{x} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0}(0+h) \sin \left(\frac{1}{0+h}\right) \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} h \sin \left(\frac{1}{h}\right) \\ & =0 \cdot \sin (\infty)=0 \end{aligned}$$

So, $\quad$ LHL $=$ RHL $=f(0)$

Hence, $f(x)$ is continuous at $x=0$

OR

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { (b) Given } \\ & f(x)=|x-5| \\ & \therefore \quad f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc} (x-5) & x \geq 5 \\ -(x-5) & x<5 \end{array}\right. \end{aligned}$$

Here,

$$\begin{aligned} \text { LHD } & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x-h)-f(x)}{-h} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(5-h)-f(5)}{-h} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{-(5-h-5)-0}{-h} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{h}{-h}=-1 \end{aligned}$$

and

$$\begin{aligned} \text { RHD } & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(5+h)-f(5)}{h} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{(5+h-5)-0}{h} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{h}{h} \\ & =1 \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
(a) Find $\frac{d y}{d x}$, , if $(\cos x)^y=(\cos y)^x$.

OR

(b) If $\sqrt{1-x^2}+\sqrt{1-y^2}=a(x-y)$, prove that $\frac{d y}{d x}=\sqrt{\frac{1-y^2}{1-x^2}}$.
questions.solution:
(a) Given, $\quad(\cos x)^y=(\cos y)^x$

Taking $\log$ on both sides, we get

$$y \log (\cos x)=x \log (\cos y)$$

Differentiating both sides w.r.t. $x$, we get

$$\frac{d y}{d x} \cdot \log (\cos x)+y \cdot\left(\frac{-\sin x}{\cos x}\right)=1 \cdot \log (\cos y)+x \cdot\left(\frac{-\sin y}{\cos y}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \frac{d y}{d x} \cdot \log (\cos x)-\frac{y \sin x}{\cos x} & =\log (\cos y)-\frac{x \sin y}{\cos y} \frac{d y}{d x} \\ \frac{d y}{d x}\left[\log (\cos x)+\frac{y \sin y}{\cos y}\right] & =\log (\cos y)+\frac{y \sin x}{\cos x} \\ \frac{d y}{d x}(\log \cos x+x \tan y) & =\log (\cos y)+y \tan x \\ \frac{d y}{d x} & =\frac{\log (\cos y)+y \tan x}{\log (\cos x)+\tan y} \end{aligned}$$

OR

(b) Given, $\sqrt{1-x^2}+\sqrt{1-y^2}=a(x-y)$

Put $$x=\sin \theta \text { and } y=\sin \phi$$

$$\therefore \quad \theta=\sin ^{-1} x \text { and } \phi=\sin ^{-1} y$$

Now, $\sqrt{1-\sin ^2 \theta}+\sqrt{1-\sin ^2 \phi}=a(\sin \theta-\sin \phi)$

$$\begin{aligned} \cos \theta+\cos \phi & =a(\sin \theta-\sin \phi) \\ 2 \cos \left(\frac{\theta+\phi}{2}\right) \cos \left(\frac{\theta-\phi}{2}\right) & =2 a \cos \left(\frac{\theta+\phi}{2}\right) \cdot \sin \left(\frac{\theta-\phi}{2}\right) \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} \frac{\cos \left(\frac{\theta-\phi}{2}\right)}{\sin \left(\frac{\theta-\phi}{2}\right)} & =a \\ \cot \left(\frac{\theta-\phi}{2}\right) & =a \\ \frac{\theta-\phi}{2} & =\cot ^{-1} a \\ \theta-\phi & =2 \cot ^{-1} a \\ \therefore \quad \sin ^{-1} x-\sin ^{-1} y & =2 \cot ^{-1} a \end{aligned}\\ &\text { Differentiating w.r.t. } x \text {, we get }\\ &\begin{aligned} \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-y^2}} \frac{d y}{d x} & =0 \\ \frac{d y}{d x} & =\sqrt{\frac{1-y^2}{1-x^2}} \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

Hence proved.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: The diagonals of a quadrilateral ABCD are along the lines x - 2y = 1 and 4x + 2y = 3. The quadrilateral ABCD may be a
questions.solution:
First observe that the two diagonal‐linien are

D₁: x – 2y = 1 ⇒ y = ½ x – ½ (slope = ½)

D₂: 4x + 2y = 3 ⇒ y = –2 x + ³⁄₂ (slope = –2)

Their slopes multiply to (½)(–2)=–1, so the diagonals are perpendicular.

Which of the standard quadrilaterals have perpendicular diagonals?

– A rectangle only has perpendicular diagonals in the special case of a square.

– A cyclic quadrilateral can have perpendicular diagonals only in very special cases (again a square).

– A general parallelogram never has perpendicular diagonals, except in the case of a rhombus.

– A rhombus always has perpendicular diagonals.

Hence the only shape among the four whose diagonals are guaranteed to be perpendicular is the rhombus.

Answer: rhombus. (Option 4)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: If P(2, 4), Q(8, 12), R(10, 14) and S(x, y) are vertices of a parallelogram, then what is (x + y) equal to?
questions.solution:
One convenient way is to use the fact that the diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.

Midpoint of PR

$$\Bigl(\frac{2+10}{2},\,\frac{4+14}{2}\Bigr)=(6,9).$$

Midpoint of QS

$$\Bigl(\frac{8+x}{2},\,\frac{12+y}{2}\Bigr)=(6,9).$$

From

$$\frac{8+x}{2}=6\quad\Longrightarrow\quad x=4,$$

$$\frac{12+y}{2}=9\quad\Longrightarrow\quad y=6.$$

So

$$x+y=4+6=10.$$

Answer: Option 2 (10).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: What are the coordinates of vertex D?
questions.solution:
ABCD is an isosceles trapezium, with AB parallel to DC. The given vertices are A(2, 3), B(4, 3), and C(5, 1). We need to find the coordinates of vertex D.

In an isosceles trapezium, the diagonals bisect each other. Let O be the midpoint of both diagonals AC and BD.

To find the midpoint O:

For diagonal AC:

x-coordinate of O: $\frac{5+2}{2} = \frac{7}{2} = 3.5$

y-coordinate of O: $\frac{1+3}{2} = \frac{4}{2} = 2$

For diagonal BD:

Using the same midpoint, equate it for x and y:

$\frac{4 + a}{2} = 3.5$ which implies $a = 3$

$\frac{3 + b}{2} = 2$ which implies $b = 1$

Thus, the coordinates of D are (3, 1).

Therefore, the correct option is (3, 1).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: What is the point of intersection of the diagonals of the trapezium?
questions.solution:
In the isosceles trapezium ABCD, with AB parallel to DC, we need to find the point of intersection for the diagonals $\overline{AC}$ and $\overline{BD}$.

Given the vertices of the trapezium:

$A(2, 3)$

$B(4, 3)$

$C(5, 1)$

Point of Intersection of Diagonals

For an isosceles trapezium, the diagonals bisect each other. Therefore, the point of intersection, denoted as $O$, is the midpoint of both diagonals.

To find $O$, we use the midpoint formula:

$ O = \left( \frac{x_1 + x_2}{2}, \frac{y_1 + y_2}{2} \right) $

Calculation for the Diagonal $\overline{AC}$:

Coordinates of $A$ are $(2, 3)$.

Coordinates of $C$ are $(5, 1)$.

Applying the formula:

$ O = \left( \frac{2 + 5}{2}, \frac{3 + 1}{2} \right) $

This simplifies to:

$ O = \left( \frac{7}{2}, \frac{4}{2} \right) = \left( \frac{7}{2}, 2 \right) $

Therefore, the point of intersection $O$ is $\left( \frac{7}{2}, 2 \right)$.

Conclusion: The point of intersection of the diagonals is $(\frac{7}{2}, 2)$.

Thus, Option 3 is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: The number of points represented by the equation $x = 5$ on the $xy$-plane is
questions.solution:
The equation $$x = 5$$ represents a vertical line on the $xy$-plane where the $$x$$-coordinate of every point on that line is 5. To understand why this is the case, consider the following:

In a standard Cartesian coordinate system, an equation of the form $$x = c$$ (where $$c$$ is a constant) describes a vertical line that intersects the x-axis at $$x = c$$. All points on this line share the same $$x$$-coordinate, but their $$y$$-coordinates can vary freely. Consequently, the line will include every possible $$y$$-coordinate associated with the given $$x$$-coordinate.

Therefore, the number of points described by the equation $$x = 5$$ is indeed infinitely many, because for every possible value of $$y$$, there exists a corresponding point (5, $$y$$) on the line.

Based on the options provided:

Option A: Zero - This would imply no points exist, which is incorrect.

Option B: One - This would imply only one point exists, which is incorrect.

Option C: Two - This would imply only two points exist, which is incorrect.

Option D: Infinitely many - This accurately describes the situation.

The correct answer is:

Option D: Infinitely many

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: If a variable line passes through the point of intersection of the lines $x + 2y - 1 = 0$ and $2x - y - 1 = 0$ and meets the coordinate axes in $A$ and $B$, then what is the locus of the mid-point of $AB$?
questions.solution:
To solve this problem, we need to find the locus of the midpoint $M$ of a variable line $AB$ that passes through a fixed point $P$ and meets the coordinate axes at points $A$ and $B$. Here are the steps:

Step 1: Find the Point of Intersection $P$

First, find the intersection point $P$ of the two given lines:

$x + 2y - 1 = 0$  (Equation 1)

$2x - y - 1 = 0$  (Equation 2)

Solve Equation 1 for $x$:

$x = 1 - 2y$

Substitute $x$ into Equation 2:

$2(1 - 2y) - y - 1 = 0$

$2 - 4y - y - 1 = 0$

$1 - 5y = 0$

$y = \frac{1}{5}$

Now find $x$:

$x = 1 - 2\left( \frac{1}{5} \right) = \frac{3}{5}$

So, point $P$ is $\left( \frac{3}{5}, \frac{1}{5} \right)$.

Step 2: Write the Equation of the Variable Line $L$

Since $L$ passes through $A(a, 0)$, $B(0, b)$, and $P$, its equation in intercept form is:

$\frac{x}{a} + \frac{y}{b} = 1$

Substitute $P$ into the equation:

$\frac{\frac{3}{5}}{a} + \frac{\frac{1}{5}}{b} = 1$

$\frac{3}{5a} + \frac{1}{5b} = 1$

$ \frac{3}{a} + \frac{1}{b} = 5$  (Equation 3)

Step 3: Find the Coordinates of the Midpoint $M$

The midpoint $M$ of $AB$ has coordinates:

$M_x = \frac{a + 0}{2} = \frac{a}{2}$

$M_y = \frac{0 + b}{2} = \frac{b}{2}$

So:

$a = 2M_x$

$b = 2M_y$

Step 4: Derive the Locus Equation

Substitute $a$ and $b$ into Equation 3:

$\frac{3}{2M_x} + \frac{1}{2M_y} = 5$

Multiply both sides by $2$:

$\frac{3}{M_x} + \frac{1}{M_y} = 10$

Multiply both sides by $M_x M_y$:

$3M_y + M_x = 10M_x M_y$

Rewriting:

$10M_x M_y - M_x - 3M_y = 0$

Step 5: Match with the Given Options

Compare the derived equation with the options:

Option A: $3x + y = 10xy$

Option B: $x + 3y = 10xy$

Option C: $3x + y = 10$

Option D: $x + 3y = 10$

Substituting $M_x = x$ and $M_y = y$ into our equation:

$10xy - x - 3y = 0$

This matches Option B when rearranged:

$x + 3y = 10xy$

Answer:

$\boxed{x\ +\ 3y\ =\ 10xy}$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: What is the equation to the straight line passing through the point $(-sin\theta, cos\theta)$ and perpendicular to the line $xcos\theta + ysin\theta = 9$?
questions.solution:
To determine the equation of a straight line passing through the point $(-\sin\theta, \cos\theta)$ and perpendicular to the line $x\cos\theta + y\sin\theta = 9$, let's go through the steps systematically:

1. Identify the slope of the given line:

The given line is $x\cos\theta + y\sin\theta = 9$. This equation can be written in the slope-intercept form $y = mx + c$.

Rewriting, we get:

$$ y\sin\theta = -x\cos\theta + 9 $$

$$ y = -\frac{\cos\theta}{\sin\theta} x + \frac{9}{\sin\theta} $$

Thus, the slope $ m $ of the line is:

$$ m = -\frac{\cos\theta}{\sin\theta} = -\cot\theta $$

2. Determine the slope of the perpendicular line:

The slope of a line perpendicular to another line with slope $ m $ is the negative reciprocal of $ m $. So, if the slope of the given line is $ -\cot\theta $, the slope of the perpendicular line will be:

$$ m_{\text{perpendicular}} = \tan\theta $$

3. Equation of the line with the derived slope:

The line passes through the point $(-\sin\theta, \cos\theta)$ and has a slope $ \tan\theta $. The point-slope form of the equation of a line is:

$$ y - y_1 = m (x - x_1) $$

where $ (x_1, y_1) $ is the point $(-\sin\theta, \cos\theta)$ and $ m $ is the slope $ \tan\theta $.

Substitute the values:

$$ y - \cos\theta = \tan\theta (x + \sin\theta) $$

$$ y - \cos\theta = \tan\theta x + \tan\theta \sin\theta $$

4. Simplify the equation:

Recall that $ \tan\theta = \frac{\sin\theta}{\cos\theta} $, so:

$$ y - \cos\theta = \frac{\sin\theta}{\cos\theta} x + \frac{\sin\theta}{\cos\theta} \sin\theta $$

Multiply through by $ \cos\theta $ to clear the fraction:

$$ y \cos\theta - \cos^2\theta = x \sin\theta + \sin^2\theta $$

Rearrange terms to standard linear form $ Ax + By + C = 0 $:

$$ x \sin\theta - y \cos\theta + ( \sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta ) = 0 $$

Since $ \sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = 1 $:

$$ x \sin\theta - y \cos\theta + 1 = 0 $$

Thus, the final equation of the line is:

Option B: $$ x \sin\theta - y \cos\theta + 1 = 0 $$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: Two points $P$ and $Q$ lie on line $y = 2x + 3$. These two points $P$ and $Q$ are at a distance 2 units from another point $R(1, 5)$. What are the coordinates of the points $P$ and $Q$?
questions.solution:
To find the coordinates of points $P$ and $Q$, we need to use the properties of the line and distance formula. Let's go through the calculations step-by-step.

First, we know the line equation is $y = 2x + 3$. Any point $(x, y)$ on this line can be expressed in the terms of $x$ as $(x, 2x + 3)$. Given a point $R(1, 5)$ and knowing that points $P$ and $Q$ are at a distance of 2 units from $R$, we will use the distance formula:

$$ d = \sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2} $$

Here, $d = 2$, $(x_1, y_1) = (1, 5)$, and $(x_2, y_2) = (x, 2x + 3)$. Therefore:

$$ 2 = \sqrt{(x - 1)^2 + (2x + 3 - 5)^2} $$

Simplify the expression inside the square root:

$$ 2 = \sqrt{(x - 1)^2 + (2x - 2)^2} $$

Further simplifying:

$$ 2 = \sqrt{(x - 1)^2 + 4(x - 1)^2} $$

Combine the terms inside the square root:

$$ 2 = \sqrt{1( x - 1 )^2 + 4 ( x - 1 )^2} $$

$$ 2 = \sqrt{5 ( x - 1 )^2} $$

Square both sides to eliminate the square root:

$$ 4 = 5(x - 1)^2 $$

Divide by 5:

$$ \frac{4}{5} = (x - 1)^2 $$

Take the square root of both sides:

$$ x - 1 = \pm \sqrt{\frac{4}{5}} $$

$$ x - 1 = \pm \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}} $$

Solve for $x$:

$$ x = 1 \pm \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}} $$

Therefore, the x-coordinates of points $P$ and $Q$ are:

$$ x_1 = 1 + \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}} $$

$$ x_2 = 1 - \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}} $$

To find the corresponding $y$ coordinates, substitute back into the line equation $y = 2x + 3$:

For $P \left(1 + \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}, y_P\right)$:

$$ y_P = 2 \left(1 + \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}\right) + 3 $$

$$ y_P = 2 + \frac{4}{\sqrt{5}} + 3 $$

$$ y_P = 5 + \frac{4}{\sqrt{5}} $$

For $Q \left(1 - \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}, y_Q\right)$:

$$ y_Q = 2 \left(1 - \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}\right) + 3 $$

$$ y_Q = 2 - \frac{4}{\sqrt{5}} + 3 $$

$$ y_Q = 5 - \frac{4}{\sqrt{5}} $$

Therefore, the coordinates of points $P$ and $Q$ are:

$$\left(1 + \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}, 5 + \frac{4}{\sqrt{5}}\right)$$ and $$\left(1 - \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}, 5 - \frac{4}{\sqrt{5}}\right)$$

Hence, the correct option is:

Option A: $(1 + \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}, 5 + \frac{4}{\sqrt{5}}), (1 - \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}, 5 - \frac{4}{\sqrt{5}})$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: If two sides of a square lie on the lines $2x + y - 3 = 0$ and $4x + 2y + 5 = 0$, then what is the area of the square in square units?
questions.solution:
To determine the area of the square when two sides lie on the lines $2x + y - 3 = 0$ and $4x + 2y + 5 = 0$, we must first identify that these lines are parallel. We can see that the second equation, when simplified by dividing by 2, also transforms into $2x + y + \frac{5}{2} = 0$, which verifies the parallelism as the coefficients of $x$ and $y$ match.

Next, we need to find the distance between these two parallel lines as it will represent the side length of the square. The general formula for the distance $d$ between two parallel lines of the form $Ax + By + C_1 = 0$ and $Ax + By + C_2 = 0$ is given by:

$$ d = \frac{|C_2 - C_1|}{\sqrt{A^2 + B^2}} $$

In our case, $A = 2$, $B = 1$, $C_1 = -3$ (from the line $2x + y - 3 = 0$), and $C_2 = \frac{5}{2}$ (from the simplified line $2x + y + \frac{5}{2} = 0$). Plugging these values into the equation, we get:

$$ d = \frac{\left|\frac{5}{2} - (-3)\right|}{\sqrt{2^2 + 1^2}} $$

Simplify further:

$$ d = \frac{\left|\frac{5}{2} + 3\right|}{\sqrt{4 + 1}} = \frac{\left|\frac{5}{2} + \frac{6}{2}\right|}{\sqrt{5}} = \frac{\left|\frac{11}{2}\right|}{\sqrt{5}} = \frac{11}{2\sqrt{5}} $$

To rationalize the denominator, we multiply numerator and denominator by $\sqrt{5}$:

$$ d = \frac{11 \cdot \sqrt{5}}{2 \cdot 5} = \frac{11\sqrt{5}}{10} $$

Thus, the side length of the square is $\frac{11\sqrt{5}}{10}$. The area $A$ of a square is given by the square of its side length:

$$ A = \left(\frac{11\sqrt{5}}{10}\right)^2 = \frac{121 \cdot 5}{100} = \frac{605}{100} = 6.05 $$

Hence, the area of the square is 6.05 square units.

The correct option is:

Option A: 6.05

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: ABC is a triangle with A(3, 5). The mid-points of sides AB, AC are at (-1, 2), (6, 4) respectively. What are the coordinates of centroid of the triangle ABC?
questions.solution:
To find the coordinates of the centroid of the triangle ABC, let's first determine the coordinates of points B and C using the given information about the midpoints of the sides AB and AC.

Let $ B(x_1, y_1) $ and $ C(x_2, y_2) $. Given the midpoints of AB and AC are at $-1, 2$ and $6, 4$ respectively, we can use the midpoint formula to find the coordinates of B and C.

The midpoint of AB is given by:

$$(-1, 2) = \left(\frac{3 + x_1}{2}, \frac{5 + y_1}{2}\right)$$

This gives us two equations:

$$\frac{3 + x_1}{2} = -1$$

$$\frac{5 + y_1}{2} = 2$$

Solving these equations:

$$3 + x_1 = -2 \Rightarrow x_1 = -5$$

$$5 + y_1 = 4 \Rightarrow y_1 = -1$$

So, the coordinates of point B are (-5, -1).

Similarly, the midpoint of AC is given by:

$$ (6, 4) = \left(\frac{3 + x_2}{2}, \frac{5 + y_2}{2}\right) $$

This gives us two equations:

$$ \frac{3 + x_2}{2} = 6 $$

$$ \frac{5 + y_2}{2} = 4 $$

Solving these equations:

$$ 3 + x_2 = 12 \Rightarrow x_2 = 9 $$

$$ 5 + y_2 = 8 \Rightarrow y_2 = 3 $$

So, the coordinates of point C are (9, 3).

Now that we have the coordinates of all vertices A(3, 5), B(-5, -1), and C(9, 3), we can find the centroid $G$ of the triangle using the centroid formula:

$$ G\left(\frac{x_1 + x_2 + x_3}{3}, \frac{y_1 + y_2 + y_3}{3}\right) $$

Substituting the coordinates:

$$ G\left(\frac{3 + (-5) + 9}{3}, \frac{5 + (-1) + 3}{3}\right) $$

$$ G\left(\frac{7}{3}, \frac{7}{3}\right) $$

Hence, the coordinates of the centroid of triangle ABC are:

$$\left(\frac{7}{3}, \frac{7}{3}\right)$$

The correct option is:

Option B

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: ABC is an acute angled isosceles triangle. Two equal sides AB and AC lie on the lines 7x - y - 3 = 0 and x + y - 5 = 0. If θ is one of the equal angles, then what is cotθ equal to?
questions.solution:
To solve this problem, we need to find $\cot \theta$ where $\theta$ is one of the equal angles in an acute-angled isosceles triangle $ABC$ with equal sides $AB = AC$. The sides $AB$ and $AC$ lie on the lines $7x - y - 3 = 0$ and $x + y - 5 = 0$, respectively.

Step 1: Find the Point of Intersection (Point A)

First, we find point $A$ by solving the equations of the two lines:

$7x - y - 3 = 0$ $\implies y = 7x - 3$

$x + y - 5 = 0$ $\implies y = 5 - x$

Set the $y$ values equal:

$7x - 3 = 5 - x$

$8x = 8$

$x = 1$

$y = 5 - 1 = 4$

So, point $A$ is $(1, 4)$.

Step 2: Determine the Slopes of the Lines

Calculate the slopes ($m_1$ and $m_2$) of the lines:

Line $AB$: $7x - y - 3 = 0$ $\implies y = 7x - 3$, so $m_1 = 7$.

Line $AC$: $x + y - 5 = 0$ $\implies y = -x + 5$, so $m_2 = -1$.

Step 3: Find the Angle Between the Lines (Angle at A, $\alpha$)

Use the formula for the angle between two lines:

$\tan \alpha = \left| \frac{m_2 - m_1}{1 + m_1 m_2} \right|$

$\tan \alpha = \left| \frac{-1 - 7}{1 + (7)(-1)} \right| = \left| \frac{-8}{1 - 7} \right| = \left| \frac{-8}{-6} \right| = \frac{4}{3}$

So, $\tan \alpha = \frac{4}{3}$.

Step 4: Calculate $\theta$

Since the triangle is isosceles with equal angles $\theta$, and the sum of angles in a triangle is $180^\circ$:

$\alpha + 2\theta = 180^\circ$

$\theta = \frac{180^\circ - \alpha}{2}$

Calculate $\alpha$ using $\tan \alpha = \frac{4}{3}$:

$\alpha = \arctan\left( \frac{4}{3} \right) \approx 53.13^\circ$

Now, find $\theta$:

$\theta = \frac{180^\circ - 53.13^\circ}{2} \approx 63.43^\circ$

Step 5: Find $\cot \theta$

Recall the identity:

$\cot \theta = \tan \left( \frac{\alpha}{2} \right)$

Compute $\tan \left( \frac{\alpha}{2} \right)$:

$\alpha = 53.13^\circ \implies \frac{\alpha}{2} = 26.565^\circ$

$\tan \left( \frac{\alpha}{2} \right) = \tan 26.565^\circ \approx 0.5$

Therefore:

$\cot \theta = \tan \left( \frac{\alpha}{2} \right) = 0.5$

Answer:

$\displaystyle \frac{1}{2}$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: Let $x+2 y+1=0$ and $2 x+3 y+4=0$ are two lines of regression computed from some bivariate data. If $\theta$ is the acute angle between them, then what is the value of $488 \tan 3 \theta$ ?
questions.solution:
The slopes of the regression lines are given by the coefficients of $x$ in the equations. So, the slopes are $-1/2$ and $-2/3$.

Let $\theta$ be the acute angle between the lines. Then, we have:

$$ \tan \theta = \left| \frac{\frac{-1}{2} - \frac{-2}{3}}{1 + \frac{-1}{2} \cdot \frac{-2}{3}} \right| = \frac{1}{8} $$.

Using the triple angle formula for tangent, we get:

$$ \tan 3 \theta = \frac{3 \tan \theta - \tan^3 \theta}{1 - 3 \tan^2 \theta} $$.

Substituting $\tan \theta = \frac{1}{8}$, we get:

$$ \tan 3 \theta = \frac{3 \cdot \frac{1}{8} - \left( \frac{1}{8} \right)^3}{1 - 3 \left( \frac{1}{8} \right)^2} = \frac{191}{488} $$.

Therefore, $488 \tan 3 \theta = \boxed{191}$.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question:
Match List I with List II.

List I
$($ Complex)		 List II
(Oxidation
number of metal)
A. $\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CO})_4$ I. +1
B. $\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_5 \mathrm{NO}\right]^{2+}$ II. Zero
C. $\left[\mathrm{Co}(\mathrm{CO})_5\right]^{2-}$ III. -1
D. $\left[\mathrm{Cr}_2(\mathrm{CO})_{10}\right]^{2-}$ IV. -2

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Let’s determine the oxidation number of the metal in each complex step by step.

For A. $$\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CO})_4$$

Each $$CO$$ ligand is neutral.

The overall complex is neutral.

Therefore, the oxidation number on Ni is $$0$$.

This matches List II item: II. Zero.

For B. $$\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_2\mathrm{O}\right)_5 \mathrm{NO}\right]^{2+}$$

Water ($$H_2O$$) is neutral.

In many nitrosyl complexes, when the $$NO$$ ligand binds linearly, it is considered to act as $$NO^+$$ (nitrosonium).

If we assign $$+1$$ to the $$NO$$ ligand, let the oxidation state of Fe be $$x$$:

$$x + (+1)= +2$$

So, $$x = +1$$.

This matches List II item: I. +1.

For C. $$\left[\mathrm{Co}(\mathrm{CO})_5\right]^{2-}$$

Again, all carbonyl ($$CO$$) ligands are neutral.

The overall complex carries a charge of $$2-$$.

Hence, the oxidation number on Co is $$-2$$.

This matches List II item: IV. -2.

For D. $$\left[\mathrm{Cr}_2(\mathrm{CO})_{10}\right]^{2-}$$

There are two chromium atoms and all $$CO$$ ligands are neutral.

Let the oxidation number on each Cr be $$x$$. Since the overall charge is $$2-$$:

$$2x = -2$$

So, $$x = -1$$.

This matches List II item: III. -1.

Thus, the correct matching is:

A-II, B-I, C-IV, D-III

Among the options given, this corresponds to Option A.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Chemistry Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
The oxidation number of the central atom in a complex is defined as the charge it would carry if all the ligands are removed along with the electron pairs that are shared with the central atom. Similarly the charge on the complex is the sum of the charges of the constituent parts i.e. the sum of the charges on the central metal ion and its surrounding ligands. Based on this, the complex is called neutral if the sum of the charges of the constituents is equal to zero. However, for an anion or cationic complex, the sum of the charges of the constituents is equal to the charge on the coordination sphere.

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

(a) Define ambidentate ligand with an example.

(b) What type of isomerism is shown by

$$\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_5 \mathrm{Cl}\right] \mathrm{SO}_4 \text { and }\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_5 \mathrm{SO}_4\right] \mathrm{Cl} \text { ? }$$

(c) Define chelate effect. How it affects the stability of complex?

OR

(c) Find the coordination number and oxidation state of chromium in $\mathrm{Na}_3\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4\right)_3\right]$.
questions.solution:
(a) A ligand which has two different donor atoms and either of the two sites in the ligand can form coordinate bonds with the central atom is called an ambidentate ligand.

$$E x:-O-N=O$$

(b) Ionisation isomerism

(c) When a ligand attaches to the metal ion in a manner that forms a ring, then a chelate is formed. A bidentate or a polydentate ligand contains donor atoms positioned in such a way that when they coordinate with the central mental ion, a 5 - or 6 - membered ring is formed, the effect is known as the chelate effect. The complex resulting from coordination with the chelating ligand is much more thermodynamically stable.

OR

(c) Coordination no. $=$ No. of ligands $\times$ denticity of ligand

$$\begin{aligned} & =3 \times 2 \\ & =6 \end{aligned}$$

Since overall charge of the ligand + metal ion is +3 , the oxidation no. of Cr is +3

questions.question:
Among the following complexes, the total number of diamagnetic species is ___________.

$\left[\mathrm{Mn}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{3+},\left[\mathrm{MnCl}_6\right]^{3-},\left[\mathrm{FeF}_6\right]^{3-},\left[\mathrm{CoF}_6\right]^{3-},\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{3+}$, and $\left[\mathrm{Co}(\mathrm{en})_3\right]^{3+}$

[Given, atomic number: $\mathrm{Mn}=25, \mathrm{Fe}=26, \mathrm{Co}=27$;

$$ \text { en } \left.=\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{NCH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{NH}_2\right] $$
questions.solution:
$$ \mathrm{Mn}^{3+} \Rightarrow[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^4 $$

$\mathrm{d}^4$ configuration in $\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}$ and $\mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}}$ orbitals will always have unpaired electrons irrespective of SFL and WFL.

$$ \mathrm{Fe}^{3+} \Rightarrow[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^5 $$

$\mathrm{d}^5$ configuration will also have unpaired electron irrespective of SFL and WFL.

$$ \mathrm{Co}^{3+} \Rightarrow[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^6 $$

$\mathrm{d}^6 \Rightarrow$ it can be both paramagnetic or diamagnetic based on field of ligands.

In case of $\mathrm{F}^{-} \Rightarrow$ weak field ligand, configuration will be $\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}^4 \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}}^2$ hence it is paramagnetic but in case of $\mathrm{en} \Rightarrow$ strong filed ligand, configuration will be $\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}^6 \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}}^0$ hence it will be diamagnetic.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: A conducting wire is stretched by applying a deforming force, so that its diameter decreases to $ 40 \% $ of original value. The percentage change in its resistance will be
questions.solution:
When a conducting wire is stretched due to a deforming force, its diameter decreases to 40% of its original value. We need to determine the percentage change in its resistance.

Conceptual Framework:

Volume Conservation:

When the wire is stretched, its volume ($V$) remains constant. Therefore, the relationship between volume, cross-sectional area ($A$), and length ($l$) is given by:

$ V = A \times l $

Solving for $l$, we have:

$ l = \frac{V}{A} $

Resistance Formula:

The resistance ($R$) of a wire is determined by its resistivity ($\rho$), length, and cross-sectional area:

$ R = \rho \cdot \frac{l}{A} = \frac{\rho V}{A^2} $

Given the relation between area and diameter ($D$), we express $A$ in terms of $D$:

$ A = \frac{\pi D^2}{4} $

Thus, resistance can be reformulated as:

$ R = \frac{\rho V}{A^2} = \frac{16 \rho V}{\pi^2 D^4} $

Change in Resistance:

The percentage change in resistance can be calculated using the change in diameter:

$ \frac{\Delta R}{R} = -4 \frac{\Delta D}{D} $

Given the diameter decreases to 40% of its original value, the change in diameter is $\Delta D = -0.6D$.

Substituting this into our percentage change formula, we get:

$ \frac{\Delta R}{R} = -4(-0.6) = 4 \times 0.6 = 1.6\% $

Therefore, the percentage change in resistance as a result of the stretching is 1.6%.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Find the average value of current shown graphically from $ t=0 $ to $ t=2 \mathrm{~s} $.
questions.solution:
To find the average value of the current from $ t = 0 $ to $ t = 2 $ seconds, we analyze the current-time ($ i-t $) graph.

Calculate the Area under the Graph:

The graph consists of two triangular sections.

First Triangle: From $ t = 0 $ to $ t = 1 $ second, with a height of 10 A.

$ \text{Area of first triangle} = \frac{1}{2} \times \text{base} \times \text{height} = \frac{1}{2} \times 1 \times 10 = 5 \text{ A} $

Second Triangle: From $ t = 1 $ to $ t = 2 $ seconds, with a height of 10 A.

$ \text{Area of second triangle} = \frac{1}{2} \times (2-1) \times 10 = 5 \text{ A} $

Total Area:

Total area under the graph from $ t = 0 $ to $ t = 2 $ seconds is:

$ 5 \text{ A} + 5 \text{ A} = 10 \text{ A} $

Calculate the Average Current:

The average current $(i_{\mathrm{av}})$ is given by:

$ i_{\mathrm{av}} = \frac{\text{Total Area}}{\text{Time Interval}} = \frac{10}{2} = 5 \text{ A} $

Thus, the average current over the interval from $ t = 0 $ to $ t = 2 $ seconds is $ 5 \, \text{A} $.

questions.question: In the given circuit, $ E_{1}=E_{2}=E_{3}=2 \mathrm{~V} $ and $ R_{1}=R_{2}=4 \Omega $, then current flowing through the branch $ A B $ is
questions.solution:
The given circuit can be redrawn as,

where, equivalent emf of the combination,

$ \begin{aligned} E_{\mathrm{eq}} & =\frac{E_{1} R_{1}+E_{2} R_{2}}{R_{1}+R_{2}} \\ & =\frac{2 \times 4+2 \times 4}{4+4}=2 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned} $

and $ R_{e q}=\frac{R_{1} R_{2}}{R_{1}+R_{2}}=\frac{4 \times 4}{4+4}=\frac{16}{8}=2 \Omega $

Net emf of the circuit,

$ \begin{aligned} E^{\prime} & =E_{2}+E_{\mathrm{eq}} \\ & =2+2 \\ & =4 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned} $

$ \therefore $ Current flowing through branch $ A B $,

$ I=\frac{E^{\prime}}{R_{\mathrm{eq}}}=\frac{4}{2}=2 \mathrm{~A} $

Clearly from figure, it is clear that direction of current is from point $ A $ to $ B $ clockwise is loop.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: A galvanometer of resistance $50 \Omega$ is converted into a voltmeter of range $(0-2 \mathrm{~V})$ using a resistor of $1.0 \mathrm{k} \Omega$. If it is to be converted into a voltmeter of range ( $0-10 \mathrm{~V}$ ), the resistance required will be
questions.solution:
To convert a galvanometer into a voltmeter, certain resistors are added in series with the galvanometer. Here's the derivation to determine the resistance needed for a specified voltage range of the voltmeter:

Initial Setup for Range (0-2 V):

The original configuration uses the formula:

$ V = I_g(G + R) $

where:

$ V $ is the voltage,

$ I_g $ is the current through the galvanometer,

$ G $ is the resistance of the galvanometer (50 Ω),

$ R $ is the added series resistance.

Substituting the given values for the first configuration:

$ 2 = I_g(50 + 1000) $

Solving for $ I_g $:

$ I_g = \frac{2}{1050} \text{ A} $

Setup for New Range (0-10 V):

For the new desired range, the formula becomes:

$ 10 = I_g(50 + R) $

Substituting the expression found for $ I_g $:

$ 10 = \frac{2}{1050}(50 + R) $

Solving for $ R $:

$ R = \frac{10 \times 1050}{2} - 50 $

$ R = 5.2 \text{ kΩ} $

This calculation shows that to convert the galvanometer to a voltmeter with a range of 0-10 V, a resistance of $ 5.2 \text{ kΩ} $ is required.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: A battery supplies 0.9 A current through a $2 \Omega$ resistor and 0.3 A current through a $7 \Omega$ resistor when connected one by one. The internal resistance of the battery is
questions.solution:
Circuit current

$$I=\frac{E}{r+R} \Rightarrow E=I(r+R)$$

For $2 \Omega,\quad$ $$E=0.9(r+2)\quad\text{.... (i)}$$

$$\text { For } 7 \Omega, \quad E=0.3(r+7) \quad\text{.... (ii)}$$

From equations (i) & (ii),

$$\begin{aligned} 0.9(r+2) & =0.3(r+7) \\ r & =0.5 \Omega \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The spin only magnetic moment of $ \mathrm{Fe}^{3+} $ ion (in BM) is approximately.
questions.solution:
The spin-only magnetic moment of the $ \mathrm{Fe}^{3+} $ ion is determined by its electronic configuration and the number of unpaired electrons.

Electronic Configuration:

The neutral iron atom ($_{26} \mathrm{Fe}$) has the electronic configuration:

$ [\mathrm{Ar}] 3d^6 4s^2 $

When it forms a $ \mathrm{Fe}^{3+} $ ion, it loses three electrons. The configuration becomes:

$ [\mathrm{Ar}] 3d^5 $

Number of Unpaired Electrons:

In the $ \mathrm{Fe}^{3+} $ ion, there are 5 electrons in the 3d subshell. All these electrons are unpaired, hence:

$ n = 5 $

Calculating the Spin-Only Magnetic Moment:

The formula for the spin-only magnetic moment ($\mu_s$) is:

$ \mu_s = \sqrt{n(n+2)} \, \text{BM} $

Substituting $ n = 5 $ into the formula:

$ \mu_s = \sqrt{5(5+2)} = \sqrt{35} $

Calculating further gives:

$ \mu_s \approx 5.85 \, \text{BM} $

Therefore, the spin-only magnetic moment of $ \mathrm{Fe}^{3+} $ is approximately 5.85 Bohr Magnetons ($\mathrm{BM}$), which can be rounded to about 6 $\mathrm{BM}$.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question:
In the following species, how many species have same magnetic moment?

(i) $ \mathrm{Cr}^{2+} $ (ii) $ \mathrm{Mn}^{3+} $ (iii) $ \mathrm{Ni}^{3+} $ (iv) $ \mathrm{Sc}^{2+} $ (v) $ \mathrm{Zn}^{2+} $ (vi) $ \mathrm{V}^{3+} $ (vii) $ \mathrm{Ti}^{+} $
questions.solution:
Given the species, we need to determine which have the same magnetic moment by counting their unpaired electrons.

For the species $ \text{Cr}^{2+} $ and $ \text{Mn}^{3+} $, both have the same number of unpaired electrons:

Number of unpaired electrons, $ n = 4 $

The magnetic moment $\mu$ is calculated using the formula:

$ \mu = \sqrt{n(n+2)} = \sqrt{4(4+2)} = \sqrt{24} \, \text{BM} $

Thus, $ \text{Cr}^{2+} $ and $ \text{Mn}^{3+} $ have the same magnetic moment.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Chemistry Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
Attempt any five of the following:

(a) $\mathrm{Ce}(\mathrm{III})$ is easily oxidised to $\mathrm{Ce}(\mathrm{IV})$. Comment.

(b) $\mathrm{E}^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{Mn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Mn}\right)$ is -1.18 V . Why is this value highly negative in comparison to neighbouring $d$ block elements?

(c) Which element of $3 d$ series has lowest enthalpy of atomisation and why?

(d) What happens when sodium chromate is acidified?

(e) $\mathrm{Zn}, \mathrm{Cd}$ and Hg are soft metals. Why?

(f) Why is permanganate titration not carried out in the presence of HCl ?

(g) The lower oxides of transition metals are basic whereas the highest are amphoteric/acidic. Give reason.
questions.solution:
(a) Ce , having the configuration of $4 f^1 5 d^1 6 s^2$.

Ce (III) will have the configuration: $4 f^1 5 d^0 6 s^0$, it can easily lose an electron to acquire the stable configuration of $4 f^0 5 d^0 6 s^0$.

This loss of an electron transforms it into $\mathrm{Ce}(\mathrm{IV})$, which has the noble gas configuration of xenon (Xe).

(b) The electronic configuration of Mn :

Mn $1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^6 3 s^2 3 p^6 3 d^5 4 s^2$

$\mathrm{Mn}^{+2}$ has a half-filled state of $3 d^5$.

Negative $\mathrm{E}^{\circ}$ values of $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Mn}$ is because of the stabilities of half-filled $\left(3 d^5: \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}\right)$ configuration.

(c) The greater the number of unpaired electrons, the stronger is the resultant bonding and hence, higher is the enthalpy of atomisation. In the $3 d$ series, from Sc to $\mathrm{Zn}, \mathrm{Zn}$ has no unpaired electrons. Hence, it exhibits weak interactions and thus possesses the least enthalpy of atomisation.

(d) At acidic pH , sodium chromate converts to sodium dichromate:

$$2 \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7+2 \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$$

(e) Zinc, cadmium and mercury have a fully filled $d$-subshell, due to which metallic bonds are weak. That's why they are soft and have low melting point.

(f) Permanganate titration is not carried out in the presence of hydrochloric acid because some of the hydrochloric acid gets oxidised to chlorine gas. Hence, we do not get the correct endpoint for any given titration.

(g) In low oxidation state of the metal, some of the valence electrons of the metal atom are not involved in bonding. Hence it can donate electrons and behave as a base. On the other hand, in higher oxidation state of the metal, valence electrons are involved in bonding and are not available. , effective nuclear charge is high and hence it can accept electrons and behave as an acid.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: $ \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)} $ is
questions.solution:
Let $ I=\int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)} d x $

$ \begin{array}{l} I=\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)-\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)} d x \\ I=\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}-\frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)} d x \\ I=\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left[\frac{1}{b} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{b}-\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}\right]_{0}^{\infty} \\ I=\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left[\frac{1}{b} \times \frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{1}{a} \times \frac{\pi}{2}\right] \\ I=\frac{1}{(a+b)(a-b)}\left[\frac{a-b}{a b}\right] \times \frac{\pi}{2} \\ I=\frac{\pi}{2 a b(a+b)} \end{array} $

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The value of definite integral $ \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \log (\tan x) d x $ is .
questions.solution:
Let $ I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log (\tan x) d x \quad \ldots \text { (i) } $

By applying property,

$ \begin{aligned} \int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x & =\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x \\ I & =\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \left(\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)\right) d x \\ I & =\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log (\cot x) d x \end{aligned} \quad \ldots \text { (ii) } $

Adding Eqs. (i) and (ii),

$ \begin{array}{l} 2 I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}[\log (\tan x)+\log (\cot x)] d x \\ 2 I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log 1 d x=0 \end{array} $
