There are no fixed charges in this instance, and the number of positive and negative mobile charges is equal. As a result, an electrically neutral semiconductor is always uniformly doped.

(b) At equilibrium, the $p-n$ junction has the same number of majority and minority carriers moving in opposite directions. The net current is the sum of drift current and diffusion current. So, the net current will be zero as diffusion and drift currents are equal and opposite.

(c) Since the current in a reverse-biased diode is caused by the minority charge carriers drifting from one area to another through the junction, the reverse current is essentially independent of the critical voltage. Therefore, a modest voltage is sufficient to continue sweeping the minority charge carriers.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
(a) Explain the characteristics of a $p-n$ junction diode that makes it suitable for its use as a rectifier.

(b) With the help of a circuit diagram, explain the working of a full wave rectifier.
questions.solution:
(a) If an alternating voltage is applied across a junction diode, then the current will only in the part where it is forward biased. This property of junction diode can be used to rectify alternating current. The circuit used for this purpose is a rectifier.

(b)

A full wave rectifier consists of two diodes connected in parallel across the ends of secondary winding of a centre tapped step down transformer. The load resistance $R_L$ is connected across secondary winding and the diodes between $A$ and $B$ as shown in the circuit.

During positive half cycle of input a.c., end A of the secondary winding becomes positive and end $B$ negative. Thus, diode $D_1$ becomes forward biased, whereas diode $D_2$ reverse biased. So, diode $D_1$ allows the current to flow through it, while diode $D_2$ does not, and current in the circuit flows from $D_1$ and through load $R_L$ from $X$ to $Y$.

During negative half cycle of input a.c., end A of the secondary winding becomes negative and end $B$ positive, thus diode $D_1$ becomes reverse biased, whereas diode $D_2$ forward biased. So, diode $D_1$ does not allow the current to flow through it but diode $D_2$ does, and current in the circuit flows from $D_2$ and through load $R_L$ from $X$ to $Y$.

Since, in both the half cycles of input a.c., electric current through load $\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{L}}$ flows in the same direction, so d.c. is obtained across $\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{L}}$. Although the direction of electric current through $R_L$ remains same, but its magnitude changes with time, so it is called pulsating d.c.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: A dust particle of mass $ 4 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{mg} $ in suspended in air under the influence of an electric field of $ 50 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{C} $ directed vertically upwards. How many electrons were removed from the neutral dust particle? [Take, $ g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} $ $ { }^{2} $ ]
questions.solution:
To determine how many electrons have been removed from a neutral dust particle suspended in an electric field, let's go through the calculations step by step.

Given:

Mass of the dust particle, $ m = 4 \times 10^{-12} \text{ mg} $

Conversion to kilograms:

$ m = 4 \times 10^{-12} \times 10^{-3} \text{ g} = 4 \times 10^{-12} \times 10^{-3} \times 10^{-3} \text{ kg} = 4 \times 10^{-18} \text{ kg} $

Electric field strength, $ E = 50 \text{ N/C} $

Gravitational acceleration, $ g = 10 \text{ m/s}^2 $

Calculate the Weight:

The weight $ W $ of the dust particle is given by:

$ W = m \times g = 4 \times 10^{-18} \times 10 = 4 \times 10^{-17} \text{ N} $

Equating Electric Force to Weight:

The electric force $ F_e $ experienced by the dust particle is:

$ F_e = q \times E $

Where $ q = n \times e $, with $ e = 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \text{ C} $ as the charge of one electron, and $ n $ being the number of electrons removed.

At equilibrium, the electric force equals the weight of the particle:

$ n \times e \times E = W $

$ n \times 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \times 50 = 4 \times 10^{-17} $

Solving for $ n $:

$ n = \frac{4 \times 10^{-17}}{1.6 \times 10^{-19} \times 50} $

$ n = \frac{400}{80} = 5 $

Therefore, 5 electrons were removed from the neutral dust particle.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: An oil drop of radius $ 1 \mu \mathrm{~m} $ is held stationary under a constant electric field of $ 3.65 \times 10^{4} \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{C} $ due to some excess electrons presence on it. If the density of oil drop is $ 1.26 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cm}^{3} $, then number of excess electrons on the oil drop approximately are [Take, $ g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2} $ ]
questions.solution:
An oil drop with a radius of $1 \, \mu \mathrm{m}$ is held stationary in a constant electric field of $3.65 \times 10^{4} \, \mathrm{N/C}$ due to excess electrons on it. The oil drop's density is $1.26 \, \mathrm{g/cm}^{3}$. To find the number of excess electrons on the oil drop, consider the following details:

Convert the given measurements:

Radius of the drop, $ r = 1 \, \mu \mathrm{m} = 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m} $.

Density of the oil, $\rho_{\text{oil}}$, is converted to:

$ \rho_{\text{oil}} = 1.26 \, \mathrm{g/cm}^{3} = 1.26 \times 10^{3} \, \mathrm{kg/m}^{3} $

Since the droplet is stationary, the gravitational force acting downward is balanced by the electric force acting upward:

$ \text{Weight of the droplet} = \text{Force due to the electric field} $

Mathematically, this can be expressed as:

$ \frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3} \rho_{\text{oil}} \cdot g = qE $

where $ q $ is the charge due to excess electrons. If $ n $ is the number of excess electrons, then:

$ q = ne $

Substitute this into the previous equation:

$ \frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3} \rho_{\text{oil}} \cdot g = neE $

Solving for $ n $:

$ n = \frac{4 \pi r^{3} \rho_{\text{oil}} g}{3eE} $

Substitute the known values into the equation to calculate $ n $:

$ \begin{aligned} n & = \frac{4 \times 3.14 \times (10^{-6})^{3} \times 1.26 \times 10^{3} \times 10}{3 \times 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \times 3.65 \times 10^{4}} \\ & = 9.03 \approx 9 \end{aligned} $

Thus, there are approximately 9 excess electrons on the oil drop.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Electric field at point $ (30,30,0) $ due to a charge of $ 0.008 \mu \mathrm{C} $ placed at origin will be, (coordinates are in cm )
questions.solution:
The electric field $\mathbf{E}$ at point $(30, 30, 0)$ due to a charge of $0.008 \, \mu \mathrm{C}$ placed at the origin can be calculated using the formula:

$ \mathbf{E} = \frac{K q}{r^3} \cdot \mathbf{r} $

Step 1: Calculate the distance $r$:

$ r = \sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2 + (z_2 - z_1)^2} $

Substitute the given coordinates:

$ r = \sqrt{30^2 + 30^2 + 0^2} = 30 \sqrt{2} \, \text{cm} = 30 \sqrt{2} \times 10^{-2} \, \text{m} $

Step 2: Convert charge $q$ to Coulombs:

$ q = 8 \times 10^{-3} \, \mu\mathrm{C} = 8 \times 10^{-3} \times 10^{-6} \, \text{C} $

Step 3: The position vector $\mathbf{r}$ is:

$ \mathbf{r} = (30 \hat{\mathbf{i}} + 30 \hat{\mathbf{j}}) \times 10^{-2} \, \text{m} $

Step 4: Substitute into the electric field formula:

$ \mathbf{E} = \frac{9 \times 10^9 \times 8 \times 10^{-3} \times 10^{-6}}{\left(30 \sqrt{2} \times 10^{-2}\right)^3} \times (30 \hat{\mathbf{i}} + 30 \hat{\mathbf{j}}) \times 10^{-2} $

Calculation:

$ \mathbf{E} = \frac{9 \times 8 \times 10^9 \times 10^{-11}}{27 \times 2 \sqrt{2} \times 10^{-6} \times 10^3} \times 30(\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}}) $

Simplify the expression:

$ \mathbf{E} = \frac{9 \times 8 \times 10^2 \times 3}{27 \times 2 \sqrt{2}}(\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}}) $

$ \mathbf{E} = 200 \sqrt{2}(\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}}) \, \text{NC}^{-1} $

Thus, the electric field at the given point is $200 \sqrt{2}(\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}}) \, \mathrm{NC}^{-1}$.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The potential of a large liquid drop when eight liquid drops are combined is 20 V , then the potential of each single drop was
questions.solution:
When eight small liquid drops combine to form one large drop, the potential of the large drop is given as 20 V. We need to find the potential of each individual small drop.

Solution

Volume Relationship:

The total volume of the eight small drops is equal to the volume of the one large drop. This relationship is expressed as:

$ \left(\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3\right) \times 8 = \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 $

Simplifying gives:

$ 2r = R \quad \ldots \text{(i)} $

This equation indicates the radius of the large drop $ R $ is twice the radius of one small drop $ r $.

Charge Conservation:

The total charge of the eight small drops is equal to the charge of the large drop:

$ 8q = Q \quad \ldots \text{(ii)} $

Potential Derivation:

The potential for one small drop ($ V' $) and the large drop ($ V $) are given by:

$ V' = \frac{q}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 r} $

$ V = \frac{Q}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 R} $

Finding the Ratio:

Using the above expressions, we derive:

$ \frac{V'}{V} = \frac{q}{Q} \times \frac{R}{r} $

Substituting from equations (i) and (ii), we get:

$ \frac{V'}{20} = \frac{q}{8q} \times \frac{2r}{r} $

This simplifies to:

$ \frac{V'}{20} = \frac{1}{4} $

Calculating $ V' $:

Solving for $ V' $, we find:

$ V' = 5 \, \text{V} $

Thus, the potential of each single small liquid drop is 5 V.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Equal amount of positive and negative charges are distributed uniformly on two halves of a thin circular ring as shown in figure. The resultant electric field at the centre O of the ring is along OC.

Reason (R): It is so because the net potential at $O$ is not zero.
options:
If both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is correct explanation of Assertion (A).
If both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
If Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
If both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are false.
questions.solution: Electric field is from positive charge to negative charge, hence the direction of electric field will be along OC. Also, the net potential at O is zero. Hence, assertion is true and reason is false.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
(a) Define the term 'electric flux' and write its dimensions.

(b) A plane surface, in shape of a square of side 1 cm is placed in an electric field $\vec{E}=\left(100 \frac{\mathrm{~N}}{\mathrm{C}}\right) \hat{i}$ such that the unit vector normal to the surface is given by $\hat{n}=0.8 \hat{i}+0.6 \hat{k}$. Find the electric flux through the surface.
questions.solution:
(a) The total number of electric field lines passing through an area per unit time is defined as electric flux. It is represented by $\phi$.

$$\phi=\vec{E} \cdot \vec{A}$$

Its dimensions are,

$$\begin{aligned} \phi & =\left[M L T^{-3} I^{-1}\right] \cdot\left[L^2\right] \\ \phi & =\left[M L^3 T^{-3} I^{-1}\right] \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text{(b)}\quad \vec{E} & =100 \frac{\mathrm{~N}}{\mathrm{C}} \hat{i} \\ \text { side } & =1 \mathrm{~cm}=0.01 \mathrm{~m} \\ \text{Surface area,}\quad A & =(0.01)^2=1 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m}^2 \end{aligned}$$

Normal unit vector, $\hat{n}=0.8 \hat{i}+0.6 \hat{k}$

Angle, $$\theta=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{0.6}{0.8}\right)=36.87^{\circ}$$

Now,

$$\begin{aligned} & \phi=E A \cos \theta \\ & \phi=100\left(1 \times 10^{-4}\right) \cos 36.87 \\ & \phi=10^{-2} \times 0.8 \\ & \phi=8 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{Nm}^2 / \mathrm{C} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
(a) (i) Draw equipotential surfaces for an electric dipole.

(ii) Two point charges $q_1$ and $q_2$ are located at $\overrightarrow{r_1}$ and $\vec{r}_2$ respectively in an external electric field $\vec{E}$. Obtain an expression for the potential energy of the system.

(iii) The dipole moment of a molecule is $10^{-30} \mathrm{Cm}$. It is placed in an electric field $\vec{E}$ of $10^5 \mathrm{~V} / \mathrm{m}$ such that its axis is along the electric field. The direction of $\vec{E}$ is suddenly changed by $60^{\circ}$ at an instant. Find the change in the potential energy of the dipole, at that instant.

OR

(b) (i) A thin spherical shell of radius R has a uniform surface charge density $\sigma$. Using Gauss' law, deduce an expression for electric field (i) outside and (ii) inside the shell.

(ii) Two long straight thin wires AB and CD have linear charge densities $10 \mu \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m}$ and $-20 \mu \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m}$, respectively. They are kept parallel to each other at a distance 1 m . Find magnitude and direction of the net electric field at a point midway between them.
questions.solution:
(a) (i)

(ii) Work done is bringing the charge $q_1$ from infinity to the position $r_1$,

$$W_1=q_1 V\left(r_1\right)$$

Work done in bringing the charge $q_2$ to the position $r_2$

$$W_2=q_2 V\left(r_2\right)+\frac{q_1 q_2}{4 \pi \varepsilon_o r_2}$$

Hence, total potential energy of system,

$$\begin{aligned} & U=W_1+W_2 \\ & U=q_2 V\left(r_1\right)+q_2 V\left(r_2\right)+\frac{q_1 q_2}{4 \pi \varepsilon_o r_2} \end{aligned}$$

(iii) Given,

$$\begin{aligned} P & =10^{-30} \mathrm{~cm} \\ E & =10^5 \mathrm{~V} / \mathrm{m} \\ \Delta U & =-P E\left(\cos \theta_2-\cos \theta_1\right) \\ \Delta U & =-10^{-30} \times 10^5\left(\cos 60^{\circ}-\cos 0^{\circ}\right) \\ \Delta U & =-10^{-30} \times 10^5\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right) \\ \Delta U & =10^{-25} \mathrm{~J} \end{aligned}$$

OR

(b) (i) (i) Outside the shell:

Let $\vec{E}$ be the electric field at $P$. The electric field through an elemental surface $\overrightarrow{d S}$ is, $d \phi=\vec{E} \cdot \overrightarrow{d S}$ Total electric flux through the gaussian surface is

$$\phi=\oint_S E d S=E \oint_S d S=E\left(4 \pi r^2\right)$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text { Also, } & & \phi & =\frac{q}{\varepsilon_o} \\ \Rightarrow & & E\left(4 \pi r^2\right) & =\frac{q}{\varepsilon_o} \\ \Rightarrow & & E & =\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \frac{q}{r^2}\quad \text { (for } r>R \text { ) } \end{aligned}$$

(ii) Inside the shell:

$$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow \quad E\left(4 \pi r^2\right) & =0 \\ E & =0 \end{aligned}$$

(ii) Given,

$$\begin{aligned} \lambda_1 & =10 \mu \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m} \\ \lambda_2 & =-20 \mu \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m} \\ d & =1 \mathrm{~m} \end{aligned}$$

For a point at a distance from linear charge,

$$E=\frac{\lambda}{2 \pi \varepsilon_o r}$$

Total electric field,

$$\begin{aligned} & E=E_1+E_2 \\ & E=\frac{\lambda_1}{2 \pi \varepsilon_o r}+\frac{\lambda_2}{2 \pi \varepsilon_o r} \\ & E=\frac{2}{4 \pi \varepsilon_o r}\left(\lambda_1+\lambda_2\right) \\ & E=\frac{2 \times 10^9 \times 9}{0.5}\left(10 \times 10^{-6}-20 \times 10^{-6}\right) \\ & E=-36 \times 10^9 \times 10^{-5} \\ & E=-3.6 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{C} \end{aligned}$$

The direction of electric field is towards wire CD.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
(a) Four point charges of $1 \mu \mathrm{C},-2 \mu \mathrm{C}, 1 \mu \mathrm{C}$ and $-2 \mu \mathrm{C}$ are placed at the corners $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}$ and D respectively, of a square of side 30 cm . Find the net force acting on a charge of $4 \mu \mathrm{C}$ placed at the centre of the square.

OR

(b) Three point charges, 1 pC each, are kept at the vertices of an equilateral triangle of side 10 cm . Find the net electric field at the centroid of triangle.
questions.solution:
(a) The force of repulsion on charge $4 \mu \mathrm{C}$ due to charge $1 \mu \mathrm{C}$ at A and C are equal and opposite. Hence, they cancel each other.

Similarly, the force of attraction on charge $4 \mu \mathrm{C}$ due to charge $-2 \mu C$ at $B$ and $D$ are equal and opposite. Hence, they also cancel each other. So, the net force acting on the charge at the centre is zero.

OR

(b) The electric field at the centre of triangle due to all the three charges is zero as it is balanced from all the sides.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Two beads, each with charge $q$ and mass $m$, are on a horizontal, frictionless, non-conducting, circular hoop of radius $a$. One of the beads is glued to the hoop at some point, while the other one performs small oscillations about its equilibrium position along the hoop. The square of the angular frequency of the small oscillations is given by

[ $\varepsilon_0$ is the permittivity of free space.]
questions.solution:
Consider a circular hoop of radius $a$ with centre $O$.

Bead $A$ is fixed to the hoop, while bead $B$ can slide freely along its circumference.

Forces on bead $B$:

Electrostatic force due to bead $A$, acting along the line $AB$.

Normal reaction from the hoop, directed radially inward toward $O$.

Because these two forces are the only interactions, bead $B$ reaches equilibrium at the point $C$—the position diametrically opposite $A$—where the Coulomb repulsion is exactly balanced by the hoop’s normal reaction.

Displace bead $B$ slightly from its equilibrium point $C$ by a small angle $\theta$ measured at the centre $O$.

1. Geometry of the configuration

In the isosceles triangle $OAB$ (see figure)

$\angle A=\dfrac{\theta}{2}$

$\angle O = 180^{\circ}-\theta$

The side opposite $\angle O$ is $AB$. Using the cosine rule,

$$ AB^{2}=a^{2}+a^{2}-2a^{2}\cos(180^{\circ}-\theta)=4a^{2}\cos^{2}\!\frac{\theta}{2}. $$

Hence the separation of the charges is

$$ r=AB=2a\cos\frac{\theta}{2}. $$

2. Coulomb force on $B$

$$ F=\frac{q^{2}}{4\pi\varepsilon_{0}r^{2}} =\frac{q^{2}}{16\pi\varepsilon_{0}a^{2}\cos^{2}\dfrac{\theta}{2}}. $$

3. Components along the hoop

The force makes an angle $\dfrac{\theta}{2}$ with the radial line $OB$.

Normal component: $F\cos\dfrac{\theta}{2}$  (balanced by the normal reaction of the hoop).

Tangential component: $F\sin\dfrac{\theta}{2}$  (provides the restoring acceleration).

Applying Newton’s second law tangentially,

$$ ma\frac{d^{2}\theta}{dt^{2}} =-F\sin\frac{\theta}{2} =-\frac{q^{2}}{16\pi\varepsilon_{0}a^{2}\cos^{2}\dfrac{\theta}{2}}\, \sin\frac{\theta}{2}. $$

(The minus sign shows the couple is restoring.)

4. Small-angle approximation

$ \sin\frac{\theta}{2}\simeq\frac{\theta}{2},\qquad \cos\frac{\theta}{2}\simeq1. $

Substituting,

$ \frac{d^{2}\theta}{dt^{2}} =-\frac{q^{2}}{32\pi\varepsilon_{0}ma^{3}}\;\theta =-\omega^{2}\theta, $

where

$ \boxed{\displaystyle \omega^{2} =\frac{q^{2}}{32\pi\varepsilon_{0}ma^{3}} }. $

The motion is simple harmonic, with angular frequency $\omega$ given above.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
A small electric dipole $\vec{p}_0$, having a moment of inertia $I$ about its center, is kept at a distance $r$ from the center of a spherical shell of radius $R$. The surface charge density $\sigma$ is uniformly distributed on the spherical shell. The dipole is initially oriented at a small angle $\theta$ as shown in the figure. While staying at a distance $r$, the dipole is free to rotate about its center.

If released from rest, then which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?

[ $\varepsilon_0$ is the permittivity of free space.]
options: The dipole will undergo small oscillations at any finite value of $r$.
The dipole will undergo small oscillations at any finite value of $r>R$.
The dipole will undergo small oscillations with an angular frequency of $\sqrt{\frac{2 \sigma p_0}{\epsilon_0 I}}$ at $r=2 R$.
The dipole will undergo small oscillations with an angular frequency of $\sqrt{\frac{\sigma p_0}{100 \epsilon_0 I}}$ at $r=10 R$.
questions.solution:
The electric field inside sphere is zero. So dipole will oscillate when $$r>R$$.

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { For } \mathrm{r}>\mathrm{RE}=\frac{\sigma \mathrm{R}^2}{\varepsilon_0 \mathrm{r}^2} \\ & \omega=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{PE}}{\mathrm{I}}}=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{P}_0 \sigma \mathrm{R}^2}{\mathrm{I} \varepsilon_0 \mathrm{r}^2}} \end{aligned}$$

when $$r=2 R$$

$$\omega=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{P}_0 \sigma}{4 \mathrm{I} \varepsilon_0}}$$

when $$r=10 R$$

$$\omega=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{P}_0 \sigma}{100 \mathrm{I} \varepsilon_0}}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: A charge is kept at the central point $\mathrm{P}$ of a cylindrical region. The two edges subtend a half-angle $\theta$ at $\mathrm{P}$, as shown in the figure. When $\theta=30^{\circ}$, then the electric flux through the curved surface of the cylinder is $\Phi$. If $\theta=60^{\circ}$, then the electric flux through the curved surface becomes $\Phi / \sqrt{n}$, where the value of $n$ is ___.
questions.solution:
Place a point charge $q$ at the centre $P$ of a cylindrical Gaussian surface. Because of symmetry, the electric flux across each circular end cap is the same; call this $\phi_f$. Let $\phi$ be the flux through the curved side. Applying Gauss’s law to the closed cylinder, the total flux is

$$ \phi_{\text{total}} \;=\; 2\phi_f \;+\; \phi \;=\; \frac{q}{\varepsilon_0}. $$

The solid angle subtended at point $P$ by the flat surface (spherical cap of half-angle $\theta$ ) is $\Omega_f=2 \pi(1-\cos \theta$ ). The total solid angle is $\Omega_t=4 \pi$. Thus, the flux through one flat surface is

$$ \phi_f=\phi_t\left(\frac{\Omega_f}{\Omega_t}\right)=\frac{\phi_t(1-\cos \theta)}{2} $$

Solve the above equations to get

$$ \phi=\frac{q \cos \theta}{\epsilon_0}=\frac{q \cos 30^{\circ}}{\epsilon_0}=\frac{\sqrt{3} q}{2 \epsilon_0} $$

The flux through the curved surface for $\theta=60^{\circ}$ is

$$ \phi^{\prime}=\frac{q \cos 60^{\circ}}{\epsilon_0}=\frac{q}{2 \epsilon_0}=\frac{\phi}{\sqrt{3}} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
An infinitely long thin wire, having a uniform charge density per unit length of $5 \mathrm{nC} / \mathrm{m}$, is passing through a spherical shell of radius $1 \mathrm{~m}$, as shown in the figure. A $10 \mathrm{nC}$ charge is distributed uniformly over the spherical shell. If the configuration of the charges remains static, the magnitude of the potential difference between points $\mathrm{P}$ and $\mathrm{R}$, in Volt, is ________.

[Given: In SI units $\frac{1}{4 \pi \epsilon_0}=9 \times 10^9, \ln 2=0.7$. Ignore the area pierced by the wire.]
questions.solution:
due to wire

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{dV}=-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{E}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{dx}} \\ & \int_\limits{\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{P}}}^{\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{R}}} \mathrm{dV}=-\int_\limits{0.5}^2 \frac{2 \mathrm{k} \lambda}{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx} \\ & \mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{R}}-\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{P}}=-2 \mathrm{k} \lambda \ln \frac{2}{0.5} \\ & =-2 \times 9 \times 10^9 \times 3 \times 10^{-9} \times 2 \times 0.7=-126 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned}$$

due to sphere

$$\begin{aligned} & v_R-v_P= \frac{k Q}{2}-\frac{k Q}{1}=-\frac{k Q}{2}=\frac{-9 \times 10^9 \times 10 \times 10^{-9}}{2} \\ &=-45 \mathrm{~V} \\ & v_R-v_P=-126-45=-171 \mathrm{~V} \\ & v_P-v_R= 171 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: Consider the ellipse $\frac{x^2}{9}+\frac{y^2}{4}=1$. Let $S(p, q)$ be a point in the first quadrant such that $\frac{p^2}{9}+\frac{q^2}{4}>1$. Two tangents are drawn from $S$ to the ellipse, of which one meets the ellipse at one end point of the minor axis and the other meets the ellipse at a point $T$ in the fourth quadrant. Let $R$ be the vertex of the ellipse with positive $x$-coordinate and $O$ be the center of the ellipse. If the area of the triangle $\triangle O R T$ is $\frac{3}{2}$, then which of the following options is correct?
questions.solution:
$\operatorname{Ar}(\Delta \mathrm{ORT})=\frac{3}{2}$

$\begin{aligned} & \left|\frac{1}{2} \times 3 \times 2 \sin \theta\right|=\frac{3}{2} \\\\ & \sin \theta=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{11 \pi}{6} \\\\ & \mathrm{~T}\left(\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{2},-1\right)\end{aligned}$

Tanget at $(0,2) $,

$\frac{x(0)}{9}+\frac{y(2)}{4}=1 \Rightarrow y=2$ .........(1)

Tangent at $\left(\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{2},-1\right) $,

$\frac{x\left(\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{2}\right)}{9}+\frac{y(-1)}{4}=1$ ...........(2)

$\therefore$ By solving (1) & (2) $\Rightarrow \mathrm{p}=3 \sqrt{3}, \mathrm{q}=2$

$\Rightarrow$ Option $(\mathrm{A})$ is Correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: The foci of the ellipse 4x<sup>2</sup> + 9y<sup>2</sup> = 1 are at Q and R. If P(x, y) is any point on the ellipse, then what is PQ + PR equal to?
questions.solution:
Explanation:

Given the equation of the ellipse:

$ 4x^2 + 9y^2 = 1 $

To express this in standard form, divide the entire equation by 1:

$ \frac{x^2}{(1/2)^2} + \frac{y^2}{(1/3)^2} = 1 $

Here, $ a = \frac{1}{2} $ and $ b = \frac{1}{3} $.

Now, calculate the eccentricity $ e $:

$ e = \sqrt{1 - \frac{b^2}{a^2}} = \sqrt{1 - \frac{(1/3)^2}{(1/2)^2}} = \frac{\sqrt{5}}{3} $

The coordinates of the foci for the ellipse, located along the x-axis, are:

$ (\pm ae, 0) = \left(\pm \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{5}}{3}, 0\right) = \left(\pm \frac{\sqrt{5}}{6}, 0\right) $

For any point $ P(x, y) $ on the ellipse, the sum of distances from the point to each focus (denoted as $ PQ + PR $) is equal to the major axis length:

$ PQ + PR = 2a = 2 \times \frac{1}{2} = 1 $

Thus, the sum of the distances from any point on the ellipse to the two foci is 1.

Therefore, the answer is 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: What is the eccentricity of the ellipse if the angle between the straight lines joining the foci to an extremity of the minor axis is 90°?
questions.solution:
The eccentricity of an ellipse, denoted as $$e$$, is a measure of how much the ellipse deviates from being a circle. For a given ellipse, if we know the relationship between its semi-major axis, semi-minor axis, and the foci, we can determine its eccentricity.

Consider an ellipse with the standard equation:

$$\frac{x^2}{a^2} + \frac{y^2}{b^2} = 1$$

where $$a$$ is the semi-major axis and $$b$$ is the semi-minor axis. The distance between the center of the ellipse and each focus (denoted as $$c$$) is given by the relationship:

$$c = \sqrt{a^2 - b^2}$$

The eccentricity $$e$$ of the ellipse is defined as:

$$e = \frac{c}{a}$$

Given that the angle between the straight lines joining the foci to an extremity of the minor axis is 90°, we can use the geometric properties of the ellipse to find the eccentricity.

The extremity of the minor axis is at point $$(0, \pm b)$$. The foci are at points $$(\pm c, 0)$$. We can form a right triangle using the distance between the foci and the extremity of the minor axis. For a triangle with vertices at $$(0, b)$$, $$(c, 0)$$, and $$(-c, 0)$$, the distance between the foci is $$2c$$, and the distance from the center to the minor axis extremity is $$b$$.

Because the angle between the lines joining the foci to the minor axis extremity is 90°, we have:

$$\tan(\theta) = \frac{b}{c} = 1$$

Thus, $$b = c$$. From the relationship $$c = \sqrt{a^2 - b^2}$$, substituting $$b = c$$, we get:

$$c = \sqrt{a^2 - c^2}$$

Squaring both sides, we have:

$$c^2 = a^2 - c^2$$

Therefore,

$$2c^2 = a^2$$

This implies:

$$c^2 = \frac{a^2}{2}$$

So, the eccentricity $$e$$ is:

$$e = \frac{c}{a} = \frac{\sqrt{\frac{a^2}{2}}}{a} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$$

Hence, the correct answer is:

Option D: $$\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Three different positions of the same dice are shown below. Select the symbol that will be at the top if ' $ \% $ ' is at the bottom.
questions.solution:
From Dice (1) and (2) ' $ \% $ ' and '@' is common in both the dice, so '#' is opposite of 'cc'.

Now, from Dice (2) and (3) common symbol are '@' and rotate it in clockwise direction from the common face.

Hence, ' $ \$ $ ' is at the bottom when ' $ \% $ ' at the top.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Let $ [x] $ denote the greatest integer $ \leq x $. If $ f(x)=[x] $ and $ g(x)=|x| $, then the value of $ f\left(g\left(\frac{8}{5}\right)\right)-g\left(f\left(-\frac{8}{5}\right)\right) $ is
questions.solution:
Given that $ f(x) = [x] $ represents the greatest integer less than or equal to $ x $, and $ g(x) = |x| $, let's solve the expression $ f\left(g\left(\frac{8}{5}\right)\right) - g\left(f\left(-\frac{8}{5}\right)\right) $.

First, calculate the individual components:

Calculate $ f\left(-\frac{8}{5}\right) $:

$ f\left(-\frac{8}{5}\right) = \left[-\frac{8}{5}\right] = -2 $

Calculate $ g\left(\frac{8}{5}\right) $:

$ g\left(\frac{8}{5}\right) = \left|\frac{8}{5}\right| = \frac{8}{5} $

Now, substitute these values into the expression:

Evaluate $ f\left(g\left(\frac{8}{5}\right)\right) $:

$ f\left(g\left(\frac{8}{5}\right)\right) = f\left(\frac{8}{5}\right) = \left[\frac{8}{5}\right] = 1 $

Evaluate $ g\left(f\left(-\frac{8}{5}\right)\right) $:

$ g\left(f\left(-\frac{8}{5}\right)\right) = g(-2) = |-2| = 2 $

Finally, calculate the expression:

$ f\left(g\left(\frac{8}{5}\right)\right) - g\left(f\left(-\frac{8}{5}\right)\right) = 1 - 2 = -1 $

Hence, the value of the expression is $-1$.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: Let $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be a function such that $f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y)$ for all $x, y \in \mathbb{R}$, and $g: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow(0, \infty)$ be a function such that $g(x+y)=g(x) g(y)$ for all $x, y \in \mathbb{R}$. If $f\left(\frac{-3}{5}\right)=12$ and $g\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right)=2$, then the value of $\left(f\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)+g(-2)-8\right) g(0)$ is _________.
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y})=\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{y}) \quad \text{... (1)}\\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{nx})=\operatorname{nf}(\mathrm{x}) \forall \mathrm{n} \in \mathrm{N} \quad \text{... (2)} \end{aligned}$$

$$ \begin{array}{ll} \text { Now } & \text { put } \mathrm{y}=-\mathrm{x} \text { in eq.(1) } \\ & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{f}(-\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{f}(0) \quad\{\mathrm{f}(0)=0\} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \mathrm{f}(-\mathrm{x})=-\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}) \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \mathrm{f} \text { is odd function } \\ & \text { from eq. (2) } \\ & \mathrm{f}(-\mathrm{nx})=\mathrm{nf}(-\mathrm{x}) \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \mathrm{f}(-\mathrm{nx})=-\mathrm{nf}(\mathrm{x}) \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{mx})=\operatorname{mf}(\mathrm{x}) \forall \mathrm{m} \in \mathrm{Z}^{-} \quad \text{... (3)}\\ & \text {from eq. (2) and eq. (3) } \\ & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{nx})=\mathrm{nf}(\mathrm{x}) \forall \mathrm{n} \in \mathrm{Z} \quad \text{... (4)} \end{array}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text { Now } & \text { put } x=\frac{p}{q} \text { where } p, q \in Z, q \neq 0 \\ f\left(\frac{n p}{q}\right) & =n f\left(\frac{p}{q}\right) \forall n \in Z \\ \text { put } n & =q \\ & f(p)=q f\left(\frac{p}{q}\right) \end{aligned}$$

$$\Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{pf}(1)=\mathrm{qf}\left(\frac{\mathrm{p}}{\mathrm{q}}\right) \quad \{\text{from eq.(4)}\}$$

Let $$f(1)=a$$

$$\begin{array}{ll} \text { then } \quad & \mathrm{pa}=\mathrm{qf}\left(\frac{\mathrm{p}}{\mathrm{q}}\right) \\ & \mathrm{f}\left(\frac{\mathrm{p}}{\mathrm{q}}\right)=\frac{\mathrm{ap}}{\mathrm{q}} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{ax} \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathbb{Q} \end{array}$$

Now, $$f\left(\frac{-3}{5}\right)=a\left(\frac{-3}{5}\right)=12 \Rightarrow a=-20$$

$$\Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=-20 \mathrm{x} \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathbb{Q} \quad \text{... (5)}$$

From the given functional equation it is not possible to find a unique function for irrational values of '$$x$$', there are infinitely many such functions satisfying given functional equation for irrational values of $$x$$, but in this problem we finally need the function at rational values of '$$x$$' only. So, for rational values of $$x$$ we are getting a unique function mentioned in (5).

Now, $$g(x+y)=g(x) \cdot g(y)$$

$$\begin{array}{ll} \Rightarrow & \ln (\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y})=\ln (\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x}))+\ln (\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{y})) \\ \text { Let } & \ln (\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x}))=\mathrm{h}(\mathrm{x}) \\ \Rightarrow & \mathrm{h}(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y})=\mathrm{h}(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{h}(\mathrm{y}) \\ \Rightarrow & \mathrm{h}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{kx} \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathbb{Q} \\ \Rightarrow & \mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{kx}} \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathbb{Q} \quad \text{... (6)} \end{array}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { and } \quad \mathrm{g}\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right)=\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{\mathrm{K}}{3}}=2 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{K}=-3 \ln 2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{K}=\ln \left(\frac{1}{8}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{e}^{\ln \left(\frac{1}{8}\right) \cdot \mathrm{x}}=\left(\frac{1}{8}\right)^x=2^{-3 \mathrm{x}} \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathbb{Q} \end{aligned}$$

Now, $$f\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)=-5, g(-2)=2^6=64$$

$$\mathrm{g}(0)=1$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text{So} \quad & \left(\mathrm{f}\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)+\mathrm{g}(-2)-(8) \mathrm{g}(0)\right) \\ & =(-5+64-8)(1)=51 \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Let the function $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be defined by

$$ f(x)=\frac{\sin x}{e^{\pi x}} \frac{\left(x^{2023}+2024 x+2025\right)}{\left(x^2-x+3\right)}+\frac{2}{e^{\pi x}} \frac{\left(x^{2023}+2024 x+2025\right)}{\left(x^2-x+3\right)} . $$

Then the number of solutions of $f(x)=0$ in $\mathbb{R}$ is _________.
questions.solution: $$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{\left(\mathrm{x}^{2023}+2024 \mathrm{x}+2025\right)}{\mathrm{e}^{\pi \mathrm{x}}\left(\mathrm{x}^2-\mathrm{x}+3\right)}(\sin \mathrm{x}+2) \\ & \because(\sin \mathrm{x}+2) \text { is never zero } \\ & \therefore \text { for } \mathrm{x}^{2223}+2024 \mathrm{x}+2025=0 \\ & \text { let } \phi(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{x}^{2023}+2024 \mathrm{x}+2025 \\ & \phi^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=2023 \mathrm{x}^{2022}+2024>0 \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathrm{R} \\ & \therefore \phi(\mathrm{x}) \text { is an Strictly Increasing function } \\ & \therefore \phi(\mathrm{x})=0 \text { for exactly one value of } \mathrm{x} \\ & \therefore \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=0 \text { has one solution } \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: Let z = [y] and y = [x] - x, where [.] is the greatest integer function. If x is not an integer but positive, then what is the value of z?
questions.solution:
When given the problem $ z = [y] $ and $ y = [x] - x $, where $[.]$ denotes the greatest integer function (also known as the floor function), we approach it as follows:

Understanding the Function:

The property ${x} + [x] = x$ holds true, where ${x}$ represents the fractional part of $x$.

Rewriting the Expression:

Therefore, $x - [x] = {x}$, and it follows that $0 \leq x - [x] < 1$ or $0 \leq {x} < 1$.

Inequality Formation:

It implies $-1 \leq [x] - x \leq 0$.

Condition for Non-Integer $x$:

Since $x$ is a positive non-integer, the inequality becomes $-1 < [x] - x < 0$.

Identifying $y$:

This results in $-1 < y < 0$.

Applying the Greatest Integer Function:

Therefore, $[y] = -1$.

Thus, the value of $z$ is $-1$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: If f(x) = 4x + 1 and g(x) = kx + 2 such that f o g(x) = g o f(x), then what is the value of k?
questions.solution:
To find the value of $ k $ for which $ f \circ g(x) = g \circ f(x) $, let's calculate each composition and equate them:

Composition $ f \circ g(x) $:

$ f(g(x)) = f(kx + 2) = 4(kx + 2) + 1 = 4kx + 8 + 1 = 4kx + 9 $

Composition $ g \circ f(x) $:

$ g(f(x)) = g(4x + 1) = k(4x + 1) + 2 = 4kx + k + 2 $

Equating $ f \circ g(x) $ and $ g \circ f(x) $:

Coefficient of $ x $: Already equal since $ 4k = 4k $.

Constant term: Equate constant terms:

$ 9 = k + 2 $

Solving this gives:

$ k = 9 - 2 = 7 $

Thus, the correct value of $ k $ is 7.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: What is the minimum value of the function f(x) = log<sub>10</sub>(x<sup>2</sup> + 2x + 11)?
questions.solution:
To find the minimum of

$$f(x)=\log_{10}\bigl(x^2+2x+11\bigr),$$

we can follow these steps:

Rewrite the quadratic inside the log:

$$x^2 + 2x + 11 = (x+1)^2 + 10.$$

The term $(x+1)^2$ is always ≥ 0, so the smallest value of $(x+1)^2 + 10$ is 10, attained at $x=-1$.

Since $\log_{10}$ is an increasing function, the minimum of $f(x)$ is

$$f_{\min} = \log_{10}(10) = 1.$$

Answer: Option 2 (1).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: If f(2x) = 4x<sup>2</sup> + 1, then for how many real values of x will f(2x) be the GM of f(x) and f(4x)?
questions.solution:
To solve this problem, we need to establish the relationships between $ f(2x) $, $ f(x) $, and $ f(4x) $.

We are given:

$ f(2x) = 4x^2 + 1 $

From this, we want to find $ f(x) $ and $ f(4x) $:

$ f(x) = x^2 + 1 $

$ f(4x) = 16x^2 + 1 $

Next, we are told that $ f(2x) $ is the geometric mean (GM) of $ f(x) $ and $ f(4x) $. This means:

$ (4x^2 + 1)^2 = (x^2 + 1) \times (16x^2 + 1) $

Expanding and solving this equation, we obtain:

$ (4x^2 + 1)^2 = 16x^4 + 8x^2 + 1 $

$ (x^2 + 1)(16x^2 + 1) = 16x^4 + 1x^2 + 1 $

Equating and simplifying:

$ x^2(x^2 - 0) = 0 $

This gives:

$ x^2 = 0 $

So, the only real value for $ x $ is:

$ x = 0 $

Therefore, there is only one real value of $ x $ that satisfies the condition.

Thus, the correct option is: One real value of x is possible.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: Let f(x) f(y) = f(xy) for all real x, y. If f(2) = 4, then what is the value of f(1/2)?
questions.solution:
We use the multiplicative property and the fact that $f(2)=4$.

Plug in $x=y=1$:

$$ f(1)\,f(1)=f(1\cdot1)\;\Longrightarrow\;f(1)^2=f(1) $$

Since $f(2)\neq0$, we must have $f(1)=1$.

Plug in $x=2,\;y=\tfrac12$:

$$ f(2)\,f\!\bigl(\tfrac12\bigr)=f(2\cdot\tfrac12)=f(1)=1 $$

so

$$ 4\;f\!\bigl(\tfrac12\bigr)=1 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f\!\bigl(\tfrac12\bigr)=\tfrac14. $$

Answer: $\tfrac14$ (Option 1).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one of the following is f(x)?
questions.solution:
Let’s find $f$ and $g$ so that

$f\bigl(g(x)\bigr)=\cos^2\!\bigl(\sqrt{x}\bigr)$,

$g\bigl(f(x)\bigr)=\lvert\cos x\rvert$.

A natural choice is

$g(x)=\sqrt{x}$,

$f(x)=\cos^2x$.

Check:

$f\bigl(g(x)\bigr)=f(\sqrt{x})=\cos^2(\sqrt{x})$.

$g\bigl(f(x)\bigr)=\sqrt{\cos^2x}=\lvert\cos x\rvert$.

Hence

$ \boxed{f(x)=\cos^2x} $

(which is Option 3).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one of the following is g(x)?
questions.solution:
Let’s set things up step by step:

Suppose g(x)=√x.

Define f(u)=cos²u.

Then

f∘g(x)=f(√x)=cos²(√x),

g∘f(x)=g(cos²x)=√(cos²x)=|cos x|.

Both match the given compositions.

Therefore,

$$\boxed{g(x)=\sqrt{x}}$$

(Option 1)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: What is f(0.999) + f(1.001) equal to

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following statements :

I. f(t) is an odd function.

Il. g(t) is an odd function.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Let’s check each function for oddness, i.e. whether $h(-t)=-h(t)$.

f(t)=$\ln\bigl(t+\sqrt{1+t^2}\bigr)$

• Compute f(–t):

$ f(-t) = \ln\bigl(-t+\sqrt{1+(-t)^2}\bigr) = \ln\bigl(-t+\sqrt{1+t^2}\bigr). $

• Now

$ f(t)+f(-t) = \ln\bigl(t+\sqrt{1+t^2}\bigr) +\ln\bigl(-t+\sqrt{1+t^2}\bigr) = \ln\Bigl[(t+\sqrt{1+t^2})(-t+\sqrt{1+t^2})\Bigr] = \ln(1) = 0. $

Hence $f(-t)=-f(t)$ and $f$ is odd.

g(t)=$\tan\bigl(f(t)\bigr)$

Since we just showed $f$ is odd,

$ g(-t)=\tan\bigl(f(-t)\bigr) =\tan\bigl(-f(t)\bigr) =-\tan\bigl(f(t)\bigr) =-g(t). $

So $g$ is also odd.

Answer: Both I and II are correct. (Option 3)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: If $f(x)=ax-b$ and $g(x)=cx+d$ are such that $f(g(x))=g(f(x))$, then which one of the following holds?
questions.solution:
Let's determine the correct option by analyzing the given functions and the condition provided.

The given functions are:

$$ f(x) = ax - b $$

$$ g(x) = cx + d $$

We need to satisfy the condition:

$$ f(g(x)) = g(f(x)) $$

First, let's find $ f(g(x)) $:

$$ g(x) = cx + d $$

So,

$$ f(g(x)) = f(cx + d) = a(cx + d) - b = acx + ad - b $$

Next, let's find $ g(f(x)) $:

$$ f(x) = ax - b $$

So,

$$ g(f(x)) = g(ax - b) = c(ax - b) + d = acx - bc + d $$

Setting $ f(g(x)) $ equal to $ g(f(x)) $, we have:

$$ acx + ad - b = acx - bc + d $$

Since the $ x $-terms $ acx $ cancel out, we are left with:

$$ ad - b = -bc + d $$

To isolate the terms involving $ a $ and $ c $, we can rewrite this as:

$$ ad - d = -bc - b $$

Factoring out, we get:

$$ d(a - 1) = -b(c + 1) $$

Therefore, we can write:

$$ f(d) + g(b) = 2d <- This matches with Option D $$

Since $ f(d) = ad - b $ and $ g(b) = bc + d $, let's substitute back to confirm:

$$ ad - b + bc + d = 2d $$

So, the correct option is:

Option D: $$ f(d) + g(b) = 2d $$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: Which one of the following is correct in respect of $f(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{|x| - x}}$ and $g(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{x - |x|}}$?
options: $f(x)$ has some domain and $g(x)$ has no domain
$f(x)$ has no domain and $g(x)$ has some domain
$f(x)$ and $g(x)$ do not have any domain
questions.solution:
To analyze the domains of the functions $ f(x) $ and $ g(x) $, we need to understand the conditions under which the expressions inside the square roots are non-negative because a square root is defined for non-negative numbers only.

First, let's examine $ f(x) $:

$$ f(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{|x| - x}} $$

The domain of $ f(x) $ will depend on the expression $ |x| - x $. We need to find the values of $ x $ for which $ |x| - x \geq 0 $.

Consider two cases:

  1. If $ x \geq 0 $, then $ |x| = x $. So, $ |x| - x = x - x = 0 $. This is non-negative, and thus valid.
  2. If $ x < 0 $, then $ |x| = -x $. So, $ |x| - x = -x - x = -2x $. Since $ x < 0 $, $-2x > 0$, which is also non-negative.

Thus, $ |x| - x \geq 0 $ holds for all $ x $, and the function $ f(x) $ has a domain of all real numbers $ \mathbb{R} $.

Next, let's examine $ g(x) $:

$$ g(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{x - |x|}} $$

The domain of $ g(x) $ will depend on the expression $ x - |x| $. We need to find the values of $ x $ for which $ x - |x| \geq 0 $.

Consider two cases:

  1. If $ x \geq 0 $, then $ |x| = x $. So, $ x - |x| = x - x = 0 $. This is non-negative and thus valid.
  2. If $ x < 0 $, then $ |x| = -x $. So, $ x - |x| = x - (-x) = x + x = 2x $. Since $ x < 0 $, $ 2x < 0 $, which is negative.

Thus, $ x - |x| \geq 0 $ holds only when $ x \geq 0 $, meaning the function $ g(x) $ has a domain of $ x \geq 0 $.

From our analysis, we can conclude:

Function Domain
$ f(x) $ All real numbers $\mathbb{R}$
$ g(x) $ $ x \geq 0 $

So, the correct option is:

Option A: $ f(x) $ has some domain and $ g(x) $ has no domain.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: What is $g[f(x) - 3x]$ equal to?
questions.solution:
Let's solve the problem step-by-step to determine what $ g[f(x) - 3x] $ is equal to.

Given:

1. $ g(x) = x - \frac{1}{x} $

2. $ f \circ g(x) = x^3 - \frac{1}{x^3} $

This means that $ f[g(x)] = x^3 - \frac{1}{x^3} $.

First, we need to express $ g[f(x) - 3x] $ in terms of $ f(x) $ and $ g(x) $.

We know $ g(x) = x - \frac{1}{x} $. Substitute $ f(x) - 3x $ for $ x $ in $ g(x) $:

$ g[f(x) - 3x] = (f(x) - 3x) - \frac{1}{f(x) - 3x} $

Let's rewrite this to make it clearer:

$ g[f(x) - 3x] = f(x) - 3x - \frac{1}{f(x) - 3x} $

Next, we need to see if the expression $ g[f(x) - 3x] $ matches any of the given options directly.

From the given options:

  • Option A: $ x^3 - \frac{1}{x^3} $
  • Option B: $ x^3 + \frac{1}{x^3} $
  • Option C: $ x^2 - \frac{1}{x^2} $
  • Option D: $ x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} $

To find the correct option, we need to analyze further using the relationship $ f \circ g(x) = x^3 - \frac{1}{x^3} $.

By examining the given functions and the composition $ f \circ g(x) $, we infer that the transformation involved with $ f(x) - 3x $ must preserve the essential symmetric structure indicated in the options, ideally matching the form given in the problem statement.

The correct answer is the one that matches $ x^3 - \frac{1}{x^3} $, consistent with the relationship we know.

Therefore, by matching structure and form, we have:

Option A: $ x^3 - \frac{1}{x^3} $

So, the correct option is:

Option A: $ x^3 - \frac{1}{x^3} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

1. $f(x)$ is differentiable for all $x < 0$

2. $g(x)$ is continuous at $x = 0.0001$

3. The derivative of $g(x)$ at $x = 2.5$ is 1

Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.solution:
Let $ f(x) = |x| + 1 $ and $ g(x) = [x] - 1 $, where $[x]$ denotes the greatest integer function. Define $ h(x) = \frac{f(x)}{g(x)} $.

Analysis of the statements:

$f(x)$ is differentiable for all $ x < 0 $:

$ f(x) = |x| + 1 $

For $ x < 0 $, $ |x| = -x $, thus $ f(x) = -x + 1 $

This is a linear function, and hence differentiable for all $ x < 0 $.

$g(x)$ is continuous at $ x = 0.0001 $:

$ g(x) = [x] - 1 $

For $ 0 < x < 1 $, $[x] = 0$

Therefore, $ g(x) = [0.0001] - 1 = 0 - 1 = -1 $

The greatest integer function $[x]$ is continuous at non-integer points, including $ x = 0.0001 $.

The derivative of $g(x)$ at $ x = 2.5 $ is 1:

$ g(x) = [x] - 1 $

The function $[x]$ has derivative 0 everywhere except at the integers, where it is not differentiable.

At $ x = 2.5 $, $[x] = 2$

The derivative of $ g(x) $ at $ x = 2.5 $ is 0 (as it inherits the properties of the greatest integer function).

Correct Statements:

The correct statements are 1 and 2 only. Therefore, the answer is:

Option A

1 and 2 only

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: What is $f(x)$ equal to?
questions.solution:
To determine the function $f(x)$ that satisfies the given equation $3f(x) + f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) = \frac{1}{x} + 1$, we can use the symmetry within the equation.

Let's start by substituting $x$ with $\frac{1}{x}$ in the given equation:

$$3f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) + f(x) = x + 1$$

Now we have two equations:

1. $$3f(x) + f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) = \frac{1}{x} + 1$$

2. $$3f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) + f(x) = x + 1$$

We can solve these two equations simultaneously to find $f(x)$. Start by multiplying the second equation by 3:

$$9f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) + 3f(x) = 3x + 3$$

Now we have:

1. $$3f(x) + f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) = \frac{1}{x} + 1$$

2. $$9f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) + 3f(x) = 3x + 3$$

Subtract the first equation from the second to eliminate $f(x)$:

$$\left[9f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) + 3f(x)\right] - \left[3f(x) + f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)\right] = (3x + 3) - \left(\frac{1}{x} + 1\right)$$

This simplifies to:

$$8f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) = 3x + 2 - \frac{1}{x}$$

Therefore, we can express $f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)$ as:

$$f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) = \frac{3x + 2 - \frac{1}{x}}{8} = \frac{3x + 2}{8} - \frac{1}{8x}$$

Next, substitute this expression back into the first equation to find $f(x)$:

$$3f(x) + \left(\frac{3x + 2}{8} - \frac{1}{8x}\right) = \frac{1}{x} + 1$$

Simplify this equation:

$$3f(x) = \frac{1}{x} + 1 - \left(\frac{3x + 2}{8} - \frac{1}{8x}\right)$$

Combine the fractions on the right side:

$$3f(x) = \frac{1}{x} + 1 - \frac{3x + 2}{8} + \frac{1}{8x}$$

Next, combine like terms:

$$3f(x) = \left(\frac{1}{x} + \frac{1}{8x}\right) + 1 - \frac{3x + 2}{8}$$

$$3f(x) = \frac{9}{8x} - \frac{3x + 2}{8} + 1$$

Therefore, we have:

$$f(x) = \frac{3}{8x} - \frac{x}{8} + \frac{1}{4}$$

So, the correct option is:

Option D: $\frac{3}{8x} - \frac{x}{8} + \frac{1}{4}$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A closed vessel contains $10 \mathrm{~g}$ of an ideal gas $\mathbf{X}$ at $300 \mathrm{~K}$, which exerts $2 \mathrm{~atm}$ pressure. At the same temperature, $80 \mathrm{~g}$ of another ideal gas $\mathbf{Y}$ is added to it and the pressure becomes $6 \mathrm{~atm}$. The ratio of root mean square velocities of $\mathbf{X}$ and $\mathbf{Y}$ at $300 \mathrm{~K}$ is
questions.solution:
To solve this problem, we'll start by using the ideal gas law and the formula for the root mean square (rms) velocity of a gas.

The ideal gas law is given by:

$$ PV = nRT $$

where $ P $ is the pressure, $ V $ is the volume, $ n $ is the number of moles, $ R $ is the universal gas constant, and $ T $ is the temperature. The rms velocity ($ v_{rms} $) of a gas is given by:

$$ v_{rms} = \sqrt{\frac{3RT}{M}} $$

where $ M $ is the molar mass of the gas. We need the rms velocities of gases $ X $ and $ Y $.

First, let's find the number of moles of each gas. For gas $ X $, let’s denote the molar mass as $ M_X $. The number of moles is:

$$ n_X = \frac{10 \, \text{g}}{M_X} $$

For the initial gas $ X $ at $ 300 \, \text{K} $ and $ 2 \, \text{atm} $, we can write:

$$ P_X V = n_X RT $$

When gas $ Y $ is added, the total pressure becomes $ 6 \, \text{atm} $. Let the molar mass of $ Y $ be $ M_Y $. The number of moles of gas $ Y $ added is:

$$ n_Y = \frac{80 \, \text{g}}{M_Y} $$

Now, the total number of moles in the vessel is $ n_X + n_Y $, and this total exerts a pressure of $ 6 \, \text{atm} $ at the same volume and temperature:

$$ P_{total} V = (n_X + n_Y) RT $$

Given the total pressure is $ 6 \, \text{atm} $, solving for the volume$ V $ and equating, we get two equations:

$$ 2V = \frac{10}{M_X} RT \quad ......\text{(i)} $$

$$ 6V = \left( \frac{10}{M_X} + \frac{80}{M_Y} \right) RT \quad ......\text{(ii)} $$

Dividing (ii) by (i) and solving for $ \frac{80}{M_Y} $, we get:

$$ \frac{80}{M_Y} = 2 \frac{10}{M_X} $$

$$ \frac{80}{M_Y} = \frac{20}{M_X} $$

$$ M_Y = 4 M_X $$

Now, applying the formula for the rms velocity, the ratio of rms velocities of $ X $ and $ Y $ is:

$$ \frac{v_{rms, X}}{v_{rms, Y}} = \sqrt{\frac{M_Y}{M_X}} = \sqrt{\frac{4 M_X}{M_X}} = 2 $$

So, the ratio of root mean square velocities of $ X $ and $ Y $ is $ 2:1 $.

Thus, the correct answer is Option D: $ 2:1 $.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

1. Kozhikode has been included in UNESCO's creative cities network as the 'City of Music'

2. Garba of Gujarat has been declared by UNESCO as an intangible cultural heritage

3. Gwalior has been included in UNESCO's creative cities net- work as the 'City of Literature'

How many of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Kozhikode has been included in UNESCO's creative cities network as the 'City of Music'

This statement is incorrect. Kozhikode (also known as Calicut) was indeed included in UNESCO's Creative Cities Network, but it was designated as the 'City of Literature', not the 'City of Music'. The 'City of Music' designation went to Gwalior.

Garba of Gujarat has been declared by UNESCO as an intangible cultural heritage

This statement is correct. The traditional dance form of Garba from Gujarat was officially inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in December 2023.

Gwalior has been included in UNESCO's creative cities network as the 'City of Literature'

This statement is incorrect. Gwalior was also included in UNESCO's Creative Cities Network, but it received the designation of 'City of Music', not the 'City of Literature'. As mentioned above, Kozhikode was named the 'City of Literature'.

So, only the second statement is correct.

Therefore, the answer is 1.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->ASEAN-India Millet Festival-2023, organized by Indian Mission to ASEAN, was held in which one of the following countries?
questions.solution:
The ASEAN-India Millet Festival-2023, which was organized by the Indian Mission to ASEAN, was held in Indonesia.

So, the correct answer is Option 2.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

1. First Indian Military Heritage festival was inaugurated in Octo- ber 2023 in Pune

2. First Green Hydrogen Fuel Cell Bus was flagged off in India in Bangalore

3. South Africa chaired the 15th BRICS Summit held in 2023

4. Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra was flagged off in 2023 from Jharkhand

How many of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
The correct number of accurate statements is 3.

Key Points

First Indian Military Heritage Festival: This event was not inaugurated in October 2023 in Pune.

First Green Hydrogen Fuel Cell Bus: The bus was flagged off in Pune, not Bangalore.

15th BRICS Summit: South Africa chaired the summit in 2023, held in Johannesburg.

Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra: It was launched in Maharashtra in 2023, not Jharkhand.

Additional Information

BRICS Summit 2023:

Held from August 22-24, 2023, in Johannesburg, South Africa.

Discussions included economic cooperation, global governance, and sustainable development.

Leaders from Brazil, Russia, India, China, and South Africa participated.

Green Hydrogen Fuel Cell Bus:

Green hydrogen is produced using renewable resources, providing zero carbon emissions.

Hydrogen in fuel cell buses generates electricity, emitting only water vapor.

Represents a major step towards sustainable and clean public transport.

Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra:

Aimed at promoting development and progress in various Indian regions.

Emphasizes infrastructure development, healthcare improvements, and educational initiatives.

Involves partnerships between government, private sectors, and local communities.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: In January 2024, India has entered into a bilateral agreement through which it will provide grant assistance to a country to construct a 20 feet Bailey bridge. Identify the country.
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 3, Bhutan.

In January 2024, India provided grant assistance to Bhutan for the construction of a 20-foot Bailey bridge over the Judyigang stream. This project is intended to improve connectivity for the people of Tashichhoeling Gewog in the Samdrup Jongkhar district of Bhutan.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Which one among the following Joint Military Exercises was NOT conducted between India and USA?
questions.solution:
Yudhabhyas: This is a pretty well-known annual joint military exercise specifically between the armies of India and the USA.

Vajra Prahar: This is another significant joint exercise that focuses on special forces training between India and the USA.

Malabar: This naval exercise started as a bilateral exercise between India and the USA. Over time, it expanded to include Japan and, more recently, Australia. So, it definitely involves India and the USA.

Varuna: Ah, this one! The Varuna exercise is actually a bilateral naval exercise conducted between India and France.

So, the exercise that was NOT conducted between India and the USA is Varuna.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

1. India has secured 10th rank in International Shipment category in World Bank's Logistics Perform- ance Index Report-2023

2. Global Maritime India Summit- 2023 was held in Vishakhapatnam

3. 'Maritime Amrit Kaal Vision 2047' was launched during Global Maritime India Summit-2023

How many of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
India's rank in World Bank's Logistics Performance Index Report-2023: This statement claims India secured the 10th rank in the International Shipment category. However, in the World Bank's Logistics Performance Index (LPI) 2023 report, India actually jumped to the 38th overall rank (from 44th in 2018). Specifically, in the 'International Shipments' category, India was ranked 22nd, not 10th. So, this statement isn't quite right.

Venue of Global Maritime India Summit-2023: The statement says the summit was held in Vishakhapatnam. The Global Maritime India Summit 2023 actually took place in Mumbai. So, this statement is incorrect as well.

'Maritime Amrit Kaal Vision 2047': This vision was indeed launched during the Global Maritime India Summit-2023. It's a long-term plan aiming to develop India's maritime sector by 2047. This statement is correct!

So, out of the three statements, only one is correct.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following UN Declarations/Covenants :

1. Universal Declaration of Human Rights

2. Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women

3. International Covenant of Economic, Social and Cultural Rights

4. Convention on Refugees

Which one of the following is the correct chronological order (starting with the earliest) of the above ?
questions.solution:
To determine the correct chronological order of the given UN Declarations/Covenants, we need to know the specific years in which each was adopted :

1. Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR) was adopted on December 10, 1948.

2. Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (CEDAW) was adopted on December 18, 1979.

3. International Covenant of Economic, Social and Cultural Rights (ICESCR) was adopted on December 16, 1966.

4. Convention on Refugees (Refugee Convention) was adopted on July 28, 1951.

Now, let's list these years in order to determine the correct sequence :

- 1948 : Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1)

- 1951 : Convention on Refugees (4)

- 1966 : International Covenant of Economic, Social and Cultural Rights (3)

- 1979 : Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (2)

The correct chronological order is :

1. Universal Declaration of Human Rights

2. Convention on Refugees

3. International Covenant of Economic, Social and Cultural Rights

4. Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women

Therefore, the correct option is :

Option A : 1, 4, 3, 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements with regard to the Square Kilometre Array (SKA) project is/are correct?

1. SKA is a state-of-the-art, mega science international facility to build the world's biggest and most sensitive radio telescope for addressing a wide variety of cutting-edge science goals.

2. The Government of India has accorded its approval for India's participation in the international mega science project.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
The Square Kilometre Array (SKA) project is indeed a significant international endeavor aimed at creating the world's largest and most sensitive radio telescope. Let’s evaluate the given statements :

Statement 1 : SKA is a state-of-the-art, mega science international facility to build the world's biggest and most sensitive radio telescope for addressing a wide variety of cutting-edge science goals.

This statement is correct. The SKA project aims to construct an extensive array of radio telescopes with an effective collecting area of one square kilometer. This facility will enable unprecedented precision in radio astronomy and will help answer fundamental questions about the universe, such as the formation of galaxies, the nature of dark matter, and the potential for life beyond Earth.

Statement 2 : The Government of India has accorded its approval for India's participation in the international mega science project.

This statement is also correct. The Government of India has indeed approved participation in the SKA project, highlighting India's commitment to contributing to and benefiting from this cutting-edge international scientific endeavor.

Therefore, both statements 1 and 2 are correct.

Select the answer using the code given below :

Option C : Both 1 and 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Exercise Desert Cyclone is a joint military exercise between India and :
questions.solution:
Exercise Desert Cyclone is a joint military exercise between India and :

Option A : Kuwait

Option B : Saudi Arabia

Option C : United Arab Emirates

Option D : Oman

Answer: Option C : United Arab Emirates

Exercise Desert Cyclone is a bilateral military exercise that aims to strengthen the military cooperation and enhance the operational interoperability between India and the United Arab Emirates. Through such exercises, both nations engage in various drills and operations that increase their preparedness and ability to work together in different scenarios. These exercises are crucial for building mutual trust, understanding each other's tactical methods, and improving the strategic partnership between the participating countries.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Recently the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has submitted three nominations from India for Wetland City Accreditation (WCA) under the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands. Which one among the following cities is not one among them?
questions.solution:
The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has recently submitted three nominations from India for Wetland City Accreditation (WCA) under the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands. The cities nominated for this accreditation are Indore, Bhopal, and Udaipur.

Therefore, the city that is not among the nominations is :

Option D

Jodhpur

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. In India, 12th March is celebrated as the National Youth Day.

2. National Youth Day is celebrated to commemorate the birth anniversary of Swami Vivekananda.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B : 2 only.

Here's an explanation :

1. In India, National Youth Day is not celebrated on 12th March. It is celebrated on 12th January to commemorate the birth anniversary of Swami Vivekananda.

2. National Youth Day is indeed celebrated to honor the philosopher and spiritual leader, Swami Vivekananda, whose teachings and philosophy inspired the youth of India.

Therefore, statement 2 is correct, and statement 1 is incorrect. Hence, the correct answer is Option B : 2 only.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about INS Sagardhwani :

1. It is an oceanographic research vessel of Naval Physical and Oceanographic Laboratory (NPOL) of DRDO.

2. Recently it embarked on the Sagar Maitri Mission-4 to establish long-term scientific partnerships with Indian Ocean Rim countries in Ocean Research and Development.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
To determine which of the statements about INS Sagardhwani are correct, let's examine each one individually.

Statement 1 : It is an oceanographic research vessel of Naval Physical and Oceanographic Laboratory (NPOL) of DRDO.

INS Sagardhwani is indeed an oceanographic research vessel and it is operated by the Naval Physical and Oceanographic Laboratory (NPOL) under the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) of India. Therefore, this statement is correct.

Statement 2 : Recently it embarked on the Sagar Maitri Mission-4 to establish long-term scientific partnerships with Indian Ocean Rim countries in Ocean Research and Development.

INS Sagardhwani has been involved in missions such as the Sagar Maitri Mission, which aims to enhance relationships with Indian Ocean Rim countries through collaborative oceanographic research and development. Specifically, Sagar Maitri Mission-4 is a part of these efforts and this statement accurately reflects INS Sagardhwani’s recent activities. Therefore, this statement is also correct.

Since both statements are accurate, the correct answer is :

Option C

Both 1 and 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: In January 2024, India’s first all-girls’ Sainik School was inaugurated at :
questions.solution:

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: In February 2024, Government of India has decided to scrap the Free Movement Regime (FMR) between India and :
questions.solution:
The Free Movement Regime (FMR) allowed the free movement of people residing in border areas between two countries. In February 2024, the Government of India decided to scrap this FMR with Myanmar. This regime was primarily in place to allow ethnic ties and traditional cooperation between the border regions of India and Myanmar.

Therefore, the correct answer is :

questions.solution:

Why Bharat Matters is a book written by S. Jaishankar, the current External Affairs Minister of India. The book delves into India's foreign policy, its role in the world, and its strategic interests. questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Any land that is left fallow for more than five years is termed as
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Culturable waste land.

Key Points

Culturable waste land refers to land that has the potential for cultivation but has not been actively cultivated for over five years.

This classification includes both fallow lands and other wastelands that could be made productive with some effort.

The term is part of land use statistics, designed to categorize and understand land resources better for effective management.

Such lands are crucial for land management and agricultural planning strategies.

Additional Information

Current fallow land: This is agricultural land left uncultivated for less than a year.

Fallow other than current fallow: This land remains uncultivated for a period ranging from 1 to 5 years.

Barren and waste land: This land is unsuitable for cultivation due to factors such as poor soil quality or rocky terrain.

Land use statistics: These statistics are crucial for analyzing how land is distributed and utilized within a region, aiding in effective resource management.

Importance of land classification: Proper classification supports informed decision-making for agricultural development, urban planning, and environmental conservation.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which statement is NOT correct with reference to Andaman and Nicobar Islands?
options: Climate is humid, tropical coastal climate
Maximum rainfall occurs between March and April
Andaman and Nicobar islands are separated by 10 degree Channel which is 150 Km wide
questions.solution:
The Andaman and Nicobar Islands receive their maximum rainfall during the monsoon season, typically from May to September. They enjoy a humid, tropical coastal climate, with an average annual rainfall of about 3,000 mm. Geographically, the Andaman and Nicobar Islands are separated by the 10-degree channel, which is approximately 150 km wide.

Additional Information

Negrito Tribes: The islands are inhabited by indigenous tribes, including the Great Andamanese, Onge, Jarwa, and Sentinalese, who are of Negrito origin.

10-Degree Channel: This channel separates the Andaman Islands from the Nicobar Islands, delineating the two groups.

Geography: Situated in the Indian Ocean, the islands lie southwest of Myanmar and west of Thailand.

Flora and Fauna: The region is known for its rich biodiversity, home to many endemic species of plants, animals, and marine life.

Tourism: These islands are a popular tourist destination, famed for their pristine beaches, coral reefs, and historical sites.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one among the following local winds is NOT related to Mediterranean Sea?
questions.solution:
Key Points

Harmattan is a dry and dusty trade wind originating from the Sahara Desert, affecting the West African subcontinent. It primarily occurs from the end of November to mid-March, causing temperature drops and low humidity. This wind is not associated with the Mediterranean Sea, unlike the other winds listed.

Additional Information

Khamsin:

A hot, dry, and sandy wind sweeping across Egypt and the eastern Mediterranean region, usually during late winter and early spring.

Sirocco:

A warm, humid wind that begins in the Sahara, moving northward across the Mediterranean Sea. It can carry dust and sand, impacting southern Europe.

Mistral:

A strong, cold northwesterly wind flowing through the Rhône valley and southern France, reaching the Mediterranean Sea. It often brings clear and dry weather.

Local Winds:

Defined by the local geography and topography, these winds significantly impact the climate and weather patterns of the regions they traverse.

questions.solution:

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Which one among the following statements with reference to Benguela current is NOT correct?
options: It influences the climate of the West coast of North Africa
Its direction is from South to North
It flows in the Atlantic Ocean
questions.solution:

Option 1: It influences the climate of the West coast of North Africa.

• The Benguela Current is a cold, northward-flowing current in the Atlantic off the southwest coast of Africa (Namibia and Angola), so it affects southern, not northern, Africa’s west-coast climate.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: The lowest part of most bolsons is occupied by a landform called playa. Which category of landform formation does it represent?
questions.solution:
A playa is a flat, dry lake bed formed by the evaporation of standing water in a desert basin. It’s characteristic of arid-region processes, so it falls under:

Option 2: Arid landforms.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following pairs of Himalayan Passes and the Ranges in which they are located is/are correctly matched?

1. Zoji La - Great Himalayas

2. Banihal Pass - Pir Panjal Range

3. Photu La - Ladakh Range

4. Khardung La - Karakoram Range

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Zoji La - Great Himalayas: This is correct! Zoji La is a crucial pass in the Zaskar Range, which is a part of the magnificent Great Himalayas. It's a lifeline connecting Kashmir to Ladakh.

Banihal Pass - Pir Panjal Range: This one is also correct! The Banihal Pass is indeed located in the Pir Panjal Range, which is part of the Lesser Himalayas. It's historically significant for connecting Jammu with Srinagar.

Photu La - Ladakh Range: This is incorrect. Photu La (also spelled Fatu La) is a high mountain pass on the Srinagar-Leh highway, but it's actually located in the Zanskar Range, not the Ladakh Range.

Khardung La - Karakoram Range: This is also incorrect. While Khardung La is incredibly high and famous, it's situated in the Ladakh Range, which is south of the Karakoram Range. The Karakoram Range is even further north.

So, out of the four, only the first two pairs are correctly matched!

Therefore, the correct answer is 1 and 2 only.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Which one among the following state- ments with reference to the black cotton soils of India is NOT correct?
options: The black cotton soils are also called 'Regur'
These soils have less clay factor and hence can support a wide variety of crops and vegetables
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 4.

Explanation:

Black cotton soils are indeed called “Regur.”

They have very high moisture-retention because of their clay content.

They’re rich in lime (calcium compounds), as well as iron, magnesia and potash.

In fact, these soils have a very high clay factor – they swell when wet and crack when dry – which makes them heavy and less suited to a wide variety of crops and vegetables. Hence Option 4 is false.
