questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists:

List – I

(Tropical Cyclone)

List - II

(Country)

A.

 Hurricanes

1.

 China

B.

 Willy Willy

2.

 Philippines

C.

 Baguio

3.

 Australia

D.

 Typhoons

4.

 USA
questions.solution:
A. Hurricanes: These powerful storms are what we call tropical cyclones in the Atlantic Ocean and the northeastern Pacific. They frequently affect the USA and the Caribbean. So, A goes with 4.

B. Willy Willy: This is the local name for a tropical cyclone in Australia. Sounds quite distinct, doesn't it? So, B goes with 3.

C. Baguio: When a tropical cyclone hits the Philippines, especially from the Pacific, it's often referred to as a Baguio. So, C goes with 2.

D. Typhoons: These are tropical cyclones that form in the northwestern Pacific Ocean, impacting countries like China, Japan, Korea, and also the Philippines (where they can be called Baguio, a specific type of typhoon). So, D goes with 1.

Putting it all together, we have:

A - 4

B - 3

C - 2

D - 1

This matches with Option 1.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following facts of the rivers of the Indus system:

1. The Satluj originates in Tibet where it is known as Langchen Khambab

2. The Satluj is a classic example of an antecedent river

3. The Jhelum is the largest tributary of the Indus System

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
The Satluj originates in Tibet where it is known as Langchen Khambab.

This is absolutely correct! The Satluj River, also spelled Sutlej, does indeed originate in Tibet, near Lake Rakshastal, and is known there as Langqên Zangbo, which translates to "Elephant River." "Langchen Khambab" is another common transliteration for its Tibetan name.

The Satluj is a classic example of an antecedent river.

This statement is also true! An antecedent river is one that existed before the current topography was formed and maintained its course by eroding through rising landforms. The Satluj is a prime example of this, as it cuts right through the mighty Himalayan range, maintaining its path even as the mountains uplifted.

The Jhelum is the largest tributary of the Indus System.

This statement is actually incorrect. While the Jhelum is a very important tributary, the Chenab River is generally considered the largest tributary of the Indus, both in terms of discharge and total length, within the Indus system in India. The Satluj is also a very significant tributary.

So, based on our review, statements 1 and 2 are correct, while statement 3 is incorrect.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 3: 1 and 2.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Which one of the following is an example of both an igneous as well as a metamorphic rock?
questions.solution:
The answer is Granite.

Key Points

Granite is an igneous rock that forms from the slow crystallization of magma beneath the Earth's surface.

It primarily consists of quartz, feldspar, and mica minerals, contributing to its granular texture.

Under high pressure and temperature, granite can transform into a metamorphic rock known as gneiss.

During this metamorphic process, the mineral components of granite can realign, forming new structures while retaining identifiable original minerals.

Additional Information

Igneous Rocks:

Formed from the cooling and solidification of magma or lava.

Examples include basalt, granite, and pumice.

Classified into intrusive (plutonic) and extrusive (volcanic) types.

Metamorphic Rocks:

Formed from the alteration of existing rocks in the Earth's crust due to heat, pressure, and chemically active fluids.

Examples include gneiss, schist, and marble.

There are two main types: foliated (with a layered appearance) and non-foliated (without a layered appearance).

Formation of Gneiss:

Gneiss is a high-grade metamorphic rock formed from pre-existing rocks such as granite, shale, or volcanic rocks.

It is characterized by its banded texture, resulting from the segregation of mineral layers.

Geological Significance:

Granite is widely used in construction because of its durability and aesthetic qualities.

Metamorphic rocks like gneiss provide insights into geological processes and conditions within the Earth's crust.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Where is the Coriolis Effect the largest ?
options: At the Tropic of Capricorn and Tropic of Cancer
At 45 degree latitude in each. hemisphere
questions.solution:
The Coriolis parameter is given by

$$f = 2\,\Omega\,\sin\varphi$$

where

$\Omega$ is Earth’s rotation rate,

$\varphi$ is latitude.

Key points:

At the Equator ($\varphi=0^\circ$), $\sin0^\circ=0$, so $f=0$.

At mid-latitudes ($\varphi=45^\circ$), $\sin45^\circ\approx0.71$.

At the pole ($\varphi=90^\circ$), $\sin90^\circ=1$, so $f$ is at its maximum.

Therefore the Coriolis effect is largest at the poles.

Answer: Option 3 – At the North Pole.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
In which among the following states in India, the Sun rays are never perpendicular?

1. Bihar

2. Chhattisgarh

3. Manipur

4. Rajasthan

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
The Tropic of Cancer (23°26′ N) is the northernmost latitude at which the Sun can be directly overhead. Any part of a state lying south of that line will see the Sun’s rays perpendicular at least once a year.

• Bihar lies between roughly 24°20′ N and 27°31′ N — entirely north of 23°26′ N.

• Manipur lies between about 23°50′ N and 25°41′ N — also entirely north of 23°26′ N.

• Chhattisgarh and Rajasthan both straddle the Tropic, dipping below 23°26′ N, so parts of them do get the Sun overhead.

Hence the Sun’s rays are never perpendicular in Bihar and Manipur.

Answer: Option 2 (1 and 3)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following is/are east flowing rivers in India?

1. Palar

2. Periyar

3. Pennar

4. Kalinadi

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Palar: This river originates in Karnataka and flows through Tamil Nadu, eventually emptying into the Bay of Bengal. The Bay of Bengal is located on India's eastern coast, so Palar is an east-flowing river.

Periyar: This is a significant river in Kerala. It flows westward into the Arabian Sea. Therefore, Periyar is a west-flowing river.

Pennar: Also known as Penna, this river originates in Karnataka and flows eastward through Andhra Pradesh before draining into the Bay of Bengal. So, Pennar is an east-flowing river.

Kalinadi: When referred to as Kalinadi in the context of major Indian rivers distinguishing east/west flow, it typically refers to the Kali River in Karnataka. This river flows westward and joins the Arabian Sea. Therefore, Kalinadi (in this context) is a west-flowing river.

Based on this, Palar and Pennar are the east-flowing rivers from the given list.

So, the correct choice is:

1 and 3

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following tributaries is/are correctly matched with the Deccan Rivers?

Tributary

River

1.

 Bhavani Krishna

2.

 Kabini Kaveri

3.

 Manjra Godavari

4.

 Bhima Mahanadi


Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Bhavani - Krishna: This pair is actually incorrect. The Bhavani River is a significant tributary of the Kaveri River, flowing through parts of Kerala and Tamil Nadu.

Kabini - Kaveri: This one is correct! The Kabini River is indeed a major tributary of the Kaveri River, originating in Wayanad, Kerala, and flowing into Karnataka.

Manjra - Godavari: This pair is also correct! The Manjra River is an important tributary of the Godavari River, flowing through Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Telangana.

Bhima - Mahanadi: This pair is incorrect. The Bhima River is a major tributary of the Krishna River, originating in the Bhimashankar hills and flowing through Maharashtra and Karnataka.

So, the correctly matched pairs are 2 and 3.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 2 (2 and 3).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one of the following is found in appreciable amount in Laterite soil in India?
questions.solution:
Phosphate is notably present in Laterite soil in India.

Key Points

Composition: Laterite soils are rich in iron oxide and aluminum.

Formation: They develop in hot, wet tropical areas, characterized by intense weathering.

Geographic Distribution: In India, Laterite soils are mainly found in Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Madhya Pradesh, and the hilly regions of Odisha and Assam.

Suitability for Crops: The presence of phosphate makes Laterite soil suitable for cultivating crops such as tea, coffee, and cashew.

Additional Information

Formation of Laterite Soil:

The process of laterization results in Laterite soil, where silica and other minerals are leached out, leaving behind iron and aluminum oxides.

This process typically occurs in tropical regions with high rainfall and temperatures.

Agricultural Suitability:

Generally, Laterite soil is not very fertile for traditional agriculture due to its high iron and aluminum content.

It is, however, suitable for some specific crops like tea, coffee, rubber, coconut, and cashew.

Challenges with Laterite Soil:

Once dry, Laterite soil can become very hard and compact, complicating tillage.

The soil is prone to erosion because of its loose top layer.

Soil Management:

Enhancing Laterite soil's fertility often requires the addition of organic matter and fertilizers.

Implementing proper irrigation and drainage systems is crucial to manage moisture levels and control erosion.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding River Brahmaputra :

1. It rises in Tibet, near Lake Mansarovar

2. It takes a "U" turn near Namcha Barwa and enters India through a gorge

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Statement 1: It rises in Tibet, near Lake Mansarovar

This is absolutely correct. The Brahmaputra, known as Yarlung Tsangpo in Tibet, originates from the Angsi Glacier, which is located in the Himalayas, very close to the sacred Mount Kailash and Lake Manasarovar.

Statement 2: It takes a "U" turn near Namcha Barwa and enters India through a gorge

This is also completely true. The river flows eastward for a long stretch in Tibet and then makes a dramatic, sharp southward bend, often called a "hairpin bend" or "U-turn," around the majestic Namcha Barwa peak. After this turn, it carves its way through a deep gorge as it enters Arunachal Pradesh in India.

So, the correct option is that both 1 and 2 are correct.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the International Date Line :

1. It is roughly 180° meridian, which deviates slightly East and West to avoid land areas surrounded by the Atlantic Ocean.

2. The date to the East of this line is (24 hours) earlier than to the West.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement regarding the International Date Line (IDL):

Statement 1: It is roughly 180° meridian, which deviates slightly East and West to avoid land areas surrounded by the Atlantic Ocean.

This statement is partially correct. The International Date Line is indeed close to the 180° meridian, but the part about deviating to avoid land areas surrounded by the Atlantic Ocean is incorrect. In reality, the IDL deviates around land masses, particularly in the Pacific Ocean, to prevent splitting countries into different calendar days.

Statement 2: The date to the East of this line is 24 hours earlier than to the West.

This statement is correct. When you cross the IDL from East to West, you move into the next calendar day, effectively gaining 24 hours. Conversely, when you cross from West to East, you move back one calendar day, losing 24 hours.

Given the analysis:

Correct Answer: Option B - 2 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding 'aurora' is/are correct?

1. The solar wind upon reaching the Earth's atmosphere is directed towards two magnetic poles and a colourful display of lights is seen in the night sky.

2. The particles interact with the different gases of the atmosphere and each gas glows with a particular colour.

3. In April 2023, aurora was captured in the night sky in India by the Indian Astronomical Observatory at Hanle, Ladakh.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
The aurora, also known as the northern or southern lights, is a natural light display predominantly seen in high-latitude regions around the Arctic and Antarctic. It is caused by the interaction of charged particles from the solar wind with the Earth's magnetosphere and atmosphere. Let's analyze the given statements :

1. The solar wind upon reaching the Earth's atmosphere is directed towards two magnetic poles and a colourful display of lights is seen in the night sky.

This statement is correct. When the solar wind, which consists of charged particles (mainly electrons and protons), reaches the Earth, its magnetic field directs these particles towards the magnetic poles. As these particles collide with atoms and molecules in the Earth's atmosphere, they excite these atoms and molecules, causing them to emit light. This light emission results in the aurora.

2. The particles interact with the different gases of the atmosphere and each gas glows with a particular colour.

This statement is also correct. Different gases emit different colors when they are excited by the charged particles. For example, oxygen emits green or red light, while nitrogen emits blue or purplish-red light. The specific colors observed in an aurora depend on the types of gas molecules involved and the altitude at which the interactions occur.

3. In April 2023, aurora was captured in the night sky in India by the Indian Astronomical Observatory at Hanle, Ladakh.

This statement is also correct. In recent times, there have been reports of aurora sightings in regions where they are not commonly seen, due to increased solar activity. In April 2023, the Indian Astronomical Observatory at Hanle, Ladakh, did capture images of aurora in the night sky, making it an unusual but documented event.

Thus, all three statements are accurate. Based on the given options, the correct answer is :

Option D

1, 2 and 3

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding Barchan is/are correct?

1. It is a crescent shaped mound of sand, which is deposited by the wind blowing constantly from one direction in a desert.

2. The windward side has a convex steep slope with maximum height at the centre.

3. Two ends of Barchan are called horns and point opposite to the direction the wind blows.

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To address the statements regarding Barchans and determine which ones are correct, let's analyze each statement in detail:

Statement 1: "It is a crescent-shaped mound of sand, which is deposited by the wind blowing constantly from one direction in a desert."

This statement is correct. A barchan is indeed a crescent-shaped sand dune formed in deserts by winds consistently blowing from one direction.

Statement 2: "The windward side has a convex steep slope with maximum height at the centre."

This statement is incorrect. The windward side of a barchan (the side facing the wind) typically has a gentle slope rather than a steep one. It is the leeward side (the side away from the wind) that has a steeper, concave slope.

Statement 3: "Two ends of Barchan are called horns and point opposite to the direction the wind blows."

This statement is correct. The horns of a barchan indeed point in the direction that the wind is blowing towards.

Based on the analysis, only statements 1 and 3 are correct. Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: 1 and 3 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding Regur soil is/are correct?

1. It is a light coloured, clayey and fertile soil.

2. It is developed on Deccan basaltic lava under hot and humid conditions.

3. Cotton is extensively cultivated in this soil.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
To evaluate which of the given statements about Regur soil (also known as Black soil) are correct, let's carefully analyze each statement :

Statement 1 : It is a light coloured, clayey and fertile soil.

This statement is incorrect. Regur soil is characteristically dark (black or deep brown) in color, not light. Hence, this statement does not accurately describe Regur soil.

Statement 2 : It is developed on Deccan basaltic lava under hot and humid conditions.

This statement is correct. Regur soil is indeed formed from the weathering of basalt or trap rocks, specifically in the Deccan Plateau region. These soils have developed under semi-arid to arid conditions which are generally hot and can be humid.

Statement 3 : Cotton is extensively cultivated in this soil.

This statement is correct. Regur soil, also known as Black Cotton Soil, is famously known for being highly suitable for cotton cultivation. The soil's moisture-retentive capacity and fertility support the growth of cotton extensively.

To summarize :

Statement 1 is incorrect.

Statements 2 and 3 are correct.

So, the correct option is :

Option B : 2 and 3

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding clouds is/are correct?

1. Clouds are classified on the basis of altitude and their form.

2. According to altitude they are classified as High, Middle and Low clouds.

3. Stratus, Nimbostratus and Stratocumulus are high clouds.

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To determine the correct answer, let's analyze each statement about clouds:

1. Clouds are classified on the basis of altitude and their form.

This statement is correct. Clouds are indeed classified based on their altitude (height above the ground) and their physical form (appearance).

2. According to altitude they are classified as High, Middle and Low clouds.

This statement is also correct. Clouds are commonly categorized into high, middle, and low clouds based on their altitude:

  • High clouds: Above 20,000 feet (cirrus, cirrostratus, cirrocumulus)
  • Middle clouds: Between 6,500 and 20,000 feet (altostratus, altocumulus)
  • Low clouds: Below 6,500 feet (stratus, stratocumulus, nimbostratus)

3. Stratus, Nimbostratus and Stratocumulus are high clouds.

This statement is incorrect. Stratus, nimbostratus, and stratocumulus are actually types of low clouds. High clouds are generally cirrus, cirrostratus, and cirrocumulus.

Based on this analysis:

Statement 1 is correct.

Statement 2 is correct.

Statement 3 is incorrect.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: 1 and 2 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding 'fronts':

1. The movement of a front causes a slow change in weather in the area over which it moves.

2. Cold fronts are associated with thunderstorms.

3. Warm front is the boundary between an advancing mass of warm air where it is overriding and rising above a mass of colder air.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
To determine the correct answer to the question about 'fronts' in meteorology, let's break down each of the statements and understand their correctness.

Statement 1: "The movement of a front causes a slow change in weather in the area over which it moves."

This statement is generally not true. The movement of a front often leads to a relatively rapid change in weather conditions as warmer and colder air masses meet and clash. Therefore, this statement is incorrect.

Statement 2: "Cold fronts are associated with thunderstorms."

This statement is correct. Cold fronts can cause dramatic weather changes, including the development of thunderstorms. This happens because the advancing cold air forces the warm air to rise rapidly, creating conditions that can lead to thunderstorms.

Statement 3: "Warm front is the boundary between an advancing mass of warm air where it is overriding and rising above a mass of colder air."

This statement is correct. A warm front occurs when a warm air mass advances over a cold air mass, causing the warm air to rise above the cold air. This is often associated with steady precipitation and cloud formation.

Based on the analysis, the correct statements are 2 and 3.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C

2 and 3 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding soils :

1. Soils having a very high content of sodium and calcium and pH of more than 7.0 are alkaline soils.

2. Black cotton soil had developed on the Deccan basaltic lava under hot and humid conditions.

3. Laterite soils are characterised by a deep weathered layer from which silica has been leached.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Alkaline Soils:


  • High pH Level: Alkaline soils are typically characterized by a high pH level, usually above 7.0.

  • Salt Concentration: They often contain a high concentration of salts, particularly sodium, and sometimes calcium. However, it is uncommon to find very high concentrations of both sodium and calcium together in alkaline soils.

  • Sodic Soils: Soils with a high sodium content are generally referred to as sodic soils.

  • Conclusion: Hence, statement 1 is incorrect.

Black Cotton Soil:


  • Location: Black cotton soil, also known as Regur soil, is predominantly found in the Deccan Plateau region of India.

  • Formation: It forms from the weathering of basaltic lava under specific climatic conditions, including hot and humid weather.

  • Properties: This soil is rich in clay minerals and has high moisture retention properties, making it suitable for cotton cultivation.

  • Conclusion: Hence, statement 2 is correct.

Laterite Soils:


  • Leached Soils: Laterite soils are heavily leached soils found in tropical and subtropical regions with high rainfall.

  • Weathering: They are characterized by a deep weathered layer from which silica and other soluble minerals have been leached out.

  • Composition: These soils are rich in iron and aluminum oxides, giving them a distinctive red or yellow color.

  • Conclusion: Hence, statement 3 is correct.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: 2 and 3 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Consider the following characteristics of a grassland :

1. An extensive area of mid latitude grasslands are devoid of trees and shrubs.

2. The climate is characterised by hot summers, cold winters and relatively low rainfall occurring mainly in spring and summer.

3. In humid parts, grasses grow to a meter or more in height.

On the basis of the above characteristics, select the correct grassland from the options given below :
questions.solution:
Based on the provided characteristics, the correct answer is Steppes. Here’s a detailed explanation:

Characteristics of Grasslands

  1. Extensive Mid-Latitude Grasslands Devoid of Trees and Shrubs:
  • The Steppes are well-known for their vast expanses without trees and shrubs. This is mainly due to the semi-arid climate that does not support the growth of large vegetation.

  • These grasslands are typically found in regions with continental climates, where temperature and precipitation patterns are not conducive to tree growth.
  1. Climate:
  • The Steppes experience hot summers and cold winters, characteristic of mid-latitude regions with continental climates.

  • Rainfall in these regions is generally low, averaging between 250 to 500 mm annually, and mostly occurs during the spring and summer months.
  1. Grass Height in Humid Parts:
  • In the more humid areas of the Steppes, grasses can grow to a meter or more in height due to increased humidity and slightly better soil moisture conditions.

  • In drier parts of the Steppes, the grass remains shorter due to the lack of sufficient moisture.

Additional Information

  • Geographical Distribution:

  • Steppes are extensive plains found in regions such as Central Asia, Eastern Europe, and parts of North America.

  • They are characterized by a semi-arid climate, which limits the growth of trees and large shrubs.

  • Historically, the Steppes have been home to various nomadic tribes and civilizations.

  • Comparison with Other Grasslands:

  • Prairies:

  • Found in North America, particularly in the United States and Canada.

  • Prairies also experience hot summers and cold winters, but they receive more rainfall compared to the Steppes, supporting taller grasses and even some isolated trees.

  • Savanna:

  • A type of grassland found in tropical regions, such as Africa, South America, and Australia.

  • Savannas are characterized by a mix of grasses and scattered trees and experience a distinct wet and dry season.

  • Pampas:

  • Vast plains located in South America, particularly in Argentina and Uruguay.

  • The Pampas have a more moderate climate and receive more rainfall than the Steppes, supporting lush grasslands suitable for agriculture and cattle ranching.

Conclusion

Based on the characteristics provided, including the extensive areas devoid of trees and shrubs, hot summers and cold winters, relatively low rainfall occurring mainly in spring and summer, and tall grasses in humid parts, the correct grassland is:

Option A: Steppes

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding neap tides is/are correct?

1. It occurs every 14 – 15 days, which coincides with the first and third quarter of the Moon.

2. This tide has a small tidal range because the gravitational forces of the Moon and the Sun are in quadrature.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
Neap tides occur when the gravitational forces of the Moon and the Sun are perpendicular to each other, which typically coincides with the first and third quarters of the Moon. During neap tides, the tidal range is smaller because the gravitational forces from the Moon and the Sun partially cancel each other out.

Let's evaluate the statements one by one :

1. It occurs every 14 – 15 days, which coincides with the first and third quarter of the Moon.

This statement is correct because neap tides occur during the first and third quarters of the Moon, which happens approximately every 14-15 days.

2. This tide has a small tidal range because the gravitational forces of the Moon and the Sun are in quadrature.

This statement is also correct. The term "quadrature" refers to the Sun and the Moon being at a 90-degree angle relative to the Earth, which causes their gravitational forces to partially cancel each other, resulting in a smaller tidal range.

Therefore, both statements 1 and 2 are correct.

Thus, the correct answer is :

Option C

Both 1 and 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding earthquakes is/are correct?

1. The point on the Earth’s surface directly above the focus is called the epicentre of the earthquake.

2. Earthquakes generate Primary and Secondary waves that radiate outward from the earthquake focus.

3. Deep-focus earthquakes are likely to cause more damage than shallow-focus earthquakes.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
To determine the correct statements regarding earthquakes, let us analyze each statement :

Statement 1 : The point on the Earth’s surface directly above the focus is called the epicentre of the earthquake.

This statement is correct. The focus (or hypocenter) is the point inside the Earth where an earthquake originates. The epicentre is the point on the Earth's surface directly above the focus.

Statement 2 : Earthquakes generate Primary and Secondary waves that radiate outward from the earthquake focus.

This statement is also correct. When an earthquake occurs, it generates seismic waves. There are two main types of body waves: Primary (P) waves, which are compressional waves that travel fastest through the Earth, and Secondary (S) waves, which are shear waves that travel slower than P waves. Both types of waves radiate outward from the earthquake focus.

Statement 3 : Deep-focus earthquakes are likely to cause more damage than shallow-focus earthquakes.

This statement is incorrect. The depth of the earthquake focus significantly influences the level of damage caused. Shallow-focus earthquakes, which occur at depths less than 70 kilometers, generally cause more damage at the Earth's surface compared to deep-focus earthquakes, which occur at greater depths (300 to 700 kilometers). The energy from deep-focus earthquakes has to travel through more material, losing strength by the time it reaches the surface.

Based on the above analysis :

Option A is incorrect (Statement 1 only)

Option B is correct (Statements 1 and 2 only)

Option C is incorrect (Statements 2 and 3 only)

Option D is incorrect (Statements 1, 2, and 3)

Hence, the correct answer is :

Option B

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding air temperature is/are correct?

1. Air temperature is measured at a standard height of 1.2 m (4-0 feet) above the ground surface.

2. The average rate of temperature decrease with height is termed as the environmental temperature lapse rate.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
When analyzing the statements regarding air temperature :

  1. Air temperature is typically measured at a standard height of 1.2 meters (about 4 feet) above the ground surface, but the actual standard height is often considered to be slightly higher at around 1.25 to 2 meters (4.1 to 6.6 feet), depending on the specific guidelines being followed. Therefore, the given height is somewhat close but not entirely precise by some standards.

  1. The average rate of temperature decrease with height in the troposphere is indeed termed the environmental temperature lapse rate. It is generally about $$6.5$$ degrees Celsius per kilometer (about $$3.5$$ degrees Fahrenheit per 1000 feet) under standard atmospheric conditions.

Considering these points, we understand that :

  • Statement 1 is generally correct, though the standard height can vary slightly.

  • Statement 2 is correct.

Therefore, the most accurate answer based on the provided options is :

Option C : Both 1 and 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding humidity is/are correct?

1. It is a ratio between the actual amount of water vapour present in the atmosphere and the maximum amount it can hold at a given temperature.

2. When the relative humidity is high, more water evaporates from the skin.

3. Higher the air temperature lower the relative humidity of the air.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement regarding humidity:

Statement 1: "It is a ratio between the actual amount of water vapour present in the atmosphere and the maximum amount it can hold at a given temperature."

This statement correctly defines the concept of relative humidity. Relative humidity is indeed the ratio of the current amount of water vapor to the maximum amount the air can hold at a specific temperature, expressed as a percentage. Therefore, statement 1 is correct.

Statement 2: "When the relative humidity is high, more water evaporates from the skin."

This statement is incorrect. When the relative humidity is high, the air is already saturated with moisture, which means that the rate of evaporation of water from the skin decreases. High relative humidity makes it harder for sweat to evaporate, which is why hot and humid conditions tend to feel more uncomfortable.

Statement 3: "Higher the air temperature lower the relative humidity of the air."

This statement is generally true. As the temperature increases, the capacity of the air to hold water vapor also increases. If the actual amount of water vapor remains constant while the temperature rises, the relative humidity will decrease. Therefore, statement 3 is correct.

Considering the above analysis, the correct statements are 1 and 3. Thus, the correct option is:

Option C
1 and 3 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding cyclones and anti-cyclones is/are correct?

1. In the Northern Hemisphere, cyclones rotate counter clockwise and anticyclones rotate clockwise.

2. Cyclones are often associated with cloudy or rainy weather, whereas anticyclones are often associated with fair weather.

3. In the Southern Hemisphere, the cyclonic spiral will be clockwise because the Coriolis force acts to the left.

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To determine the correct statements regarding cyclones and anti-cyclones, let's analyze each statement individually in detail:

Statement 1: In the Northern Hemisphere, cyclones rotate counter clockwise and anticyclones rotate clockwise.

This statement is correct. Due to the Coriolis effect, which is a result of the Earth's rotation, cyclones (low pressure systems) rotate counterclockwise in the Northern Hemisphere, while anticyclones (high pressure systems) rotate clockwise.

Statement 2: Cyclones are often associated with cloudy or rainy weather, whereas anticyclones are often associated with fair weather.

This statement is also correct. Cyclones are low-pressure systems where air converges and rises, leading to cloud formation and precipitation. On the other hand, anticyclones are high-pressure systems where air descends, leading to clear skies and fair weather.

Statement 3: In the Southern Hemisphere, the cyclonic spiral will be clockwise because the Coriolis force acts to the left.

This statement is correct as well. In the Southern Hemisphere, the Coriolis effect acts in the opposite direction compared to the Northern Hemisphere. As a result, cyclones rotate clockwise and anticyclones rotate counterclockwise.

Based on this analysis, all three statements are correct. Therefore, the answer is:

Option D

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Identify the sea-port in India on the basis of the given characteristics:

1. It is more than a century old port on the west coast of India.

2. It is a natural harbour protected by a breakwater and also by a mole.

3. A deep draft channel of about 14 metres depth permits large vessels to enter the harbour.

Select the correct sea-port from the options given below:
questions.solution:
The sea-port that matches the given characteristics is Mormugao Port.

Explanation:

1. Age of the Port: Mormugao Port is more than a century old. It has been in operation for many years and is one of India's oldest ports, located on the west coast of India in the state of Goa.

2. Natural Harbour: Mormugao Port is a natural harbour, meaning it is naturally protected from high seas and currents. The port is further sheltered by a breakwater and a mole, which safeguard it from strong waves and currents, making it an ideal and safe harbor for ships.

3. Deep Draft Channel: The port has a deep draft channel of about 14 meters depth, which allows large vessels to enter the harbour without difficulty. This feature is crucial for facilitating large-scale maritime trade and the docking of sizable ships.

Considering these characteristics, the correct sea-port from the options given is:

Option A

Mormugao Port

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Identify the Iron and Steel Plant on the basis of the given characteristics:

1. It receives coal from Jharia and iron-ore from Sundargarh and Kendujhar.

2. Power for the electric furnaces is procured from Hirakund.

3. Water is obtained from Koel and Sankh rivers.

Select the correct Iron and Steel Plant from the options given below:
questions.solution:
The Iron and Steel Plant described based on the given characteristics is the Rourkela Steel Plant. Here's the reasoning:

1. It receives coal from Jharia and iron-ore from Sundargarh and Kendujhar.

2. Power for the electric furnaces is procured from Hirakund.

3. Water is obtained from Koel and Sankh rivers.

These details match precisely with the Rourkela Steel Plant's geographical and logistical setup:

Explanation:

1. Raw Materials: The Rourkela Steel Plant sources coal from Jharia, which is a coal mining area and one of the best sources of coking coal in India. It also procures iron ore from Sundargarh and Kendujhar, both located in Odisha, which are rich in iron ore deposits.

2. Power Supply: The Hirakund Dam supplies electricity to the plant. This dam is a significant hydroelectric power source, providing the required power for the electric furnaces in the Rourkela Steel Plant.

3. Water Supply: The plant draws water from the Koel and Sankh rivers. These rivers provide an adequate and continuous supply of water necessary for steel production processes.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C

Rourkela Steel Plant

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding Earth's internal structure is/are correct?

1. The oceanic crust is heavier than the continental crust.

2. Most of the Earth's internal heat is contained within the mantle.

3. Large convective cells in the crust circulate heat and drive plate-tectonic processes.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
To determine which statements regarding Earth's internal structure are correct, let's analyze each one:

Statement 1: The oceanic crust is heavier than the continental crust.

This statement is correct. The oceanic crust is generally denser and heavier than the continental crust because it is composed of basaltic rocks, whereas the continental crust is composed primarily of granitic rocks.

Statement 2: Most of the Earth's internal heat is contained within the mantle.

This statement is also correct. The mantle, which lies between the Earth's crust and core, is the largest layer in terms of volume and contains the majority of the Earth's internal heat. This heat is primarily due to radioactive decay and residual heat from the planet's formation.

Statement 3: Large convective cells in the crust circulate heat and drive plate-tectonic processes.

This statement is incorrect. While convection processes do drive plate-tectonic movements, these convective cells are located in the mantle, not the crust. The mantle's convective currents create the motion of tectonic plates on the Earth's surface.

Given the analysis above, the correct combination of statements is 1 and 2 only.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option B

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the given rivers is/are correct?

1. Tsangpo crosses over into India under the name of Dihang.

2. Godavari has the second largest river basin covering about 10 percent of the area of India.

3. Rivers Chambal and Betwa are the important tributaries of river Ganga which join river Ganga directly in Uttar Pradesh.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement regarding the given rivers:

Statement 1: Tsangpo crosses over into India under the name of Dihang.

This statement is correct. The river Tsangpo, which flows through Tibet, enters India in the state of Arunachal Pradesh where it is known as Dihang or Siang. It later joins other tributaries to become the Brahmaputra River that flows into Assam.

Statement 2: Godavari has the second largest river basin covering about 10 percent of the area of India.

This statement is also correct. The Godavari River, often referred to as the Dakshin Ganga, has the second largest river basin in India. It covers about 10% of India's total geographical area, making it one of the most significant river systems in the country.

Statement 3: Rivers Chambal and Betwa are the important tributaries of river Ganga which join river Ganga directly in Uttar Pradesh.

This statement is incorrect. The Chambal and Betwa rivers are indeed important tributaries, but they join the Yamuna River, not the Ganga, in Uttar Pradesh. The Yamuna River itself is a significant tributary of the Ganges, but Chambal and Betwa do not join the Ganges directly.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B

1 and 2 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the World Climate Types and their relation with Inter Tropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) is/are correct?

1. Tropical Wet Climate is one of the world's rainiest due to the dominance of the ITCZ over it.

2. Tropical Savanna Climate experiences a distinctive alternation of wet and dry seasons, caused chiefly by the seasonal shift in latitude of the subtropical highs and ITCZ.

3. The Tropical Monsoonal Climate experiences heavy rainfall as a consequence of the nearness of the ITCZ for much of the year.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
To determine which statements regarding World Climate Types and their relationship with the Inter Tropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) are correct, let us analyze each statement :

Statement 1 : Tropical Wet Climate is one of the world's rainiest climates due to the dominance of the ITCZ over it.

Explanation : The Tropical Wet Climate, also known as the tropical rainforest climate, is characterized by high humidity and consistent, heavy rainfall throughout the year. This is indeed influenced by the ITCZ, which is a zone of low pressure and converging trade winds, leading to significant precipitation. Thus, this statement is correct.

Statement 2 : Tropical Savanna Climate experiences a distinctive alternation of wet and dry seasons, caused chiefly by the seasonal shift in latitude of the subtropical highs and ITCZ.

Explanation : The Tropical Savanna Climate, also known as the tropical wet and dry or tropical monsoon climate, features a pronounced wet and dry season. This pattern is primarily due to the seasonal migration of the ITCZ and the subtropical high-pressure systems. During the wet season, the ITCZ moves into the region, bringing moisture and rainfall. During the dry season, the ITCZ shifts away, and the subtropical high-pressure system dominates, resulting in dry conditions. Hence, this statement is also correct.

Statement 3 : The Tropical Monsoonal Climate experiences heavy rainfall as a consequence of the nearness of the ITCZ for much of the year.

Explanation : The Tropical Monsoonal Climate is known for its distinct monsoon patterns with significant seasonal variations in precipitation. This climate experiences heavy rainfall during the monsoon season due to the ITCZ's proximity, which brings moist air and precipitation. For much of the year, the ITCZ remains close, contributing to the heavy rainfall. Thus, this statement is correct.

Considering the analysis, all three statements are correct. Therefore, the answer is :

Option D : 1, 2 and 3

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding tropical cyclones is/are correct?

1. These storms develop during the summer and autumn in every tropical ocean except the South Atlantic and eastern South Pacific Oceans.

2. Tropical cyclones that occur in the North Atlantic and eastern North Pacific Oceans are known as typhoons.

3. Tropical cyclones that occur in the Indian and South Pacific Oceans are called cyclones.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
To determine which of the provided statements about tropical cyclones is correct, let’s examine each one in detail:

Statement 1: These storms develop during the summer and autumn in every tropical ocean except the South Atlantic and eastern South Pacific Oceans.

This statement is accurate. Tropical cyclones generally form in the summer and autumn when sea surface temperatures are sufficiently warm. However, the South Atlantic and eastern South Pacific do not typically see these storms due to various climatic and oceanic conditions that are not conducive to their formation.

Statement 2: Tropical cyclones that occur in the North Atlantic and eastern North Pacific Oceans are known as typhoons.

This statement is incorrect. Tropical cyclones in the North Atlantic and eastern North Pacific are called hurricanes, not typhoons. Typhoons are the term used for tropical cyclones that occur in the western North Pacific Ocean.

Statement 3: Tropical cyclones that occur in the Indian and South Pacific Oceans are called cyclones.

This statement is correct. Tropical cyclones in the Indian Ocean and the South Pacific are indeed referred to as cyclones.

Given this analysis, the correct statements are 1 and 3. Therefore, the answer is:

Option C

1 and 3 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
Identify the land biome on the basis of the given characteristics :

1. Their climates are characterised by high rainfall and temperatures that vary from cold to mild.

2. These forests contain primarily deciduous trees – including maple, oak, hickory and beechwood.

3. Raccoons, opossums, bats and squirrels are found in the trees.

Select the correct land biome from the options given below :
questions.solution:
The land biome described by the given characteristics is the Temperate forest, which corresponds to :

Option B

Here's the explanation for each characteristic :

1. Climate : Temperate forests have climates that are characterized by high rainfall and temperatures that vary from cold to mild. This fits the description provided.

2. Vegetation : They primarily contain deciduous trees such as maple, oak, hickory, and beechwood. These types of trees shed their leaves annually, which is typical of temperate forests.

3. Wildlife : Animals such as raccoons, opossums, bats, and squirrels are commonly found in the trees of temperate forests. These species adapt well to the changing seasons in temperate regions.

Therefore, the correct land biome is indeed the Temperate forest.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: The focal lengths of the objective and the eyepiece of a compound microscope are 1 cm and 2 cm respectively. If the tube length of the microscope is 10 cm , the magnification obtained by the microscope for most suitable viewing by relaxed eye is
questions.solution:
For the eyepiece :

$$\begin{array}{l} & \frac{1}{f_e} & =\frac{1}{v_e}-\frac{1}{u_e} \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{1}{2} & =\frac{1}{(-25)}-\frac{1}{u_e} \\ \Rightarrow & u_e & =-1.85 \mathrm{~cm} \end{array}$$

Since, the length of pipe is 10 cm.

$$\begin{aligned} \text { So, } & & \left|v_o\right|+\left|u_e\right| & =10 \mathrm{~cm} \\ \Rightarrow & & \left|v_o\right| & =10-1.85=8.15 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$$

For the objective lens:

$$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{f_o}=\frac{1}{v_o}-\frac{1}{u_o} \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{1}{1}=\frac{1}{8.15}-\frac{1}{u_o} \\ \Rightarrow & u_o=-0.877 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} &\text { Now, magnification, }\\ &\begin{aligned} & m=\frac{v_o}{\left|u_o\right|}\left(1+\frac{D}{f_e}\right) \\ & m=\frac{8.15}{0.877}\left(1+\frac{25}{2}\right) \\ & m=125.46 \simeq 125 \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: A telescope has an objective lens of focal length 150 cm and an eyepiece of focal length 5 cm . Calculate its magnifying power in normal adjustment and the distance of the image formed by the objective.
questions.solution:
Given,

$$\begin{aligned} & f_o=150 \mathrm{~cm} \\ & f_e=5 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$$

In normal adjustment,

$$m=-\frac{f_o}{f_e}=-\frac{150}{5}=-30$$

For objective lens,

$$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{f_o}=\frac{1}{v_o}-\frac{1}{u_o} \\ & \frac{1}{150}=\frac{1}{v_o}-\frac{1}{\infty} \\ & {\left[\because u_o=\infty, \text { as object is at infinity }\right] } \\ & v_0=150 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
A prism is an optical medium bounded by three refracting plane surfaces. A ray of light suffers successive refractions on passing through its two surfaces and deviates by a certain angle from its original path. The refractive index of the material of the prism is given by $\mu=\sin \left(\frac{A+\delta_m}{2}\right) / \sin \frac{A}{2}$. If the angle of incidence on the second surface is greater than an angle called critical angle, the ray will not be refracted from the second surface and is totally internally reflected.

(i) The critical angle for glass is $\theta_1$ and that for water is $\theta_2$. The critical angle for glass-water surface would be (given ${ }_a \mu_g=1.5,{ }_a \mu_w=1.33$ )

(A) less than $\theta_2$

(B) between $\theta_1$ and $\theta_2$

(C) greater than $\theta_2$

(D) less than $\theta_1$

(ii) When a ray of light of wavelength $\lambda$ and frequency $v$ is refracted into a denser medium

(A) $\lambda$ and $v$ both increase.

(B) $\lambda$ increases but $v$ is unchanged.

(C) $\lambda$ decreases but $v$ is unchanged.

(D) $\lambda$ and $v$ both decrease.

(iii) (a) The critical angle for a ray of light passing from glass to water is minimum for

(A) red colour

(B) blue colour

(C) yellow colour

(D) violet colour

OR

(b) Three beams of red, yellow and violet colours are passed through a prism, one by one under the same condition. When the prism is in the position of minimum deviation, the angles of refraction from the second surface are $r_{\mathrm{R}}, r_Y$ and $r_{\mathrm{V}}$ respectively. Then

(A) $r_V< r_Y

(B) $r_Y< r_R

(C) $r_R< r_Y

(D) $r_{\mathrm{R}}=r_{\mathrm{Y}}=r_{\mathrm{V}}$

(iv) A ray of light is incident normally on a prism ABC of refractive index $\sqrt{ } 2$, as shown in figure. After it strikes face AC, it will

(A) go straight undeviated

(B) just graze along the face AC

(C) refract and go out of the prism

(D) undergo total internal reflection
questions.solution:
(i) Option (C) is correct.

$\sin \theta_1=\frac{1}{\mu_g} ; \sin \theta_2=\frac{1}{\mu_w}$

since, $\mu_g>\mu_w, \theta_1<\theta_2$

Critical angle $\theta$ between glass and water will be given by, $\sin \theta=\frac{\mu_w}{\mu_g} \Rightarrow \theta>\theta_2$

(ii) Option (C) is correct.

When a ray of light is refracted into a denser medium, the frequency remains same but the wavelength decreases.

We know, $$\mu=\frac{v_{\text {rarer }}}{v_{\text {denser }}}>1$$

$$\begin{array}{lcl} \Rightarrow & v_{\text {rarer }}>v_{\text {denser }} & \\ \Rightarrow & \lambda_1 v_1>\lambda_2 v_2 & {\left[\text { as } v_1=v_2\right]} \\ \Rightarrow & \lambda_1>\lambda_2 & \end{array}$$

(iii) (a) Option (D) is correct.

The critical angle is least for violet colour because its refractive index is the highest.

OR

(b) Option (D) is correct.

In the case of minimum deviation, the refraction from the second surface for all the three rays are equal. For minimum deviation, the ray should be parallel to the base of the prism.

(iv) Option (D) is correct.

$$\begin{array}{l} \text { Explanation: } \quad & \mu =\sqrt{2} \\ \Rightarrow & \sin i_c =\frac{1}{\mu}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \\ & i_c =45^{\circ} \end{array}$$

Now,

As the incident angle is greater than the critical angle, the ray will undergo total internal reflection.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
A light ray is incident on the surface of a sphere of refractive index $n$ at an angle of incidence $\theta_0$. The ray partially refracts into the sphere with angle of refraction $\phi_0$ and then partly reflects from the back surface. The reflected ray then emerges out of the sphere after a partial refraction. The total angle of deviation of the emergent ray with respect to the incident ray is $\alpha$. Match the quantities mentioned in List-I with their values in List-II and choose the correct option.

List-I List-II
(P) If $n = 2$ and $\alpha = 180^\circ$, then all the possible values of $\theta_0$ will be (1) $30^\circ$ and $0^\circ$
(Q) If $n = \sqrt{3}$ and $\alpha = 180^\circ$, then all the possible values of $\theta_0$ will be (2) $60^\circ$ and $0^\circ$
(R) If $n = \sqrt{3}$ and $\alpha = 180^\circ$, then all the possible values of $\phi_0$ will be (3) $45^\circ$ and $0^\circ$
(S) If $n = \sqrt{2}$ and $\theta_0 = 45^\circ$, then all the possible values of $\alpha$ will be (4) $150^\circ$
(5) $0^\circ$
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} & \alpha=\left(\theta_0-\phi_0\right)+\left(180-2 \phi_0\right)+\left(\theta_0-2 \phi_0\right) \\ & \alpha=180+2 \theta_0-4 \phi_0 \end{aligned}\\ &\begin{aligned} & \text { (P) } \alpha=180+2 \theta_0-4 \phi_0 \\ & 180=180+2 \theta_0-4 \phi_0 \Rightarrow \theta_0=2 \phi_0 \quad \text{... (i}\\ & \sin \theta_0=2 \sin \phi_0 \quad \text{... (ii} \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

From (i) & (ii)

$$\begin{aligned} & \sin \theta_0=2 \sin \left(\theta_0 / 2\right) \Rightarrow \cos \left(\frac{\theta_0}{2}\right)=1 \\ & \frac{\theta_0}{2}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \theta_0=0 \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { (Q) } \theta_0=2 \phi_0 \quad \text{... (i)}\\ & \sin \theta_0=\sqrt{3} \sin \phi_0 \quad \text{... (ii)} \end{aligned}$$

From (i) & (ii)

$$\begin{aligned} & \sin \theta_0=\sqrt{3} \sin \left(\frac{\theta_0}{2}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \cos \left(\frac{\theta_0}{2}\right)=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \\ & \frac{\theta_0}{2}=30,150 \\ & \theta_0=60,300 \text { (Rejected) } \\ & \theta_0=60 \& 0 \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { (R) } \theta_0=2 \phi_0 \\ & \sin \theta_0=\sqrt{3} \sin \phi_0 \\ & \sin 2 \theta_0=\sqrt{3} \sin \phi_0 \\ & \cos \phi_0=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \\ & \phi_0=30,150 \text { (Rejected) } \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \phi_0=30 \& 0 \quad \text{... (i)}\\ & (S) \sin 45=\sqrt{2} \cos \phi_0 \\ & \cos \phi_0=1 / 2 \\ & \phi_0=60 \\ & \alpha=180+2 \theta_0-4 \phi_0 \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \alpha=180+90-120 \quad \text{... (iv)}\\ & =180-30 ; \alpha=150^{\circ} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A glass beaker has a solid, plano-convex base of refractive index 1.60, as shown in the figure. The radius of curvature of the convex surface (SPU) is $9 \mathrm{~cm}$, while the planar surface (STU) acts as a mirror. This beaker is filled with a liquid of refractive index $n$ up to the level QPR. If the image of a point object $\mathrm{O}$ at a height of $h$ (OT in the figure) is formed onto itself, then, which of the following option(s) is(are) correct?
questions.solution:
Since STU is a plane mirror, we can take mirror image of the whole situation about it and final image can be assumed to be at a distance h below the base.

Since object and image are at same distance from equivalent lens, hence $$\mathrm{h}=2 \mathrm{~F}_{\mathrm{qq}}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{\mathrm{~F}_{\mathrm{eq}}}=\left(\frac{1.6-1}{1}\right)\left(\frac{2}{9}\right)+\frac{(\mathrm{n}-1)}{1}\left(\frac{-2}{9}\right) \\ & \frac{1}{\frac{\mathrm{h}}{2}}=\frac{1.2}{9}+\frac{2(1-\mathrm{n})}{9} \\ & \frac{2}{\mathrm{~h}}=\frac{3.2-2 \mathrm{n}}{9} \\ & \mathrm{~h}=\frac{9}{1.6-\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{cm} \end{aligned}$$

(A) for $$\mathrm{n}=1.42, \mathrm{~h}=50 \mathrm{~cm}$$

(B) for $$\mathrm{n}=1.35, \mathrm{~h}=36 \mathrm{~cm}$$

(C) for $$\mathrm{n}=1.45, \mathrm{~h}=60 \mathrm{~cm}$$

(D) for $$\mathrm{n}=1.48, \mathrm{~h}=75 \mathrm{~cm}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: Two equilateral-triangular prisms $\mathrm{P}_1$ and $\mathrm{P}_2$ are kept with their sides parallel to each other, in vacuum, as shown in the figure. A light ray enters prism $\mathrm{P}_1$ at an angle of incidence $\theta$ such that the outgoing ray undergoes minimum deviation in prism $\mathrm{P}_2$. If the respective refractive indices of $\mathrm{P}_1$ and $\mathrm{P}_2$ are $\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}}$ and $\sqrt{3}$, then $\theta=\sin ^{-1}\left[\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}} \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{\beta}\right)\right]$, where the value of $\beta$ is ____.
questions.solution:
At surface BC

$$\begin{aligned} & \sqrt{\frac{3}{2}} \sin r_2=\sqrt{3} \sin 30 \\ & \sqrt{\frac{3}{2}} \sin r_2=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \\ & \operatorname{sinr}_2=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \\ & r_2=45^{\circ} \\ & r_1=60^{\circ}-45^{\circ}=15^{\circ} \end{aligned}$$

At surface $$A B$$

$$\begin{gathered} 1 \sin \theta=\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}} \sin 15^{\circ} \\ \theta=\sin ^{-1}\left[\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}} \sin \frac{\pi}{12}\right] \\ \beta=12 \end{gathered}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Choose the sentence that means the same as the given sentence. I woke up early lest I should be late for office.
options: I got late for office as I didn't wake up early.
In order to avoid getting late for office, I woke up early.
I could get late for office so I tried to wake up early.
Getting late for office can be avoided by waking up early.
questions.solution:
The sentence "I woke up early lest I should be late for office" means that I woke up early specifically to avoid being late. Let's review the options:

Option A: "I got late for office as I didn't wake up early."

This suggests the opposite: not waking up early resulted in being late.

Option B: "In order to avoid getting late for office, I woke up early."

This conveys the same meaning as the original sentence by stating that the early wake-up was to prevent being late.

Option C: "I could get late for office so I tried to wake up early."

While similar, this option implies an attempt rather than actually waking up early as a preventive measure.

Option D: "Getting late for office can be avoided by waking up early."

This is a general statement about avoiding lateness, rather than a description of what actually happened.

Given these explanations, the sentence that means the same as the original is:

Option B

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. It is your duty to help the poor people in your parish.
options: The poor people in your parish are to be helped by you.
It is suggested. that you do your duty to help the poor in your parish.
The poor people in the parish are helped by you as duty.
You may be allowed to help the poor people in your parish.
questions.solution:
The task is to convert the sentence "It is your duty to help the poor people in your parish." into a passive construction without changing its meaning. Let's look at the options:

Option A: "The poor people in your parish are to be helped by you."

This version clearly transforms the original active statement into passive voice. It maintains the obligation (duty) by using "are to be helped" and correctly identifies the agent "by you." This is the best match.

Option B: "It is suggested. that you do your duty to help the poor in your parish."

This sentence introduces the idea of suggestion, which alters the original tone of obligation.

Option C: "The poor people in the parish are helped by you as duty."

This option is grammatically awkward and does not maintain the intended meaning clearly.

Option D: "You may be allowed to help the poor people in your parish."

This version implies permission rather than an obligation or duty.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option A: The poor people in your parish are to be helped by you.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: A body which is initially at rest at a height $ R $ above the surface of the Earth of radius $ R $, falls freely towards the Earth, then its velocity on reaching the surface of the Earth is
questions.solution:
To determine the velocity of a body falling from rest at a height $ R $ above the Earth's surface, we must equate the increase in kinetic energy to the decrease in potential energy.

Given:

Initial height $ h = R $

Radius of Earth $ R $

Acceleration due to gravity $ g $

The change in potential energy when the body falls to the Earth's surface can be given by:

$ \Delta U = \frac{mgh}{1 + \frac{h}{R}} $

Substituting $ h = R $, we get:

$ \Delta U = \frac{mgR}{1 + \frac{R}{R}} = \frac{mgR}{2} $

By the conservation of energy principle, the increase in kinetic energy equals the decrease in potential energy:

$ \frac{1}{2} mv^2 = \frac{mgR}{2} $

Solving for $ v $:

$ mv^2 = mgR $

$ v^2 = gR $

$ v = \sqrt{gR} $

Thus, the velocity of the body when it reaches the Earth's surface is $ v = \sqrt{gR} $.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
A particle of mass $m$ is under the influence of the gravitational field of a body of mass $M(\gg m)$. The particle is moving in a circular orbit of radius $r_0$ with time period $T_0$ around the mass $M$. Then, the particle is subjected to an additional central force, corresponding to the potential energy $V_{\mathrm{c}}(r)=m \alpha / r^3$, where $\alpha$ is a positive constant of suitable dimensions and $r$ is the distance from the center of the orbit. If the particle moves in the same circular orbit of radius $r_0$ in the combined gravitational potential due to $M$ and $V_{\mathrm{c}}(r)$, but with a new time period $T_1$, then $\left(T_1^2-T_0^2\right) / T_1^2$ is given by

[G is the gravitational constant.]
questions.solution:
To find the effect of an additional central force on the time period of a particle in a circular orbit, consider the following:

Initial Gravitational Force ($F_1$):

The force due to gravity for a particle in a circular orbit of radius $r_0$ is given by:

$ F_1 = \frac{GMm}{r_0^2} $

where $ G $ is the gravitational constant, $ M $ is the mass of the central body, and $ m $ is the mass of the particle.

Modified Force with Additional Potential ($F_2$):

When an additional force derived from the potential energy $ V_{\mathrm{c}}(r) = \frac{m\alpha}{r^3} $ acts on the particle, the effective central force becomes:

$ F_2 = \frac{GMm}{r_0^2} - \frac{3m\alpha}{r_0^4} $

Relation of Angular Velocities:

The ratio of squares of the angular velocities ($\omega_0$ and $\omega_1$) before and after the additional force is:

$ \frac{\omega_1^2}{\omega_0^2} = \frac{F_2}{F_1} = \frac{\frac{GM}{r_0^2} - \frac{3\alpha}{r_0^4}}{\frac{GM}{r_0^2}} $

Relating Time Periods:

Since the angular velocity is inversely proportional to the time period, $\frac{\omega_1^2}{\omega_0^2} = \frac{T_0^2}{T_1^2}$. Thus, we have:

$ \frac{T_0^2}{T_1^2} = 1 - \frac{3\alpha}{GMr_0^2} $

Find the Change in Time Period:

The expression for the change in the square of the time periods is:

$ \frac{T_1^2 - T_0^2}{T_1^2} = \frac{3\alpha}{GMr_0^2} $

This shows how the additional central force, represented by the potential $ V_{\mathrm{c}}(r) = \frac{m\alpha}{r^3} $, affects the particle’s orbital period when combined with the gravitational field of the mass $ M $.

$ R-\mathrm{Br}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-} \xrightarrow[\mathrm{BMF}]{ } R-\mathrm{Cl}+\mathrm{Br}^{-} $

questions.title: CBSE 12th Chemistry Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
Account for the following:

(a) Haloalkanes react with AgCN to form isocyanide as main product.

(b) Allyl chloride shows high reactivity towards $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction.

(c) Haloarenes are extremely less reactive towards nucleophilic substitution reactions.
questions.solution:
(a) Cyanide ion is an ambident ion and it reacts with alkyl halides to form isocyanides. AgCN is covalent and replaces the halogen via a $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ mechanism, when the lone pair of nitrogen in AgCN is attached to the carbon bearing the halogen yielding isocyanide.

(b) Allyl chloride undergoes heterolysis to form a carbocation. This carbocation is resonance stabilised and is stable. Hence $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ mechanism that involves the formation of a carbocation in the transition state is favoured.

(c) Halogens are highly electronegative and draws the delocalized pi cloud of the benzene ring towards itself, which makes the ring electron deficient and do not undergo electrophilic attack.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: In a mixture of gases, the average number of degree of freedoms per molecule is 6 . The rms speed of the molecule of the gas is $ c $, then the velocity of sound in the gas is
questions.solution:
In a mixture of gases where the average number of degrees of freedom per molecule is 6, the velocity of sound in the gas can be determined using the following relationships:

The root mean square (rms) speed $ v_{\mathrm{rms}} $ is given by:

$ v_{\mathrm{rms}} = \sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M}} $

The velocity of sound $ v_{\text{sound}} $ is given by:

$ v_{\text{sound}} = \sqrt{\frac{\gamma R T}{M}} $

The ratio of the velocity of sound to the rms speed is:

$ \frac{v_{\text{sound}}}{v_{\mathrm{rms}}} = \sqrt{\frac{\gamma}{3}} $

Given that the degrees of freedom $ f = 6 $, we calculate the adiabatic index $ \gamma $ as follows:

$ \gamma = 1 + \frac{2}{f} = 1 + \frac{2}{6} = \frac{4}{3} $

Substituting $ \gamma = \frac{4}{3} $ into the equation for the velocity of sound:

$ v_{\text{sound}} = \sqrt{\frac{4/3}{3}} \, v_{\mathrm{rms}} = \frac{2}{3} v_{\mathrm{rms}} $

Therefore, the velocity of sound in the gas is:

$ \frac{2c}{3} $

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: In the given cycle $ A B C D A $, the heat required for an ideal monoatomic gas will be
questions.solution:
In the cycle $ A \rightarrow B \rightarrow C \rightarrow D \rightarrow A $, heat is added to the system during the processes $ D \rightarrow A $ (at constant volume) and $ A \rightarrow B $ (at constant pressure).

Calculating the Heat Added

Heat for Process $ D \rightarrow A $ (Constant Volume):

For an ideal monoatomic gas, the molar heat capacity at constant volume $ C_V $ is given by:

$ C_V = \frac{3}{2} R $

The heat added during this process is:

$ Q_{DA} = n C_V (\Delta T)_{DA} = \frac{3}{2} n R \Delta T $

Heat for Process $ A \rightarrow B $ (Constant Pressure):

The molar heat capacity at constant pressure $ C_p $ is given by:

$ C_p = \frac{5}{2} R $

The heat added during this process is:

$ Q_{AB} = n C_p (\Delta T)_{AB} = \frac{5}{2} n R \Delta T $

Total Heat Supplied

The total heat supplied in the cycle is the sum of the heat added during these two processes:

$ Q = Q_{DA} + Q_{AB} $

Substitute the expressions for $ Q_{DA} $ and $ Q_{AB} $:

$ Q = \frac{3}{2} n R \Delta T + \frac{5}{2} n R \Delta T $

Given $ n R \Delta T = p \Delta V $, and substituting it:

$ Q = \frac{3}{2}(p_0 V_0) + \frac{5}{2}(p_0 V_0) $

This simplifies to:

$ Q = \frac{13}{2} p_0 V_0 $

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
One mole of a monatomic ideal gas undergoes the cyclic process $\mathrm{J} \rightarrow \mathrm{K} \rightarrow \mathrm{L} \rightarrow \mathrm{M} \rightarrow \mathrm{J}$, as shown in the P-T diagram.

Match the quantities mentioned in List-I with their values in List-II and choose the correct option.

[ $\mathcal{R}$ is the gas constant.]

List-I List-II
(P) Work done in the complete cyclic process (1) $RT_0 - 4RT_0 \ln 2$
(Q) Change in the internal energy of the gas in the process JK (2) $0$
(R) Heat given to the gas in the process KL (3) $3RT_0$
(S) Change in the internal energy of the gas in the process MJ (4) $-2RT_0 \ln 2$
(5) $-3RT_0 \ln 2$
questions.solution: $$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{J}\left(\mathrm{P}_0, \mathrm{~V}_0, \mathrm{~T}_0\right) \\ & K\left(P_0, 3 V_0, 3 T_0\right) \\ & \mathrm{M}\left(2 \mathrm{P}_0, \frac{\mathrm{V}_0}{2}, \mathrm{~T}_0\right) \\ & \mathrm{L}\left(2 \mathrm{P}_{\circ}, \frac{3 \mathrm{~V}_0}{2}, 3 \mathrm{~T}_0\right) \\ & \mathrm{P}_0 \mathrm{~V}_0=\mathrm{nRT}_0 \\ & \mathrm{JK} \rightarrow \text { isobaric } \Rightarrow \mathrm{W}=\mathrm{P}_0\left(2 \mathrm{~V}_0\right)=2 \mathrm{nRT}_0 \\ & \Delta \mathrm{U}=\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{nR}\left(2 \mathrm{~T}_0\right)=3 \mathrm{nRT}_0 \\ & \mathrm{KL} \rightarrow \text { isothermal } \rightarrow \mathrm{W}=\mathrm{nR}(3 \mathrm{~T}) \ell \mathrm{n}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)=-3 \mathrm{nRT}_0 \ell \mathrm{n} 2 \\ & \Delta \mathrm{U}=0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{Q}=-3 \mathrm{nRT}_0 \ell \mathrm{n} 2 \\ & \mathrm{LM} \rightarrow \text { isobaric }=2 \mathrm{P}_0\left(-\mathrm{V}_0\right)=-2 \mathrm{nRT}_0 \\ & \mathrm{MJ} \rightarrow \text { isothermal } \Rightarrow \mathrm{nRT}_0 \ln 2 ; \Delta \mathrm{U}=0 \\ & \mathrm{WD}_{\text {nes }}=-2 \mathrm{nRT}_0 \ln 2 \\ & \mathrm{P} \rightarrow 4, \mathrm{Q} \rightarrow 3, \mathrm{R} \rightarrow 5, \mathrm{~S} \rightarrow 2 \\ & \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
The specific heat capacity of a substance is temperature dependent and is given by the formula $C=k T$, where $k$ is a constant of suitable dimensions in SI units, and $T$ is the absolute temperature. If the heat required to raise the temperature of $1 \mathrm{~kg}$ of the substance from $-73^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is $n k$, the value of $n$ is ________.

[Given: $0 \mathrm{~K}=-273{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.]
questions.solution:
To solve this problem, we need to integrate the heat capacity over the given temperature range because the specific heat capacity is temperature dependent. We are given that the specific heat capacity $$C$$ is defined as $$C = kT$$, where $$k$$ is a constant, and $$T$$ is the absolute temperature.

The heat required to raise the temperature, $$Q$$, can be found using the following integral:

$$ Q = \int_{T_1}^{T_2} C \, dT $$

Given $$C = kT$$, the integral becomes:

$$ Q = \int_{T_1}^{T_2} kT \, dT $$

We need to convert the given temperatures from Celsius to Kelvin. The temperatures given are:

  • Initial temperature: $$-73^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$
  • Final temperature: $$27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$

In Kelvin, these temperatures are:

  • $$T_1 = -73^{\circ} \mathrm{C} + 273 = 200 \, \mathrm{K}$$
  • $$T_2 = 27^{\circ} \mathrm{C} + 273 = 300 \, \mathrm{K}$$

Now we can evaluate the integral:

$$ Q = k \int_{200}^{300} T \, dT $$

Integrating, we get:

$$ Q = k \left[ \frac{T^2}{2} \right]_{200}^{300} $$

Substituting the limits of integration:

$$ Q = k \left[ \frac{300^2}{2} - \frac{200^2}{2} \right] $$

Solving the values inside the brackets:

$$ Q = k \left[ \frac{90000}{2} - \frac{40000}{2} \right] $$

$$ Q = k \left[ 45000 - 20000 \right] $$

$$ Q = k \cdot 25000 $$

We are given that this heat is equal to $$nk$$:

$$ nk = k \cdot 25000 $$

Dividing both sides by $$k$$, we get:

$$ n = 25000 $$

Thus, the value of $$n$$ is 25000.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: A spherical soap bubble inside an air chamber at pressure $P_0=10^5 \mathrm{~Pa}$ has a certain radius so that the excess pressure inside the bubble is $\Delta P=144 \mathrm{~Pa}$. Now, the chamber pressure is reduced to $8 P_0 / 27$ so that the bubble radius and its excess pressure change. In this process, all the temperatures remain unchanged. Assume air to be an ideal gas and the excess pressure $\Delta P$ in both the cases to be much smaller than the chamber pressure. The new excess pressure $\Delta P$ in $\mathrm{Pa}$ is ______.
questions.solution:
Case - 1

$$\begin{aligned} & P-P_0=\Delta P=\frac{4 T}{R} \\ & P=\left(P_0+\frac{4 T}{R}\right) \end{aligned}$$

Case-2

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{P}_1-\frac{8 \mathrm{P}_0}{27}=\Delta \mathrm{P}_1=\frac{4 \mathrm{~T}}{\mathrm{R}_1} \\ & \mathrm{P}_1=\frac{4 \mathrm{~T}}{\mathrm{R}_1}+\frac{8 \mathrm{P}_0}{27} \end{aligned}$$

Constant temperature process

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{PV}=\mathrm{P}_1 \mathrm{~V}_1 \\ & \left(\mathrm{P}_0+\frac{4 \mathrm{~T}}{\mathrm{R}}\right) \frac{4}{3} \pi \mathrm{R}^3=\left(\frac{4 \mathrm{~T}}{\mathrm{R}_1}+\frac{8 \mathrm{P}_0}{27}\right) \frac{4}{3} \pi \mathrm{R}_1^3 ;\left(\frac{4 \mathrm{~T}}{\mathrm{R}}\right),\left(\frac{4 \mathrm{~T}}{\mathrm{R}_1}\right) \rightarrow \text { (Neglected) } \\ & \mathrm{R}=\frac{2}{3} \mathrm{R}_1 \Rightarrow \mathrm{R}_1=\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{R} \\ & \Delta \mathrm{P}_1=\frac{4 \mathrm{~T}}{\mathrm{R}_1}=\frac{4 \mathrm{~T}}{3 \mathrm{R}} \times 2=\frac{2}{3} \times(144)=96 \mathrm{~Pa} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Taxila was the capital of which one among the following Mahajanapadas of ancient India?
questions.solution:
Taxila was the capital of Gandhar.

Here's a breakdown of the capitals for the given Mahajanapadas:

  1. Kuru: The capital of Kuru Mahajanapada was Indraprastha.

  2. Kasi: The capital of Kasi Mahajanapada was Varanasi (also known as Kasi).

  3. Gandhar: The capital of Gandhar Mahajanapada was Taxila (also known as Takshashila). This region was strategically located on the northwestern frontier and was an important center for trade and learning.

  4. Avanti: The capital of Avanti Mahajanapada was Ujjain (or Mahishmati).

Therefore, Taxila was the capital of Gandhar.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Who among the following was the last Governor-General of India before the office was permanently abolished?
questions.solution:
1. The office of Governor-General of India existed both under British rule and for a short period after India’s independence on 15 August 1947.

  2. After independence, the first Governor-General was Lord Mountbatten (15 August 1947 to 21 June 1948).

  3. He was succeeded by Chakravarti Rajagopalachari, who served from 21 June 1948 until 26 January 1950.

  4. On 26 January 1950, India became a republic and the office of Governor-General was abolished, being replaced by the President of India.

Answer: Chakravarti Rajagopalachari (Option 2)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one of the following statements about the Industrial Revolution is correct?
options: James Watt's invention converted the steam engine from being a mere pump into one which would provide energy to power machines in factories
Mathew Boulton discovered the technique of refining gold of impurities
questions.solution:
The correct choice is Option 3.

James Watt’s key innovation (the separate condenser and improvements to generate rotary motion) transformed the steam engine from a simple pump into a prime mover capable of powering factory machinery.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->The first cotton mill in British India which began production in 1856 was set up by
questions.solution:
The first cotton mill in British India, which began production in 1856, was set up by C. N. Davar.

He established the "Bombay Spinning and Weaving Company" in 1854, and it commenced production two years later, marking a significant milestone in India's industrial history.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Inspired by the Dandi March by Mahatma Gandhi, in Tamilnadu, C Rajagopalachari led a salt march from
options: Trichinopoly to Vedaranniyam on Tanjore coast
questions.solution:
Inspired by Mahatma Gandhi's Dandi March, C. Rajagopalachari led a similar salt march in Tamil Nadu. The correct route for his march was:

Option 3: Trichinopoly to Vedaranniyam on Tanjore coast

This historic march is often referred to as the Vedaranyam Salt Satyagraha. It began from Trichinopoly (modern-day Tiruchirappalli) on April 13, 1930, and concluded at Vedaranyam, a coastal town in the Tanjore (now Thanjavur) district, where participants symbolically broke the salt laws by making salt from seawater.

questions.solution:
