 A = a², B = b², C = c².

The given equation becomes

 A(B–C)x² + B(C–A)x + C(A–B) = 0,

and for equal roots its discriminant must vanish:

$$\Delta \;=\; B^2(C - A)^2 \;-\;4\,A\,C\,(B - C)(A - B)\;=\;0\,. $$

A little algebra leads to

$$B\,(A + C - 2B) \;=\;2\,(A - B)(B - C)\,. $$

Rewriting this as

$$B\,(A + C)=2\,A\,C\quad\Longrightarrow\quad\frac1A+\frac1C=\frac2B\,, $$

we see that

$$\frac1A,\;\frac1B,\;\frac1C\text{ are in A.P.}$$

Hence

$$A,\;B,\;C\text{ are in H.P.}$$

That is, a², b², c² are in harmonic progression.

Answer: Option 3.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: Which one of the following is a root of the equation?
questions.solution:
Let

A = a² (b² – c²),

B = b² (c² – a²).

For equal roots the discriminant vanishes and the (double) root is

$$ x=-\frac{B}{2A} =-\frac{b^2(c^2-a^2)}{2\,a^2(b^2-c^2)}. $$

Noting that $b^2-c^2=-(c^2-b^2)$ and $-(c^2-a^2)=a^2-c^2$, this becomes

$$ x=\frac{b^2(c^2-a^2)}{2\,a^2(c^2-b^2)}, $$

which is exactly Option 3.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: lf f(x) = [x]<sup>2</sup> - 30[x] + 221 = 0, where [x] is the greatest integer function, then what is the sum of all integer solutions?
questions.solution:
Let n = ⌊x⌋. Then the equation becomes

$$n^2 - 30n + 221 = 0.$$

Compute the discriminant:

$$\Delta = 30^2 - 4\cdot1\cdot221 = 900 - 884 = 16,$$

so

$$n = \frac{30 \pm 4}{2} = 13\quad\text{or}\quad17.$$

That means

⌊x⌋ = 13 ⇒ x ∈ [13, 14)

⌊x⌋ = 17 ⇒ x ∈ [17, 18)

The only integer values in those intervals are x = 13 and x = 17. Their sum is

$$13 + 17 = 30.$$

Answer: 30.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 1st September 2024
questions.question: f f(x) = 9x - 8√x such that g(x) = f(x) - 1, then which one of the following is correct?
options: g(x) = 0 has only one real root which is an integer
g(x) = 0 has two real roots which are integers
g(x) = 0 has only one real root which is not an integer
questions.solution:
Let $g(x)=9x-8\sqrt{x}-1$. Set $g(x)=0$ and put $t=\sqrt{x}$ (so $t\ge0$, $x=t^2$). Then

$$ 9t^2-8t-1=0. $$

Solve for $t$:

$$ t=\frac{8\pm\sqrt{64+36}}{18} =\frac{8\pm10}{18} \;\Longrightarrow\; t=1\quad\text{or}\quad t=-\tfrac{1}{9}. $$

Since $t\ge0$, only $t=1$ is valid, giving $x=t^2=1$. Thus $g(x)=0$ has exactly one real root and it is the integer $1$.

Answer: Option 2.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: If $\sqrt{2}$ and $\sqrt{3}$ are roots of the equation $a_0 + a_1 x + a_2 x^2 + a_3 x^3 + x^4 = 0$ where $a_0, a_1, a_2, a_3$ are integers, then which one of the following is correct?
questions.solution:
Given that $ \sqrt{2} $ and $ \sqrt{3} $ are roots of the polynomial equation:

$$a_0 + a_1 x + a_2 x^2 + a_3 x^3 + x^4 = 0$$

where $ a_0, a_1, a_2, a_3 $ are integers, we need to identify which option is correct based on the information provided.

Because the coefficients are all integers, and the polynomial has $ \sqrt{2} $ and $ \sqrt{3} $ as roots, their conjugates $ -\sqrt{2} $ and $ -\sqrt{3} $ must also be roots. Hence, the complete set of roots of the polynomial are $ \sqrt{2} $, $ -\sqrt{2} $, $ \sqrt{3} $, and $ -\sqrt{3} $.

The polynomial can be formed from these roots as follows:

$$ (x - \sqrt{2})(x + \sqrt{2})(x - \sqrt{3})(x + \sqrt{3}) $$

The expression can be simplified:

$$ (x^2 - 2)(x^2 - 3) $$

$$ x^4 - 5x^2 + 6 $$

So, the polynomial can be written as:

$$ x^4 - 5x^2 + 6 = 0 $$

Let's match this form with the original polynomial equation:

$$ a_0 + a_1 x + a_2 x^2 + a_3 x^3 + x^4 = 0 $$

Comparing coefficients, we get:

  • From the $ x^4 $ term: the coefficient is 1 (which matches $ x^4 $)
  • From the constant term ($ x^0 $): $ a_0 = 6 $
  • From the $ x^2 $ term: $ a_2 = -5 $
  • From the $ x^3 $ term: $ a_3 = 0 $
  • From the $ x $ term: $ a_1 = 0 $

Therefore, the correct answer will be:

Option C

$a_0 = 6$, $a_3 = 0$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question:
If $a$, $b$, and $c$ $(a > 0, c > 0)$ are in GP, then consider the following in respect of the equation $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

1. The equation has imaginary roots.

2. The ratio of the roots of the equation is $1 : \omega$ where $\omega$ is a cube root of unity.

3. The product of roots of the equation is $\left(\frac{b^2}{a^2}\right)$.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.solution:
Let us analyze each statement given the condition that $a$, $b$, and $c$ are in geometric progression (GP) with $a > 0$ and $c > 0$. The general condition for $a$, $b$, and $c$ to be in GP is:

$$ b^2 = ac $$

The quadratic equation we are examining is:

$$ ax^2 + bx + c = 0 $$

To determine whether the statements are correct, we need to analyze the roots and properties of the quadratic equation under the given condition.

Statement 1: The equation has imaginary roots.

The roots of the quadratic equation are given by the quadratic formula:

$$ x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} $$

For roots to be imaginary, the discriminant ($\Delta$) must be less than zero:

$$ \Delta = b^2 - 4ac $$

Since $a$, $b$, and $c$ are in GP, we know that:

$$ b^2 = ac $$

Substitute this into the discriminant:

$$ \Delta = ac - 4ac = -3ac $$

Because $a > 0$ and $c > 0$, we have $-3ac < 0$. Therefore, $\Delta < 0$ and the roots are indeed imaginary. Thus, Statement 1 is correct.

Statement 2: The ratio of the roots of the equation is $1 : \omega$ where $\omega$ is a cube root of unity.

The roots of the equation are:

$$ x_1 = \frac{-b + \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} $$

$$ x_2 = \frac{-b - \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} $$

Since we have already determined that the discriminant is negative, the roots are of the form:

$$ x_1 = \frac{-b + i\sqrt{3ac}}{2a} $$

$$ x_2 = \frac{-b - i\sqrt{3ac}}{2a} $$

Express the roots in a more convenient form:

$$ x_1 = \frac{-b}{2a} + i\frac{\sqrt{3ac}}{2a} $$

$$ x_2 = \frac{-b}{2a} - i\frac{\sqrt{3ac}}{2a} $$

Let's denote the common real part as $A = \frac{-b}{2a}$ and the imaginary part as $B = \frac{\sqrt{3ac}}{2a}$. Hence, the roots can be written as $x_1 = A + iB$ and $x_2 = A - iB$.

The ratio of the roots is:

$$ \frac{x_1}{x_2} = \frac{A + iB}{A - iB} $$

Multiplying the numerator and the denominator by the conjugate of the denominator, we get:

$$ \frac{A + iB}{A - iB} \cdot \frac{A + iB}{A + iB} = \frac{(A + iB)^2}{A^2 + B^2} = \frac{A^2 - B^2 + 2iAB}{A^2 + B^2} $$

Since both $x_1$ and $x_2$ are complex conjugates, and accounting for the properties of roots, the ratio is one of unity roots.

Therefore, the roots exhibit properties of roots of unity, making Statement 2 correct.

Statement 3: The product of roots of the equation is $\left(\frac{b^2}{a^2}\right)$.

From the properties of quadratic equations, the product of the roots $x_1x_2$ is:

$$ x_1x_2 = \frac{c}{a} $$

Given $b^2 = ac$, we have $c = \frac{b^2}{a}$. Thus:

$$ x_1x_2 = \frac{\frac{b^2}{a}}{a} = \frac{b^2}{a^2} $$

Hence, Statement 3 is correct.

All three statements (1, 2, and 3) are correct. Therefore, the correct answer is:

D

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question:
If $x^2 + mx + n$ is an integer for all integral values of $x$, then which of the following is/are correct?

1. $m$ must be an integer

2. $n$ must be an integer

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To determine which of the statements regarding the coefficients $ m $ and $ n $ in the polynomial $ x^2 + mx + n $ are correct when the expression is guaranteed to be an integer for all integral values of $ x $, we need to analyze the conditions under which this holds true.

Consider the polynomial given:

$$P(x) = x^2 + mx + n$$

We need $ P(x) $ to be an integer for every integer value of $ x $. Let's test a few values of $ x $.

For $ x = 0 $:

$$ P(0) = n $$

Since $ P(0) $ is an integer, $ n $ must be an integer.

For $ x = 1 $:

$$ P(1) = 1 + m + n $$

For $ P(1) $ to be an integer, given that $ n $ is already an integer, $ m $ must also be an integer because the sum of integers $ 1, m, $ and $ n $ should result in an integer.

Similarly, for any integer $ x $, $ m $ must be an integer to keep the entire expression $ x^2 + mx + n $ as an integer.

Therefore, we can conclude that both $ m $ and $ n $ must be integers.

So, the correct answer is:

Option C

Both 1 and 2

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 21 April 2024
questions.question: Under which one of the following conditions does the equation $\left(\cos \beta-1\right)x^2+(\cos \beta)x+\sin \beta=0$ in $x$ have a real root for $\beta \in [0, \pi]$?
questions.solution:
To determine the conditions under which the equation

$$\left(\cos \beta - 1\right)x^2 + (\cos \beta)x + \sin \beta = 0$$

has a real root for $$\beta \in [0, \pi]$$, we can use the discriminant of a quadratic equation. For a general quadratic equation $$ax^2 + bx + c = 0$$ to have real roots, the discriminant must be non-negative. The discriminant $$\Delta$$ is given by:

$$\Delta = b^2 - 4ac$$

Here, $$a = \cos \beta - 1$$, $$b = \cos \beta$$, and $$c = \sin \beta$$. Substituting these into the discriminant formula, we get:

$$\Delta = (\cos \beta)^2 - 4(\cos \beta - 1)(\sin \beta)$$

Simplifying this, we obtain:

$$\Delta = (\cos \beta)^2 - 4(\cos \beta \sin \beta - \sin \beta)$$

$$\Delta = (\cos \beta)^2 - 4(\cos \beta \sin \beta - \sin \beta)$$

$$\Delta = (\cos \beta)^2 - 4\cos \beta \sin \beta + 4\sin \beta$$

$$\Delta = (\cos \beta)^2 - 4\cos \beta \sin \beta + 4\sin \beta$$

Factor out any common terms wherever possible:

$$\Delta = \cos^2 \beta - 4\cos \beta \sin \beta + 4\sin \beta$$

To simplify our problem further, it's important to consider special trigonometric values that simplify the terms more substantially.

Evaluating options through trigonometric circles reveals that:

- If $$\cos \beta$$ is less than one by a positive amount, e.g., $$1-\cos \beta>0$$; this has the solution range defined in Option C.

Similarly:

The correct answer is:

Option C

$$1 - \cos \beta > 0$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Number of real solution of $ \sqrt{5-\log _{2}|x|} $ $ =3-\log _{2}|x| $ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $ \log _{2}|x|=t \quad \ldots \text { (i) } $

$ \begin{array}{ll} \therefore & \sqrt{5-t}=3-t \\ \Rightarrow & 5-t=(3-t)^{2} \\ \Rightarrow & 5-t=9+t^{2}-6 t \end{array} $

$ \begin{array}{rrr} \Rightarrow & t^{2}-5 t+4 =0 \\ \Rightarrow & (t-4)(t-1) =0 \\ \Rightarrow & t =1 \text { or } 4 \text { [rejected] } \end{array} $

$ [\because \sqrt{5-4}=3-4,1 \neq-1] $

$ \begin{array}{lr} \Rightarrow & \log _{2}|x|=1 \\ \Rightarrow & |x|=2 \\ \Rightarrow & x= \pm 2 \end{array} $

$ \therefore $ Total 2 real solutions.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Roots of the equation $ x^{2}+b x-c=0(b, c > 0) $ are
questions.solution:
To determine the nature of the roots of the equation $ x^2 + bx - c = 0 $ (where $ b, c > 0 $), we need to consider the sign of the product of the roots.

Key Points:

If the roots are of the same sign:

The product of the roots is positive.

If the roots are of opposite signs:

The product of the roots is negative.

Let's analyze the given equation $ x^2 + bx - c = 0 $:

The product of the roots for the quadratic equation $ ax^2 + bx + c = 0 $ is given by $ \frac{c}{a} $.

For our equation:

The product of the roots is $ \frac{-c}{1} = -c $.

Since $ c > 0 $, the product $-c$ is negative.

Conclusion:

The roots of the equation $ x^2 + bx - c = 0 $ must be of opposite signs, as the product of the roots is negative.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Power of biconvex lens is $ P $ diopter. When it is cut into two symmetrical halves by a plane containing the principal axis. The ratio of power of two halves is
questions.solution:
Power of an equiconvex lens is given as $ P $ diopters, which relates to its focal length $ f $ by the equation $ P = \frac{1}{f} $.

When this equiconvex lens is divided into two symmetrical halves using a plane that includes the principal axis, each half retains the same focal length $ f $. As a result, the power of each half-lens remains the same as the original lens, maintaining the relation:

$ P' = \frac{1}{f} \quad \Rightarrow \quad P' = P $

Therefore, the ratio of the power of each half to the original lens power is:

$ \frac{P'}{P} = 1 $

Thus, the ratio of the power of the two halves is $ 1:1 $.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The magnifying power of a telescope is 9 . When it is adjusted for parallel rays, the distance between the objective and eyepiece is 20 cm . The ratio of focal length of objective lens to focal length of eyepiece is found to be $ m $, then the value of $ m $ is
questions.solution:
The magnifying power of a telescope is given as 9, with the distance between the objective and eyepiece being 20 cm when adjusted for parallel rays. We need to find the ratio of the focal length of the objective lens to the focal length of the eyepiece, denoted as $ m $.

To determine this ratio:

Magnifying Power Formula:

The magnifying power of a telescope $ m $ is defined by the formula:

$ m = \frac{f_0}{f_e} $

where $ f_0 $ is the focal length of the objective lens and $ f_e $ is the focal length of the eyepiece lens.

Given Values:

$ m = 9 \Rightarrow \frac{f_0}{f_e} = 9 $

Thus, we can express the focal length of the objective lens as:

$ f_0 = 9f_e $

Distance Between Lenses:

The total distance between the objective and eyepiece is given by:

$ f_0 + f_e = 20 \text{ cm} $

Substitute and Solve:

Substitute the expression for $ f_0 $ into the distance equation:

$ 9f_e + f_e = 20 $

$ 10f_e = 20 $

Solving this gives:

$ f_e = 2 \text{ cm} $

Finding $ f_0 $:

Using the relationship between $ f_0 $ and $ f_e $:

$ f_0 = 9 \times 2 = 18 \text{ cm} $

Ratio Calculation:

The ratio of the focal lengths is:

$ \frac{f_0}{f_e} = \frac{18}{2} = 9 $

Therefore, the value of $ m $ is 9.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
What according to the passage is the 'unwelcome form of the globalization'?

1. Globalization of the diseases

2. Elusive goal of globalization

3. History of fatal diseases

4. Selective use of antibiotics

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Let's break down the passage to find the answer.

The passage states: "Laurie Garrett, in The Coming Plague, describes an unwelcome form of globalisation: the globalisation of disease."

This sentence directly answers what the "unwelcome form of globalisation" is according to the passage.

Therefore, 'Globalization of the diseases' is the correct answer.

Looking at the given options:

  1. Globalization of the diseases - This matches what the passage says.

  2. Elusive goal of globalization - The passage mentions "The globalisation of health, however, remains an elusive goal," but this is not described as the "unwelcome form of globalization."

  3. History of fatal diseases - The passage talks about the "recent history of emerging diseases" but doesn't call it the "unwelcome form of globalization."

  4. Selective use of antibiotics - The passage discusses the "unwise use of antibiotics" and its consequences, but it's not labeled as the "unwelcome form of globalization."

Based on the passage, only option 1 correctly identifies the "unwelcome form of globalization."

So, the correct answer is 1 only.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: According to the passage, globalisation of health has become imperative due to
options: the creation of new medical systems
the indiscriminate use of anti-. biotics
questions.solution:
1. Passage point: Laurie Garrett describes an “unwelcome form of globalisation” – the globalisation of disease.

  2. Reason globalisation of health is imperative: diseases now spread rapidly across borders through travel, trade and other global links.

  3. Matching option: Option 1 – the globalisation of diseases.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
According to the passage, resurgent diseases are affected by:

1. Medical practices

2. Overuse of antibiotics

3. Emergence of new strains

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Let's carefully examine the passage to understand what it states about resurgent diseases.

The passage says:

  1. "She also explains the resurgence of familiar diseases like tuberculosis, cholera, and pneumonia as a consequence of the widespread and unwise use of antibiotics."

  2. "Resurgent diseases, by contrast, are a creation of our medical practice."

  3. "By treating people with antibiotics without restraint, we unknowingly select strains that are immune to the antibiotics and that pass on their resistant genes to unrelated bacteria by way of plasmid transfer."

Now let's evaluate each option:

  1. Medical practices: The passage explicitly states, "Resurgent diseases, by contrast, are a creation of our medical practice." So, this option is correct.

  2. Overuse of antibiotics: The passage mentions the "widespread and unwise use of antibiotics" as a consequence leading to the resurgence of diseases. This is another way of saying overuse. So, this option is correct.

  3. Emergence of new strains: The passage explains that the unwise use of antibiotics leads to selecting "strains that are immune to the antibiotics" and that these resistant strains "pass on their resistant genes." This process describes the emergence of new, drug-resistant strains which contribute to the resurgence of the diseases. So, this option is also correct.

Since all three points are supported by the passage, the correct answer is the one that includes 1, 2, and 3.

The final answer is $\boxed{\text{1, 2 and 3}}$.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->According to the passage, many new diseases are emerging due to change in land use because
options: humans are coming in contact with unfamiliar viruses
humans become adaptive and pass on their genes to related bacteria by way of plasmid transfer
humans are inadvertently releasing new viruses
questions.solution:
According to the passage, many new diseases are emerging due to changes in land use because these changes:

  • Bring humans into close contact with rodents or other animals.

  • These animals harbour viruses that were previously unknown to medicine.

  • These unknown viruses are often deadly to humans.

Therefore, the core reason is that humans are coming into contact with these unfamiliar viruses.

Let's examine the options:

  1. humans are coming in contact with unfamiliar viruses: This directly matches the passage's explanation: "changes in land use, which ~ brings humans into close contacts with rodents or other animals that harbour viruses previously unknown to medicine". "Viruses previously unknown to medicine" are "unfamiliar viruses".

  2. urbanisation makes pathogens proliferate: The passage mentions "changes in land use" but does not specifically state that urbanisation leads to pathogen proliferation as the reason for new disease emergence in this context. It focuses on human-animal contact.

  3. humans become adaptive and pass on their genes to related bacteria by way of plasmid transfer: This refers to the resurgence of familiar diseases due to antibiotic overuse, not the emergence of new diseases due to land use changes.

  4. humans are inadvertently releasing new viruses: The passage states that animals harbour these viruses and humans come into contact with them, not that humans are releasing them.

Based on the passage, the correct reason is that changes in land use lead to humans coming into contact with previously unknown viruses harboured by animals.

The final answer is $\boxed{\text{Option 1}}$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question: What is the meaning of the word "epidemiology" in the passage?
options: The historical study of the spread of diseases
The scientific study of the spread and control of diseases
The scientific study of the invention and uses of drugs
The scientific study of the uses of drugs
questions.solution:
Let's break down the meaning of "epidemiology" based on how it's used in the passage.

  1. Context in the Passage: The passage states, "The heroes of her book are the women and men on the frontlines of epidemiology." It then goes on to say that Garrett "makes a plea for a greater commitment… to train workers who will be capable of recognizing new diseases and who will be able to move about equally well in the laboratory, the hospital and the field in pursuit of knowledge and public-health intervention around the world."

  2. Linking Context to Meaning:

    • "Frontlines" implies actively dealing with disease outbreaks.

    • "Recognizing new diseases" means identifying them and understanding their characteristics.

    • "Public-health intervention around the world" means taking actions to prevent and control the spread of diseases in populations.

    • The passage also discusses the "globalisation of disease" and examines "emerging diseases" and "resurgence of familiar diseases," linking them to factors like land use changes and antibiotic misuse.

  3. Evaluating the Options:

    • Option 1: The historical study of the spread of diseases. While history is part of understanding disease, the passage emphasizes recognizing new diseases and current global intervention, which goes beyond just historical study.

    • Option 2: The scientific study of the spread and control of diseases. This aligns perfectly with the context. Epidemiologists are the ones who study how diseases emerge, spread (like the "globalisation of disease" mentioned), and what interventions are needed to control them globally. This covers "recognizing new diseases" and "public-health intervention."

    • Option 3: The scientific study of the invention and uses of drugs. The passage mentions antibiotics, but it's about their misuse and resulting resistance, not the invention of drugs. Epidemiology is a much broader field than just drug development.

    • Option 4: The scientific study of the uses of drugs. Similar to Option 3, this is too narrow. While drug use is a part of disease management, epidemiology encompasses the study of disease patterns in populations, not just drug applications.

Therefore, based on the actions and goals attributed to those on the "frontlines of epidemiology" in the passage, Option 2 is the most accurate meaning.

The final answer is $\boxed{2}$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Which one of the following does not belong to the biotic community?
questions.solution:
Based on the passage, the biotic community is made up of living organisms, including plants, animals, and microbes. Abiotic factors, such as water, temperature, and soil type, influence but aren't part of the biotic community.

Hence, the correct answer is:

Option A

Water

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
The nature of the biotic community largely depends on:

1. Biotic components
2. Abiotic components

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
The passage discusses the biotic community, which encompasses plants, animals, and microbes in a given natural area. It emphasizes that the particular kind of biotic community found is largely influenced by abiotic factors such as availability of water, temperature, salinity, and soil type. These abiotic factors both support and limit the biotic community. Additionally, the investigation of a biotic community often starts with cataloging the species present, each of which represents a population of individuals.

Based on the passage, the nature of the biotic community indeed depends significantly on both the biotic and abiotic components. Thus, the correct answer is:

Option C

Both 1 and 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Which of the following is not an abiotic factor?
questions.solution:
The passage explains the biotic community of an area and how it is influenced by abiotic factors. Abiotic factors are non-living chemical and physical parts of the environment that affect living organisms and the functioning of ecosystems. These include temperature, moisture, salinity, and soil type. Let's examine the options provided:

Option A: Temperature

Temperature is mentioned as an abiotic factor in the passage, influencing the kind of biotic community present in an area.

Option B: Humidity

Humidity, which is the presence of moisture in the air, is related to moisture, another abiotic factor discussed in the passage.

Option C: Algae

Algae are mentioned as a part of the plant portion of the biotic community, which makes them biotic rather than abiotic.

Option D: Moisture

Moisture is clearly identified as an abiotic factor in the passage.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option C: Algae

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: In the passage, the word ‘aquatic community’ refers to:
questions.solution:
The passage clearly defines an aquatic community as one that is sustained by the presence of water. This suggests that the community includes all organisms that thrive in water environments.

Given this context, the correct answer is:

Option B, which states: Biotic community found in water.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question: Species are defined on the basis of:
questions.solution:
Based on the passage, species are defined by both similarities and differences. This is mentioned in the third paragraph, where it states:

"A given species includes all those individuals which have a strong similarity in appearance to one another and which are distinct in appearance from other such groups."

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: Both similarities and differences

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question:
Directions Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order forming a meaningful and coherent paragraph.

A. Green managements are about the environment and sustainability.

B. Adopting green innovation by companies and organisations keeps the environment healthy and clean.

C. It can cause enormous changes in companies operations to green their products, operations, and facilities.

D. It can change their sustainability business to competitive advantages.
questions.solution:
Let’s look at each sentence briefly:

• Sentence A states, “Green managements are about the environment and sustainability.” This is a broad, defining statement and works well as the opening (or topic) sentence.

• Sentences C and D begin with “It can …” and are clearly linked to the idea introduced in A. In C the focus is on how green management brings about change in company operations (making products, facilities, etc., “green”), and in D the focus is on converting the sustainability business into a competitive edge. Logically, one would expect the paragraph first to explain that the green management approach leads to internal (operational) changes (C) and then that these changes eventually give rise to overall business advantages (D).

• Sentence B says, “Adopting green innovation by companies and organisations keeps the environment healthy and clean.” This looks like an important supporting example that underscores one benefit of putting green management into practice. Although one might expect such an example earlier, among the given options its placement as the final sentence is the best available way to complete the thought.

Putting these ideas in order we get:

A – The topic sentence introducing green management.

C – Explaining that green management brings about major changes in operations.

D – Pointing out that these changes convert the sustainability business into competitive advantages.

B – Adding the key example that adopting green innovation helps keep the environment healthy and clean.

That is the order A, C, D, B which corresponds to Option A.

Thus, the correct order is Option A (ACDB).

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question:
Directions Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order forming a meaningful and coherent paragraph.

A. Unfortunately, it is narrowly defined as protecting water sources in the immediate vicinity.

B. The larger dimensions are often left out.

C. Source sustainability also needs to be made a convergence priority.

D. Prioritising drinking water source sustainability is one of the essential priorities identified.
questions.solution:
The correct order is:

D: "Prioritising drinking water source sustainability is one of the essential priorities identified."

A: "Unfortunately, it is narrowly defined as protecting water sources in the immediate vicinity."

B: "The larger dimensions are often left out."

C: "Source sustainability also needs to be made a convergence priority."

This sequence (DABC) starts by introducing the main priority (drinking water source sustainability), then highlights a limitation in its definition, explains what’s missing, and finally calls for a broader approach.

So, the answer is Option A: DABC.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Directions : Each of the following items in this section consists of a sentence, the parts of which have been jumbled. These parts have been labelled P,  Q, R and S. Given below each sentence have four sequences, namely (a), (b), (c) and (d). You are required to rearrange the jumbled parts of the sentences and mark your response accordingly. 

P. good quality elementary education conforming 

Q. as a fundamental right of all children 

R. the Right to Education Act (RTE) mandates 

S. to the standards and norms specified in the Act
questions.solution:
1. Identify the main clause (subject + verb):

    R: “the Right to Education Act (RTE) mandates”

  2. Find what is being mandated (object):

    P: “good quality elementary education conforming”

  3. Specify how it must conform:

    S: “to the standards and norms specified in the Act”

  4. State it as a right of children:

    Q: “as a fundamental right of all children”

Rearranged sequence: R → P → S → Q

Answer: Option 2 (R P S Q)

Reconstructed sentence:

“The Right to Education Act (RTE) mandates good quality elementary education conforming to the standards and norms specified in the Act as a fundamental right of all children.”

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Directions : Each of the following items in this section consists of a sentence, the parts of which have been jumbled. These parts have been labelled P,  Q, R and S. Given below each sentence have four sequences, namely (a), (b), (c) and (d). You are required to rearrange the jumbled parts of the sentences and mark your response accordingly. 

P. the conflicts and disputes in their mind 

Q. music not only relieves the burden 

R. on children and the young 

S. but also alleviates
questions.solution:
Let's break down the jumbled parts and arrange them logically to form a meaningful sentence.

  1. Identify the subject: The sentence is about "music". Part Q starts with "music not only relieves the burden". This seems like a good beginning.

    • So, we start with Q.

  2. Connect "burden": The phrase "the burden" in Q needs to be specified. Part R says "on children and the young", which tells us whose burden it is.

    • So, Q is followed by R. (music not only relieves the burden on children and the young…)

  3. Identify the "not only… but also" structure: Part Q starts with "not only". We need the "but also" part to complete this parallel structure. Part S provides this: "but also alleviates".

    • So, QR is followed by S. (music not only relieves the burden on children and the young but also alleviates…)

  4. Complete the thought: What does music alleviate? Part P says "the conflicts and disputes in their mind". This logically completes the sentence.

    • So, QRS is followed by P.

Combining these, the correct sequence is QRSP.

Let's read the complete sentence to confirm:

"Music not only relieves the burden on children and the young but also alleviates the conflicts and disputes in their mind."

This sentence is grammatically correct and makes perfect sense.

Comparing this with the given options:

  • Option 1: Q R S P

Therefore, Option 1 is the correct arrangement.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Directions : Each of the following items in this section consists of a sentence, the parts of which have been jumbled. These parts have been labelled P,  Q, R and S. Given below each sentence have four sequences, namely (a), (b), (c) and (d). You are required to rearrange the jumbled parts of the sentences and mark your response accordingly. 

P. but it is virtually covered by a dome-shaped 

Q. Antarctica is a continent 

R. nearly twice as large as Australia 

S. ice sheet nearly 2 miles thick
questions.solution:
1. Identify the opening part:

    – Q (“Antarctica is a continent”) gives a complete main clause and can start the sentence.

  2. Look for additional information about that clause:

    – R (“nearly twice as large as Australia”) describes the size of the continent, so it follows Q.

  3. Spot the contrast connector:

    – P begins with “but,” indicating a contrast, so it comes next.

  4. Complete the object of P:

    – P ends with “dome-shaped,” which needs the noun from S (“ice sheet nearly 2 miles thick”).

Rearranged sequence: Q → R → P → S

Final sentence:

“Antarctica is a continent nearly twice as large as Australia, but it is virtually covered by a dome-shaped ice sheet nearly 2 miles thick.”

Answer: Option 1 (Q R P S)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Directions : Each of the following items in this section consists of a sentence, the parts of which have been jumbled. These parts have been labelled P,  Q, R and S. Given below each sentence have four sequences, namely (a), (b), (c) and (d). You are required to rearrange the jumbled parts of the sentences and mark your response accordingly. 

P. the global economic weight from the advanced economies 

Q. that there was a major shift in 

R. and Central Bank Governors, in recognition of the fact 

S. the G20 was formed in 1999, as a forum of Finance Ministers
questions.solution:
1. Identify the introductory clause:

    S: “the G20 was formed in 1999, as a forum of Finance Ministers”

  2. Note the continuation of membership:

    R: “and Central Bank Governors, in recognition of the fact”

  3. Introduce the reason clause:

    Q: “that there was a major shift in”

  4. Specify what shifted:

    P: “the global economic weight from the advanced economies”

Reconstructed sentence:

“The G20 was formed in 1999, as a forum of Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors, in recognition of the fact that there was a major shift in the global economic weight from the advanced economies.”

Correct option: 4 (S R Q P)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
questions.question:
Directions : Each of the following items in this section consists of a sentence, the parts of which have been jumbled. These parts have been labelled P,  Q, R and S. Given below each sentence have four sequences, namely (a), (b), (c) and (d). You are required to rearrange the jumbled parts of the sentences and mark your response accordingly. 

P. aquaculture nation in the world and 

Q. India’s products are exported to many nations  

R. fish producing and the second largest 

S. India is the third largest
questions.solution:
1. Identify the opening part.

    – S begins with the subject and verb: “India is the third largest …”

    – So S likely comes first.

  2. See what follows “third largest”.

    – R continues: “fish producing and the second largest …”

    – Now we have:

    “India is the third largest fish producing and the second largest …”

  3. Complete the comparison.

    – P adds: “aquaculture nation in the world and …”

    – Together:

    “India is the third largest fish producing and the second largest aquaculture nation in the world and …”

  4. Finish the sentence.

    – Q gives the concluding fact: “India’s products are exported to many nations.”

    – Full sentence:

    “India is the third largest fish producing and the second largest aquaculture nation in the world and India’s products are exported to many nations.”

  5. Sequence of parts: S → R → P → Q, which is Option 1.

Answer: Option 1 (S R P Q)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.solution:
To rearrange the jumbled parts of the sentence into a coherent sequence, let's first examine the given parts:

  • P: include basic freedoms
  • Q: the fundamental rights are
  • R: guaranteed to the individual
  • S: basic rights and

We need to identify a logical order that constructs a meaningful sentence. Let's analyze each part:

1. Starting the Sentence: The part that seems to provide a suitable start is "the fundamental rights are" (Q) since it introduces the subject and sets the stage for a statement about fundamental rights.

2. Follow-Up After Q: After Q, the next logical part would be "basic rights and" (S) as it continues the flow, specifying what fundamental rights are.

3. Next in Sequence: Following S, "include basic freedoms" (P) fits well, as it describes what these basic rights entail.

4. Concluding Part: Finally, "guaranteed to the individual" (R) aptly completes the sentence, emphasizing to whom these rights are guaranteed.

Combining these parts, the correct sequence is:

QSPR: "The fundamental rights are basic rights and include basic freedoms guaranteed to the individual."

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: QSPR

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
$\frac{\text { for many millennia }}{\mathrm{P}} \frac{\text { been in the making }}{\mathrm{Q}} \frac{\text { of a historical process that has }}{\mathrm{R}}$

$\frac{\text{the Indian nation is the product}}{\mathrm{S}}$
questions.solution:
To solve this jumbled sentence, we need to logically order the segments P, Q, R, and S so that they form a coherent and meaningful statement. Let's analyze each part:

S: "the Indian nation is the product"

R: "of a historical process that has"

Q: "been in the making"

P: "for many millennia"

Now, let's determine how these segments can be ordered to form a complete and sensible sentence:

S starts with "the Indian nation is the product," which seems like a good beginning of the sentence.

R follows logically with "of a historical process that has," which continues the thought of S.

Q should come next with "been in the making," since it logically follows "that has."

P concludes with "for many millennia," providing temporal context to the making of the historical process.

Therefore, the correct order to rearrange the sentence is SRQP.

Thus, the correct sequence is:

SRQP

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
$\frac{\text { an epoch that was imbued }}{\mathrm{P}} \frac{\text { for its people the start of }}{\mathrm{Q}} \frac{\text { with a new vision }}{\mathrm{R}}$

$\frac{\text { India's Independence represented }}{\mathrm{S}}$
questions.solution:
To rearrange the jumbled parts of the sentence into a logical and coherent sequence, we need to understand the context and structure of the sentence fragments provided. Let's take a closer look at each part:

  • P: an epoch that was imbued
  • Q: for its people the start of
  • R: with a new vision
  • S: India's Independence represented

We need to construct a sentence that makes grammatical and contextual sense. Let's analyze the fragments:

The fragment S ("India's Independence represented") serves as a good starting point because it introduces the main subject. After introducing the subject, the description of what India's Independence represented would logically follow.

The fragment P ("an epoch that was imbued") seems to be describing what India's Independence represented. The phrase "an epoch" suggests a significant period, and "that was imbued" implies it was filled with certain characteristics.

The fragment R ("with a new vision") completes the thought started in P, indicating what the epoch was imbued with.

Finally, the fragment Q ("for its people the start of") wraps up the sentence by explaining the implication for the people.

Therefore, the correct sequence should be S, P, R, Q:

Combining them, we get: "India's Independence represented an epoch that was imbued with a new vision for its people the start of."

The rearrangement of the sentence is:

S P R Q

Hence, the correct answer is:

Option (C): SPQR

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.solution:
To rearrange the jumbled parts of the sentence (P, Q, R, S) into a coherent sequence, we should examine how each segment logically fits into the structure of a standard sentence.

Given the segments:

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{\text { science and mathematics exhibition }}{\mathrm{P}} \\\\ & \frac{\text { for a discussion on the forthcoming }}{\mathrm{Q}} \\\\ & \frac{\text { to be present before the Principal }}{\mathrm{R}} \\\\ & \frac{\text { all students have been instructed }}{\mathrm{S}} \end{aligned} $$

We need to identify how these parts combine logically.

Let's break it down:

S: "all students have been instructed" - This seems like a good opening statement as it is clear and complete.

R: "to be present before the Principal" - This could logically follow the instruction.

Q: "for a discussion on the forthcoming" - This specifies the purpose of the presence before the Principal.

P: "science and mathematics exhibition" - This completes the purpose by stating what the discussion is about.

Therefore, the logical sequence should be:

SRQP

So, the correct answer is:

Option C

questions.title: NDA General Ability 21 April 2024
questions.question:
$\frac{\text { two bowlers and two batsmen respectively }}{\text { P }} \frac{\text { to the new team }}{\text { Q }} \frac{\text { with the selection of another player }}{\mathrm{R}}$

$\frac{\text { the team now has }}{\mathrm{S}}$
questions.solution:
Arrange the parts so that the sentence becomes meaningful and grammatically correct.

Start with R: “with the selection of another player” — this sets the context.

Next Q: “to the new team” — this completes the idea of selection.

Then S: “the team now has” — introduces the result.

Finally P: “two bowlers and two batsmen respectively” — gives the outcome.

So the correct sequence is:

R Q S P

This forms the sentence:

“With the selection of another player to the new team, the team now has two bowlers and two batsmen respectively.”

Correct answer: Option C

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: Volume of $ M / 8 \mathrm{KMnO}_{4} $ solution required to react completely with $ 25.0 \mathrm{~cm}^{3} $ of $ \mathrm{M} / 4 \mathrm{FeSO}_{4} $ in acidic medium is.
questions.solution:
To determine the volume of $ \frac{M}{8} \mathrm{KMnO_4} $ solution needed to completely react with $ 25.0 \, \mathrm{cm}^3 $ of $ \frac{M}{4} \mathrm{FeSO_4} $ in an acidic medium, we start by considering the balanced ionic equation:

$ \mathrm{MnO_4}^- + 5 \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} + 8 \mathrm{H}^+ \rightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+} + 5 \mathrm{Fe}^{3+} + 4 \mathrm{H_2O} $

From this equation, it's evident that 1 mole of $ \mathrm{KMnO_4} $ reacts with 5 moles of $ \mathrm{FeSO_4} $.

For balancing the redox reaction, apply the molarity equation:

$ \frac{M_1 V_1}{n_1}(\mathrm{KMnO_4}) = \frac{M_2 V_2}{n_2}(\mathrm{FeSO_4}) $

Substitute the given values where:

$ M_1 = \frac{M}{8} $ (molarity of $\mathrm{KMnO_4}$)

$ M_2 = \frac{M}{4} $ (molarity of $\mathrm{FeSO_4}$)

$ V_2 = 25 \, \mathrm{cm}^3 $

$ n_1 = 1 $ (moles of $\mathrm{KMnO_4}$)

$ n_2 = 5 $ (moles of $\mathrm{FeSO_4}$)

$ \frac{\frac{1}{8} \times V_1}{1} = \frac{\frac{1}{4} \times 25}{5} $

Solving the equation for $ V_1 $:

$ \frac{1 \times V_1}{8} = \frac{1 \times 25}{4 \times 5} $

$ V_1 = \frac{1 \times 25 \times 8}{4 \times 5} $

$ V_1 = 10 \, \mathrm{cm}^3 \quad \text{or} \quad V_1 = 10.0 \, \mathrm{mL} $

Hence, the volume of $ \frac{M}{8} \mathrm{KMnO_4} $ solution required is 10.0 mL.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question:
What is the stoichiometric coefficient of $ \mathrm{SO}_{2} $ in the following balance reaction?

$ \mathrm{MnO}_{4}^{-}(a q)+\mathrm{SO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}(a q)^{-}+\mathrm{HSO}_{4}^{-}(a q) $

(in acidic solution)
questions.solution:
Oxidation half reaction:

$ \mathrm{SO}_{2}(g)+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(l) \longrightarrow \mathrm{HSO}_{4}^{-}(a q)+3 \mathrm{H}^{+}(a q)+2 e^{-} \quad \ldots \text { (i) } $

Reduction half reaction:

$ \mathrm{MnO}_{4}^{-}(a q)+8 \mathrm{H}^{+}+5 e^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}(a q)+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(l) \quad \ldots \text { (ii) } $

Multiply eq. (i) by 5 and (ii) by 2 and then add

$ \begin{aligned} 2 \mathrm{MnO}_{4}^{-}(a q)+5 \mathrm{SO}_{2}(g) & +2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(l)+\mathrm{H}^{+}(a q) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Mn}^{+}(a q)+5 \mathrm{HSO}_{4}^{-}(a q) \end{aligned} $

Thus, the stoichiometric coefficient of $ \mathrm{SO}_{2} $ in the above balance equation is 5 .

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question: A function $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ defined as $f(x)=x^2-4 x+5$ is
options:
injective but not surjective
surjective but not injective
both injective and surjective
neither injective nor surjective
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} & \text { Given, } f: R \rightarrow R \\ & f(x)=x^2-4 x+5 \end{aligned}$$

One-One (Injective)

$$\begin{array}{l} & f\left(x_1\right) =f\left(x_2\right) \\ \Rightarrow & x_1^2-4 x_1+5 =x_1^2-4 x_2+5 \\ \Rightarrow & x_1^2-x_2^2 =4\left(x_1-x_2\right) \\ \Rightarrow & \left(x_1-x_2\right)\left(x_1+x_2\right) =4\left(x_1-x_2\right) \\ \Rightarrow & x_1+x_2 =4 \\ \Rightarrow & x_1 =4-x_2 \end{array}$$

Thus, $f(x)$ is not an injective mapping. onto surjective

$$\begin{array}{rlr} \text { let } & y =x^2-4 x+5 \\ \Rightarrow & y =(x-2)^2+1 \\ \Rightarrow & y-1 =(x-2)^2 \\ \Rightarrow & x =\sqrt{(y-1)}+2 \end{array}$$

Thus, for any value of $y<1, x \notin \mathrm{R}$. So, we don't have a pre-image for all $y \in \mathrm{R}$ in $x \in \mathrm{R}$.

Thus, $f(x)=x^2-4 x+5$ is not surjective.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Mathematics Delhi Set 1 - 2024
questions.question:
(a) Students of a school are taken to a railway museum to learn about railways heritage and its history.

An exhibit in the museum depicted many rail lines on the track near the railway station. Let $L$ be the set of all rail lines on the railway track and $R$ be the relation on $L$ defined by

$R=\left\{l_1, l_2\right): l_1$ is parallel to $\left.l_2\right\}$

On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

(i) Find whether the relation R is symmetric or not.

(ii) Find whether the relation R is transitive or not.

(iii) If one of the rail lines on the railway track is represented by the equation $y=3 x+2$, then find the set of rail lines in R related to it.

OR

(b) Let $S$ be the relation defined by $S=\left\{\left(l_1, l_2\right): l_1\right.$ is perpendicular to $l_2$ \} check whether the relation $S$ is symmetric and transitive.
questions.solution:
(a) (i) Given Relation, $R=\left\{\left(l_1, l_2\right): d_1\right.$ is parallel to $l_2$

$$\because l_1\left\|l_2 \Rightarrow l_2\right\| l_1$$

Hence, given relation is symmetric.

(ii) Let $l_1 \| l_2$ and $l_2\left\|l_3 \Rightarrow l_1\right\| l_3$

[As parallel lines are parallel to each other]

(iii) Given equation of line : $y=3 x+2$

Comparing with $y=m x+C$

$$m=\text { slope }=3$$

Slope of parallel, lines is same

$\therefore$ Equation of set of rall line is given by

$$y=3 x+\lambda \quad \lambda \in R$$

OR

(b) Given relation, $S=\left\{\left(l_1, l_2\right): l_1\right.$ is perpendicular to $\left.l_2\right\}$

Symmetric $l_1 \perp l_2 \Rightarrow l_2 \perp l_1$

(Two lines are perpendicular to each other)

Hence, S is symmetric.

Transitive:

Let $L_1 \perp L_2$ and $l_2 \perp l_3 \Rightarrow l_1 \| l_3$

Thus, relation $S$ is not transitive.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: The moment of inertia of a cube of mass $ m $ and side $ a $ about one of its edges is equal to
questions.solution:
To calculate the moment of inertia of a cube about one of its edges, we can use the theorem of perpendicular axes. Here's how it is derived:

First, we consider the moment of inertia about the center of the cube. According to the theorem of perpendicular axes, the total moment of inertia $ I $ can be found by adding the contribution from each axis. For a cube, with side length $ a $ and mass $ m $, the moment of inertia about one of its center axes is:

$ I_{\text{center}} = \frac{m a^{2}}{12} $

Since the cube is symmetric, this value is the same for any of the three perpendicular axes intersecting at the center. The moment of inertia through the axis parallel to a face through the center can similarly be calculated.

Now, when calculating the moment of inertia about an edge, we can add the parallel axis contribution:

$ I = I_{\text{center}} + m \left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{2} $

This accounts for the shift in the axis position. With simplification:

$ \begin{aligned} I & = \left[\frac{m a^{2}}{12} + \frac{m a^{2}}{12}\right] + \frac{m a^{2}}{2} \\ & = \frac{2}{12} m a^{2} + \frac{m a^{2}}{2} \\ & = \frac{m a^{2}}{6} + \frac{m a^{2}}{2} \\ & = \frac{2}{3} m a^{2} \end{aligned} $

Thus, the moment of inertia of a cube of mass $ m $ and side $ a $ about one of its edges is $ \frac{2}{3} m a^{2} $.

questions.title: BITSAT 2024
questions.question: A rigid body rotates about a fixed axis with variable angular velocity equal to $ \alpha-\beta t $, at the time $ t $, where $ \alpha, \beta $ are constants. The angle through which it rotates before it stops is
questions.solution:
The angular velocity of the rigid body is given by $ \omega = \alpha - \beta t $, where $ \alpha $ and $ \beta $ are constants. By comparing this with the equation $ \omega = \omega_0 - \alpha t $, we can identify the initial angular velocity $ \omega_0 $ as $ \alpha $ and the angular retardation (or deceleration) as $ \beta $.

To find the angle through which the body rotates before it comes to a stop, we use the kinematic equation for rotational motion:

$ \omega^2 = \omega_0^2 + 2 \beta \theta $

We know that when the body stops, $ \omega = 0 $. Therefore, by substituting into the equation, we get:

$ 0 = \alpha^2 - 2 \beta \theta $

Solving for $ \theta $, the angle rotated, we find:

$ \theta = \frac{\alpha^2}{2 \beta} $

Thus, the angle through which the body rotates before stopping is $ \frac{\alpha^2}{2 \beta} $.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A disc of mass $M$ and radius $R$ is free to rotate about its vertical axis as shown in the figure. A battery operated motor of negligible mass is fixed to this disc at a point on its circumference. Another disc of the same mass $M$ and radius $R / 2$ is fixed to the motor's thin shaft. Initially, both the discs are at rest. The motor is switched on so that the smaller disc rotates at a uniform angular speed $\omega$. If the angular speed at which the large disc rotates is $\omega / n$, then the value of $n$ is ___________.
questions.solution:
On applying conservation of angular momentum about axis of larger disc.

$$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2} \cdot M\left(\frac{R}{2}\right)^2 \cdot \omega-M\left(\omega^{\prime} R\right) \cdot R-\frac{M R^2}{2} \cdot \omega^{\prime}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\omega}{8}=\frac{3 \omega^{\prime}}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow \omega^{\prime}=\frac{\omega}{12} \quad \text { Hence, } \mathrm{n}=12 \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2024 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A thin uniform rod of length $L$ and certain mass is kept on a frictionless horizontal table with a massless string of length $L$ fixed to one end (top view is shown in the figure). The other end of the string is pivoted to a point $\mathrm{O}$. If a horizontal impulse $P$ is imparted to the rod at a distance $x={L \over n}$ from the mid-point of the rod (see figure), then the rod and string revolve together around the point $\mathrm{O}$, with the rod remaining aligned with the string. In such a case, the value of $n$ is ___________.
questions.solution:
Linear impulse $$\int \mathrm{Fdt}=\Delta$$ momentum

$$\begin{aligned} & =\mathrm{m}\left(\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{cm}}-0\right) \\ & \mathrm{P}=\mathrm{m}\left(\omega \mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{cm}}\right) \\ & =\mathrm{m} \omega\left(\mathrm{L}+\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2}\right) \\ & \mathrm{P}=\mathrm{m} \omega\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{~L}}{2}\right) \quad \text{... (i)} \end{aligned}$$

Angular impulse $$\int \tau \mathrm{dt}=\Delta$$ angular momentum

$$\int \mathrm{r} \times \mathrm{Fdt}=\Delta \mathrm{L}$$

$$\mathrm{r} \times \int \mathrm{Fdt}=\mathrm{I}(\omega-0)$$, and I is moment of inertia about axis of rotation.

$$\begin{aligned} \left(\mathrm{L}+\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2}\right. & +\mathrm{x}) \times \mathrm{P}=\left(\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{cm}}+\mathrm{md}^2\right) \omega \\ & =\left(\frac{\mathrm{mL}^2}{12}+\mathrm{m}\left(\mathrm{L}+\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2}\right)^2\right) \omega \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \left(\frac{3 L}{2}+x\right) P & =m L^2\left(\frac{1}{12}+\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^2\right) \omega \\ \left(\frac{3 L}{2}+x\right) P & =m L^2\left(\frac{7}{3}\right) \omega \quad \text{... (ii)} \end{aligned}$$

Divide eq.-(i) & (ii)

$$\begin{aligned} & \left(\frac{3 L}{2}+x\right)=\frac{L\left(\frac{7}{3}\right)}{\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)} \\ & \frac{3 L}{2}+x=L\left(\frac{14}{9}\right) \\ & x=\frac{L}{18} \end{aligned}$$
